project.goal: Convert independent artists visiting the page into early-access signups
project.target_users: Independent artists and home studio producers

frontend.approach: Island architecture with minimal client-side JS

security.headers: CSP, X-Frame-Options, HSTS configured in vercel.json

data.storage: None on-platform; email signups forwarded to ConvertKit API

devops.ci: GitHub Actions running Astro build and Lighthouse CI checks
devops.hosting: Vercel Edge Network with automatic preview deployments

reasoner_reviews.product: The landing page has a clear conversion goal: get independent artists to sign up for early access. The before/after audio widget is the key differentiator and must load fast. Feature highlights and testimonials build trust for an audience skeptical of automated mastering quality.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro is the right choice here — it ships near-zero JS by default and island hydration allows the audio comparison widget to be interactive without blocking the critical rendering path. Static deployment on Vercel Edge eliminates server costs entirely.
reasoner_reviews.security: No backend means the attack surface is minimal. CSP headers must restrict audio widget JavaScript sources. The ConvertKit integration for email capture should use a server-side proxy if the API key needs protecting; otherwise a public form embed is acceptable.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse score of 95+ requires lazy-loading audio assets, optimal image formats (AVIF/WebP), and no render-blocking third-party scripts. ConvertKit embed scripts should be loaded asynchronously.

frontend.app_structure: Astro project with pages/index.astro as single entry point, reusable island components for audio widget and signup form
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single-page layout with Hero, AudioCompare (island), Features, Testimonials, Signup CTA, and Footer sections
frontend.performance_notes: Audio files lazy-loaded via Intersection Observer; AVIF hero images with WebP fallback; Tailwind CSS purged to minimal bundle; preload critical fonts
frontend.frontend_security_notes: Content Security Policy restricts script sources; no inline scripts outside Astro islands; third-party embed (ConvertKit) loaded async

security.auth_design: No authentication required
security.abuse_prevention: Honeypot field on signup form; rate limiting delegated to ConvertKit's API; Vercel DDoS protection at edge
security.privacy_controls: No PII stored on platform; GDPR-compliant ConvertKit embed with explicit consent checkbox

data.entities: signups (managed by ConvertKit), audio_demos (static files in public/), feature_highlights (Astro content collections), testimonials (Astro content collections)
data.storage_design: No database; content managed via Astro content collections in Markdown/YAML files
data.analytics_events: signup_submitted, audio_play_started, audio_play_completed, cta_clicked — all fired via Vercel Analytics track()

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel Edge Network CDN; static build artifact deployed globally on push to main
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: astro build, Lighthouse CI with 95-score gate, deploy preview on PR, production deploy on main merge
devops.observability_stack: Vercel Analytics dashboard for pageviews, conversion events, and device breakdown

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a pure static landing page with no backend requirements. Key decisions: Astro for zero-JS-by-default output, Vercel Edge for global CDN delivery, island architecture to isolate the interactive audio comparison widget. The audio widget is the main conversion driver so it must be lazy-loaded and fast. Email collection is delegated entirely to ConvertKit to avoid any data retention obligations on this platform.
target_output.title: AI Mastering Service Early-Access Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A static Astro landing page deployed on Vercel's Edge Network that converts independent artists into early-access signups through an interactive before/after audio mastering comparison widget, social proof elements, and a ConvertKit-integrated signup form. No server-side runtime or database — all content served from CDN at global edge locations.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-page static site built with Astro and TypeScript, compiled to HTML/CSS with island hydration for the audio comparison widget and signup form. Deployed as a static artifact to Vercel's Edge Network, serving requests from the nearest of Vercel's global edge locations. Audio demo files stored as static assets in the Vercel deployment. Analytics events emitted client-side to Vercel Analytics. Email capture delegated to ConvertKit's hosted form API, keeping no PII on the platform.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4.x with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, custom AudioCompare island component using the HTML5 Web Audio API. Build: Astro static output mode. Hosting: Vercel Edge Network with vercel.json for security headers and redirects. Analytics: Vercel Analytics with custom event tracking. Email: ConvertKit API form integration. CI: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI assertion.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero section with headline copy and primary CTA linking to signup anchor. AudioCompare widget (Astro island) playing before and after mastering samples side-by-side with waveform visualization. Feature highlights section listing mastering capabilities with icons. Testimonials carousel with quotes from beta artists. Early-access signup form with email field, consent checkbox, and ConvertKit submission. Social proof counter showing number of tracks mastered in beta.
target_output.system_components: Astro static site generator compiling Markdown content collections and TypeScript island components. AudioCompare island using HTMLMediaElement API for synchronized before/after playback. Vercel Edge Network CDN serving optimized static assets. GitHub Actions CI pipeline running Astro build and Lighthouse CI score check. ConvertKit API handling email list subscription.
target_output.workflows: Audio Demo Interaction: A visitor scrolls to the AudioCompare widget, which lazy-hydrates on entry via IntersectionObserver. The visitor plays the 'before' mastered sample and toggles to 'after'; an audio_play_started event fires to Vercel Analytics on each play. Early-Access Signup: The visitor clicks the CTA, scrolls to the signup form island, enters email and ticks the consent checkbox, submits to ConvertKit's API endpoint, and sees a success confirmation; a signup_submitted event fires to Vercel Analytics.
target_output.data_model: signups — stored and managed entirely in ConvertKit (email, signup_date, source_tag). audio_demos — static MP3 files in public/audio/ with before/after naming convention (e.g., demo_guitar_before.mp3, demo_guitar_after.mp3). feature_highlights — Astro content collection YAML entries (id, title, description, icon). testimonials — Astro content collection YAML entries (id, artist_name, genre, quote, avatar_image).
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. External integrations: POST to ConvertKit /v3/forms/:form_id/subscribe with {email, fields.consent} — called directly from the signup island via fetch(). Vercel Analytics: window.va('event', {name: 'signup_submitted'}) called on form success. No API routes in the Astro project.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Vercel TLS terminates all HTTPS connections at edge. vercel.json sets Content-Security-Policy restricting script-src to 'self' and ConvertKit CDN; X-Frame-Options DENY; Strict-Transport-Security max-age=31536000. Honeypot hidden field on signup form to catch automated submissions. ConvertKit form uses explicit GDPR consent checkbox. No cookies set by the platform itself; ConvertKit consent state managed by ConvertKit.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: checkout, bun install, bun run build (astro build), Lighthouse CI with assertions (performance >= 95, accessibility >= 95, best-practices >= 90), on PR open deploy to Vercel preview URL, on push to main trigger Vercel production deployment. Vercel project configured with automatic preview deployments per branch. No environment secrets required for production; ConvertKit form ID is a public identifier embedded in source.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics captures pageviews, unique visitors, and referrer sources. Custom events tracked: audio_play_started (with demo_id property), cta_clicked (button location), signup_submitted (success), signup_failed (error). Conversion funnel visible in Vercel Analytics dashboard: visits -> audio_play -> cta_click -> signup. No alerting required for a static site.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero server costs on Vercel Hobby or Pro plan for static deployments. ConvertKit free tier supports up to 1,000 subscribers. Vercel Edge Network handles traffic spikes automatically with no configuration. Only cost if scaling: Vercel Pro at $20/month if team collaboration features needed.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (3 days): Scaffold Astro project with Tailwind, implement Hero, AudioCompare island with two demo tracks, and Signup form wired to ConvertKit. Deploy to Vercel. Phase 2 (2 days): Add Features section, Testimonials carousel with content collection entries, social proof counter. Tune Lighthouse score to 95+. Phase 3 (post-launch): A/B test hero headline variants using Vercel Edge Config feature flags; add localized copy for EU markets.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode throughout. Astro content collections enforce schema via Zod. Lighthouse CI gate in GitHub Actions blocks merges below 95 performance score. Audio files must be MP3 128kbps max to stay under 500KB per demo. No third-party analytics scripts besides Vercel Analytics to protect Lighthouse score.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ConvertKit API downtime breaks signup submission. Mitigation: display friendly error state and fallback to mailto link. Risk: audio widget autoplay blocked by browsers on mobile. Mitigation: require explicit play tap, never autoplay. Tradeoff: static Astro over Next.js static export gives smaller JS bundle but no ISR — acceptable since content changes infrequently.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose ConvertKit over Mailchimp for simpler GDPR consent handling and API. Confirmed no server-side form proxy needed since ConvertKit form ID is public. Selected Astro over plain HTML for content collections maintainability. Decided against self-hosting audio files on S3 in favor of Vercel static assets to avoid cross-origin complexity.

metadata.notes: Static landing page for AI mastering SaaS; Astro on Vercel, no backend.

project_goal.value: CLI automation tool that decomposes a monolithic audio-processing pipeline into discrete microservices handling stem separation, loudness normalization, and metadata tagging as independent Celery workers
project_goal.rationale: Eliminates single-point bottleneck in legacy audio batch pipeline and enables per-stage scaling

target_users.value: Internal audio operations engineers and pipeline operators
target_users.rationale: Tool is strictly internal; no public user access required

project_class.rationale: Operator-invoked CLI that orchestrates distributed batch workers without a browser UI

capabilities.rationale: No frontend needed; all value is in pipeline orchestration and worker integration

complexity_level.rationale: Parallel worker fan-out, stage-level retry logic, and distributed state tracking add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Internal tool processing proprietary audio assets; data loss risk is primary concern

data_sensitivity.rationale: Audio assets are proprietary but do not constitute personal or financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline triggered exclusively from internal operator workstations; never exposed to internet

access_model.value: Custom JWT for API layer; operator identity passed via CLI token stored in ~/.audioctl/credentials
access_model.rationale: Lightweight auth sufficient for internal tool; avoids OAuth overhead

feature_scope.value: Pipeline run initiation, stage fan-out (stem separation/loudness/tagging), per-stage retry, DLQ routing, structured log emission, run status CLI query
feature_scope.rationale: Full orchestration feature set for production use

mvp_scope.value: Sequential stage execution with basic retry for stem separation and loudness workers; CLI status query
mvp_scope.rationale: Parallel fan-out deferred to Wave 2 after worker stability confirmed

security_baseline.value: JWT auth for orchestrator API, TLS 1.3 for worker-to-orchestrator comms, secrets in AWS Secrets Manager
security_baseline.rationale: Internal tool minimum controls; no PHI or payment data

frontend_stack.rationale: CLI tool; no browser UI

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Celery 5 workers; FastAPI orchestrator service
backend_stack.rationale: Celery provides mature task queue primitives; FastAPI for orchestrator REST interface

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for pipeline run state; Redis 7 as Celery broker and result backend
data_platform.rationale: Postgres for durable state; Redis for low-latency queue and ephemeral task results

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate for orchestrator and workers; ECR for images
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless containers eliminate host management; per-worker task definitions enable independent scaling

privacy_retention_policy.value: Pipeline run logs retained 90 days; audio asset paths stored but raw audio never persisted by orchestrator
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Internal operational data retention policy

future_scope.value: Real-time progress streaming via WebSocket, ML-based quality scoring worker, cost-per-run attribution dashboard
future_scope.rationale: Phase 3 roadmap items pending pipeline stability

constraints.value: Workers must complete stem separation within 4 minutes per track; total pipeline budget under $800/month at 10K daily tracks
constraints.rationale: SLA and cost constraints from audio ops team

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards with Prometheus metrics; structured JSON logs to CloudWatch; PagerDuty alerts on stage failure rate
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing ops monitoring stack

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints with worker-by-worker rollout to production
execution_preference.rationale: Incremental rollout reduces blast radius during decomposition

compliance_context.value: Internal use only; proprietary audio assets must not be written to unencrypted storage or transmitted outside VPC
compliance_context.rationale: Studio IP protection policy

project.goal: Decompose monolithic audio batch pipeline into independent Celery workers for stem separation, loudness normalization, and metadata tagging
project.target_users: Internal audio operations engineers
project.timeline: Q2 delivery with incremental worker rollout

backend.api: Internal REST for orchestrator; Celery task signatures for worker invocation

security.auth: Custom JWT via CLI credentials file
security.transport: TLS 1.3 between orchestrator and ECS tasks

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 for pipeline_runs and processing_stages state
data.cache: Redis 7 as Celery broker and task result backend

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: lint, pytest, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy
devops.hosting: ECS Fargate with per-worker task definitions

open_questions.fanout_concurrency: Max parallel worker tasks per pipeline run to be confirmed with ops

reasoner_reviews.product: The microservices decomposition of the audio pipeline is well-scoped. Stem separation, loudness normalization, and metadata tagging map cleanly to independent workers with distinct resource profiles. CLI-first approach suits the internal operator audience.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Celery with Redis broker is appropriate for this workload. Worker isolation via separate ECS task definitions allows CPU-optimized instances for stem separation (compute-heavy) and lighter tasks for tagging. FastAPI orchestrator centralizes state management in Postgres rather than distributing it across workers.
reasoner_reviews.security: Internal tool with JWT auth is adequate given VPC isolation. Secrets Manager rotation for worker credentials should be confirmed. Audio asset paths in Postgres must not include presigned URLs that could leak if logs are exported.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 4-minute SLA for stem separation is achievable with 4 vCPU Fargate allocation if Demucs model is pre-loaded at container startup. $800/month budget is tight at 10K tracks/day; Fargate Savings Plans should be evaluated.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan left stage idempotency underspecified. Re-executing a loudness stage on an already-normalized file causes double-normalization. This round must add explicit idempotency checks in all worker pre-execution hooks.

backend.service_design: FastAPI orchestrator exposes POST /pipeline-runs (initiate), GET /pipeline-runs/{id} (status), GET /pipeline-runs/{id}/stages (stage detail). Celery workers: StemSeparationWorker (Demucs), LoudnessWorker (ffmpeg loudnorm), TaggingWorker (mutagen). Each worker is a separate Celery app consuming its own named queue.
backend.api_patterns: Internal REST with JWT auth header. Orchestrator fans out via Celery signatures, collecting results via AsyncResult polling with 30s timeout loops. CLI wraps orchestrator REST with pretty-printed progress output.
backend.failure_handling: Exponential backoff: base 10s, max 5 retries, max backoff 5 minutes. Dead-letter queue: Redis list 'dlq:{stage}' for tasks exceeding max retries. Orchestrator marks stage status 'dlq' and emits structured log event. Idempotency hook: each worker calls check_stage_idempotency(stage_id) querying processing_stages.status before executing; skips if status='completed'.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly Celery beat job purges pipeline_runs older than 90 days and prunes Redis result keys. Stage-config sync job pulls latest worker image tags from ECR and updates stage_configs table.

security.auth_design: Orchestrator validates JWT on every request. CLI reads token from ~/.audioctl/credentials, refreshed via 'audioctl auth login'. Worker containers receive scoped IAM task roles for S3 read and Secrets Manager access.
security.secrets_management: DB credentials and Redis auth token stored in Secrets Manager, rotated every 30 days. Worker task definitions reference secrets as environment variable injections via ECS secrets integration.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Orchestrator logs every pipeline run initiation, stage dispatch, retry event, and completion to CloudWatch Logs with correlation run_id field. No audio content in logs; only file paths and metadata.

data.entities: pipeline_runs (id UUID PK, operator_id, status ENUM queued/running/completed/failed, audio_asset_s3_key, initiated_at, completed_at). processing_stages (id UUID PK, run_id FK, stage_name ENUM stem_separation/loudness/tagging, status ENUM pending/running/completed/failed/dlq, celery_task_id, attempts INT, started_at, finished_at). stage_configs (id UUID PK, stage_name, worker_image_tag, timeout_seconds, max_retries, updated_at). worker_tasks (id UUID PK, stage_id FK, celery_task_id, result_payload_json JSONB, error_detail, created_at). audio_assets (id UUID PK, s3_key, duration_seconds, format, size_bytes, ingested_at).
data.storage_design: Postgres 16 on RDS Multi-AZ for pipeline state. Redis 7 ElastiCache for Celery broker and result backend with 24h TTL on results. S3 for audio asset storage; workers read via presigned URLs with 10-minute expiry.
data.migration_notes: Alembic for schema migrations, run as ECS one-off task in CI before rolling deploy.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS cluster with 4 task definitions: orchestrator-api (1 vCPU/2GB), stem-separation-worker (4 vCPU/8GB), loudness-worker (2 vCPU/4GB), tagging-worker (1 vCPU/2GB). Auto-scaling on Celery queue depth metric exported to CloudWatch via Prometheus remote write.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, pytest (85% coverage gate), Docker build per service, ECR push with SHA tag, ECS rolling update. Integration tests spin up localstack for S3 mocks and a real Redis container.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scrapes Celery worker /metrics endpoints: tasks_received_total, tasks_succeeded_total, tasks_failed_total, task_runtime_seconds histogram. Grafana dashboards per stage. PagerDuty alert if DLQ depth >10 or stage failure rate >5% over 5 minutes.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS service rollback to previous task definition revision on failed health check. Alembic downgrade script tagged per release for DB rollback.

ISS-001.title: Worker retry strategy underspecified
ISS-001.detail: Rounds 1 and 2 lacked explicit exponential backoff parameters, DLQ routing, and idempotency guarantees for re-executed stages

ISS-002.title: Celery queue isolation not defined
ISS-002.detail: All workers sharing single default queue prevented per-stage scaling and caused priority inversion between lightweight tagging and heavy stem separation tasks

ISS-003.title: Stage idempotency on retry not addressed
ISS-003.detail: Re-executing loudness stage on an already-normalized file causes double-normalization; plan must specify idempotency check before stage execution

focus_issues.title: Stage idempotency on retry not addressed
focus_issues.detail: Re-executing loudness stage on already-normalized file causes double-normalization; must add idempotency check before stage execution

latest_recommendations: Add explicit idempotency check in worker pre-execution hook querying processing_stages.status='completed' before re-running normalization or tagging logic
Set presigned URL expiry to at least 10 minutes to cover stem separation SLA window

The core architecture is sound after round 2 resolved retry strategy and queue isolation. Key addition: each worker queries processing_stages.status before executing; if 'completed', skips and returns cached result. fix_report.action_taken: Added check_stage_idempotency(stage_id) pre-execution hook to StemSeparationWorker, LoudnessWorker, and TaggingWorker. Hook queries processing_stages.status; if 'completed', worker returns cached result_payload without re-executing. Extended S3 presigned URL expiry from 5 to 10 minutes to cover worst-case stem separation duration.

target_output.title: Audio Pipeline Microservices Decomposition Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 Celery-based CLI automation tool decomposing a monolithic audio batch pipeline into three independent ECS Fargate workers — StemSeparationWorker (Demucs), LoudnessWorker (ffmpeg loudnorm), and TaggingWorker (mutagen) — orchestrated by a FastAPI service with PostgreSQL 16 state tracking, Redis 7 broker, per-stage retry with exponential backoff, DLQ routing, and idempotent stage execution preventing double-processing on retries.
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI orchestrator service on ECS Fargate receives CLI-triggered pipeline run requests and fans out to three dedicated Celery queues: 'stem-separation', 'loudness', and 'tagging'. Each queue is consumed by its own ECS task definition with CPU tuned to workload profile (4 vCPU for Demucs, 2 vCPU for loudnorm, 1 vCPU for tagging). Workers read audio assets from S3 via 10-minute presigned URLs and write processed output back to S3 output prefix. PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ persists pipeline_runs and processing_stages state. All stage executions are idempotent via pre-execution processing_stages.status checks. Prometheus and Grafana monitor per-stage throughput, retry rates, and DLQ depth. All components run inside a private VPC subnet with no public IP assignment.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestrator: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0, Alembic, Pydantic v2. Workers: Python 3.12, Celery 5, Demucs 4 (stem separation), ffmpeg with loudnorm filter (loudness), mutagen 1.47 (metadata tagging). CLI: Python 3.12 with Click 8, wrapping orchestrator REST. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis 7, S3, ECR, AWS Secrets Manager. Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, CloudWatch Logs structured JSON, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Pipeline Run Initiation: CLI 'audioctl run submit --asset s3://audio-bucket/track.wav' POSTs to /pipeline-runs, creating run record and fanning out three Celery tasks to dedicated queues. Idempotent Stage Execution: each worker calls check_stage_idempotency(stage_id) before executing; returns cached result if stage already completed. Stage Fan-Out: StemSeparationTask, LoudnessTask, TaggingTask execute in parallel on independent queues with per-stage concurrency limits. Status Query: 'audioctl run status {run_id}' polls GET /pipeline-runs/{id}/stages showing per-stage status, attempt count, and duration. Retry and DLQ: stages retry up to 5 times with exponential backoff; permanent failures route to dlq:{stage} Redis list with PagerDuty alert. Nightly Cleanup: Celery beat purges runs older than 90 days.
target_output.system_components: FastAPI Orchestrator (ECS Fargate 1 vCPU/2GB): REST API with JWT auth, run initiation, Celery task dispatch, stage status aggregation. StemSeparationWorker (ECS Fargate 4 vCPU/8GB): Demucs model pre-loaded at startup, check_stage_idempotency hook, writes stems to S3 output prefix. LoudnessWorker (ECS Fargate 2 vCPU/4GB): ffmpeg loudnorm pass, idempotency hook skips already-normalized files. TaggingWorker (ECS Fargate 1 vCPU/2GB): mutagen writes BPM/key/artist tags, idempotency hook prevents duplicate tag writes. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ: pipeline_runs, processing_stages, stage_configs, worker_tasks, audio_assets. Redis 7 ElastiCache: Celery broker queues (stem-separation, loudness, tagging, dlq:*), result backend with 24h TTL. S3: audio asset storage; presigned URL read access for workers (10-min expiry).
target_output.workflows: Pipeline Run Initiation: Operator runs 'audioctl run submit --asset s3://audio-bucket/track.wav'. CLI reads JWT from ~/.audioctl/credentials and POSTs to orchestrator /pipeline-runs. Orchestrator creates pipeline_runs row (status=queued), inserts three processing_stages rows (status=pending), dispatches StemSeparationTask, LoudnessTask, TaggingTask to respective Celery queues. Worker Pre-Execution Idempotency: on task pickup, each worker calls check_stage_idempotency(stage_id) which queries SELECT status FROM processing_stages WHERE id=stage_id. If status='completed', returns cached result_payload from worker_tasks without re-executing. Otherwise proceeds to execution, writes output to S3, updates processing_stages.status='completed'. Stage Failure and Retry: worker raises exception, Celery retries with backoff schedule 10s/20s/40s/80s/160s. After 5 failures, task appended to Redis dlq:{stage} list, processing_stages.status set to 'dlq'. PagerDuty alert fires if DLQ depth >10 or stage failure rate >5% over 5-minute Prometheus window.
target_output.data_model: pipeline_runs (id UUID PK, operator_id VARCHAR(64), status ENUM queued/running/completed/failed, audio_asset_s3_key TEXT NOT NULL, initiated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). processing_stages (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK REFERENCES pipeline_runs, stage_name ENUM stem_separation/loudness/tagging, status ENUM pending/running/completed/failed/dlq, celery_task_id VARCHAR(64), attempts SMALLINT DEFAULT 0, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, finished_at TIMESTAMPTZ). stage_configs (id UUID PK, stage_name ENUM UNIQUE, worker_image_tag VARCHAR(128), timeout_seconds INT, max_retries SMALLINT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). worker_tasks (id UUID PK, stage_id UUID FK REFERENCES processing_stages, celery_task_id VARCHAR(64), result_payload_json JSONB, error_detail TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audio_assets (id UUID PK, s3_key TEXT UNIQUE, duration_seconds FLOAT, format VARCHAR(16), size_bytes BIGINT, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ). No raw audio content stored in DB; idempotency enforced via processing_stages.status check.
target_output.api_design: Internal FastAPI REST at /api/v1. POST /pipeline-runs body {asset_s3_key: str, priority: int=0} returns {run_id, status, stages: []}. GET /pipeline-runs/{run_id} returns {run_id, status, initiated_at, completed_at}. GET /pipeline-runs/{run_id}/stages returns [{stage_name, status, attempts, started_at, finished_at, celery_task_id}]. POST /pipeline-runs/{run_id}/cancel revokes pending Celery tasks and marks stages as failed. GET /stage-configs returns [{stage_name, worker_image_tag, timeout_seconds, max_retries}]. All endpoints require Authorization: Bearer {jwt}. Rate limit: 500 req/min per operator token.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT validation middleware on every orchestrator endpoint; tokens issued via 'audioctl auth login' CLI command with 8-hour expiry. ECS worker task IAM roles scoped to S3 GetObject on audio bucket prefix and Secrets Manager GetSecretValue for DB and Redis credentials only. TLS 1.3 enforced between orchestrator, workers, RDS, and ElastiCache. Audio asset S3 presigned URLs expire after 10 minutes; never logged or stored in Postgres. Secrets Manager rotation every 30 days for DB password and Redis auth token. All ECS tasks run in private VPC subnets with no public IP; only orchestrator ALB is VPC-internal. Proprietary audio assets never written to unencrypted storage per studio IP protection policy.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS cluster with 4 service-backed task definitions deployed via GitHub Actions. CI pipeline: ruff lint, black format check, pytest (85% coverage gate), Docker build per service with SHA tag, ECR push, ECS rolling update with circuit-breaker (2 failed health checks triggers rollback). Alembic migration runs as ECS one-off task before worker deploy. Environments: dev (single-AZ, minimal Fargate resources for cost), staging (multi-AZ, mirrors production task definitions for integration testing), production (multi-AZ us-east-1a/1b, auto-scaling on Celery queue depth metric exported to CloudWatch via Prometheus remote write adapter). Minimum 1 warm stem-separation-worker task to prevent Demucs cold-start latency on first job. Fargate Savings Plan covers baseline stem-separation capacity.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes Celery worker /metrics endpoint (exposed via prometheus-celery-exporter sidecar on each task): tasks_received_total, tasks_succeeded_total, tasks_failed_total, task_runtime_seconds histogram by stage and queue. Grafana dashboards: per-stage throughput (tasks/min), retry rate (%), DLQ depth by stage, p95 task_runtime_seconds per stage vs SLA threshold. CloudWatch Logs: structured JSON from orchestrator and workers with fields run_id, stage_name, celery_task_id, status, duration_ms, operator_id. PagerDuty alerts: stage failure rate >5% over 5 minutes, DLQ depth >10 for any stage, orchestrator p99 latency >500ms, RDS connection pool >80%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $640/month at 10K daily tracks: ECS Fargate stem-separation-worker dominates (~$400/month for 4 vCPU burst); loudness-worker ~$80/month; tagging-worker ~$40/month; RDS Multi-AZ PostgreSQL ~$80/month (db.t3.medium); ElastiCache Redis ~$40/month. Auto-scaling: stem-separation-worker scales 1-8 tasks on queue depth >5; loudness and tagging workers scale 1-4 tasks. Fargate Compute Savings Plan covers 1 baseline stem-separation task for 30% cost reduction. S3 Intelligent-Tiering for processed output files older than 30 days.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Sequential stage execution (no parallel fan-out), StemSeparationWorker and LoudnessWorker only, basic 'audioctl run submit' and 'audioctl run status' CLI commands, Postgres state tracking with Alembic migrations, GitHub Actions CI with pytest coverage gate. Phase 2 (3 weeks): Parallel fan-out via dedicated Celery queues, TaggingWorker, exponential backoff retry with DLQ routing, idempotency hooks on all three workers, stage_configs management. Phase 3 (3 weeks): Auto-scaling on queue depth metric, Grafana per-stage dashboards, Celery beat cleanup jobs, audio_assets table with ingestion tracking, staging environment parity.
target_output.development_guidelines: ruff + black enforced in CI with zero-tolerance on lint failures. pytest at 85% coverage gate; integration tests use localstack for S3 and a real Redis 7 container. Each worker implements WorkerBase abstract class with execute() and check_stage_idempotency() interface methods. Audio asset S3 keys are the only data reference passed between services; no binary audio content in Celery task payloads or Postgres. ADR required for any change to Celery queue topology, retry parameters, or worker resource allocation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Demucs model load at container cold start takes ~45s causing first-task latency spike. Mitigation: enforce ECS service minimum count of 1 for stem-separation-worker to keep a warm task. Risk: Redis ElastiCache as sole Celery broker is a single point of failure for queue durability. Mitigation: enable Redis AOF persistence and Multi-AZ replication; evaluate Celery SQS transport for production-critical workloads. Tradeoff: idempotency check adds one Postgres SELECT per worker pre-execution; negligible at 10K tracks/day. Tradeoff: per-stage ECS task definitions increase deployment surface but enable independent scaling and image versioning per worker.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Celery over AWS SQS native for broker to retain AsyncResult introspection for CLI status polling. Presigned URL expiry set to 10 minutes (up from 5) to cover worst-case stem separation duration. Idempotency check uses processing_stages.status query rather than S3 object existence check to avoid race conditions between concurrent retries. DLQ implemented as Redis list 'dlq:{stage}' rather than SQS DLQ to keep the stack homogeneous and avoid cross-service dependencies.

metadata.notes: Round 3 of audio pipeline decomposition; closes ISS-003 idempotency gap with worker pre-execution status check.

project_goal.value: Desktop DAW plugin manager that tracks license compliance for installed VST/AU plugins across studio workstations, flags expired or unlicensed instruments, and generates PDF audit reports for studio administrators
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual spreadsheet-based plugin license tracking that fails to catch violations before vendor audits

target_users.value: Studio administrators and audio engineers at professional music production facilities
target_users.rationale: Primary users are admin staff auditing compliance and engineers checking their own workstation status

project_class.rationale: On-premise Electron application installed on studio admin machines with local SQLite storage

capabilities.rationale: Electron frontend, Node.js backend process, SQLite data layer, JWT-based admin auth

complexity_level.rationale: Network plugin scanning, license cross-referencing, and PDF report generation are moderately complex but well-bounded

risk_level.rationale: Handles confidential vendor license agreements and proprietary studio IP inventory; breach would expose contractual obligations

data_sensitivity.rationale: Plugin license keys, vendor contracts, and studio asset inventory are confidential per studio contracts

external_exposure.rationale: Application runs on studio LAN only; no internet-facing endpoints

access_model.value: Custom JWT with admin and engineer roles; admin can view all workstations, engineer sees own machine only
access_model.rationale: Role separation prevents engineers from viewing other workstations' license status

feature_scope.value: Workstation network scan for VST/AU plugins, license registry management, compliance flag generation, audit report PDF export, license expiry alerts, vendor contact directory
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for production license compliance management

mvp_scope.value: Single-workstation plugin scan, manual license entry, basic compliance flag view, PDF export for one workstation
mvp_scope.rationale: Network-wide scan deferred to Phase 2 after single-machine workflow validated

security_baseline.value: SQLite database encrypted with SQLCipher, JWT auth for admin access, license key fields encrypted at rest, no network egress outside studio LAN
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential data requires encryption at rest; LAN isolation prevents external data exposure

frontend_stack.rationale: Electron provides cross-platform desktop with web tech familiarity; React for component reuse

backend_stack.value: Node.js 22 with Express for IPC bridge; Electron main process for OS-level operations
backend_stack.rationale: Node.js 22 in Electron main process handles filesystem scan, SQLite access, and PDF generation

data_platform.value: SQLite with SQLCipher encryption for local data store
data_platform.rationale: Embedded database appropriate for desktop app; SQLCipher adds AES-256 encryption for confidential license data

hosting_target.value: On-premise desktop install via signed installer (macOS DMG, Windows MSI)
hosting_target.rationale: Studio environment has no cloud budget for this tool; fully local deployment required

privacy_retention_policy.value: Audit report history retained indefinitely per studio contract obligations; license data retained until plugin decommissioned plus 3 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Studio contracts require audit trail retention for vendor dispute resolution

future_scope.value: Network-wide multi-workstation scan, vendor license API integrations (iLok, Pace, Waves Central), automatic renewal reminders via email
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 and 3 roadmap items expanding coverage beyond manual entry

constraints.value: Must run offline (no internet dependency), support macOS 13+ and Windows 11, install size under 200MB
constraints.rationale: Studio network has strict egress filtering; offline operation is a hard requirement

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for crash reporting (opt-in, no PII transmitted), local log file in ~/Library/Logs/PluginManager/ (macOS) or %APPDATA%/PluginManager/ (Windows)
observability_baseline.rationale: Desktop app; no server-side monitoring infrastructure

execution_preference.value: Milestone-based delivery with installer release per milestone
execution_preference.rationale: Studio team prefers installers over rolling releases for stability

compliance_context.value: Studio vendor license agreements require confidential handling; audit trails must be retained per contract obligations; no license data may leave studio LAN
compliance_context.rationale: VST/AU plugin vendors (iLok, Waves, Native Instruments) enforce license audit rights in studio contracts

project.goal: Track VST/AU plugin license compliance across studio workstations; flag violations; generate PDF audit reports
project.target_users: Studio administrators and audio engineers

frontend.state: Zustand for UI state, React Query for IPC data fetching

security.auth: Custom JWT with admin/engineer roles
security.encryption: SQLCipher AES-256 for SQLite, license key fields encrypted

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: lint, test, Electron Builder (macOS DMG, Windows MSI)

open_questions.scan_method: Whether to use Node.js filesystem scan or DAW-specific plugin registry APIs for plugin discovery

reasoner_reviews.product: Plugin license compliance is a genuine pain point for professional studios facing vendor audit rights. The scope is well-bounded: scan installed plugins, cross-reference a license registry, flag violations, export audit PDFs. MVP limiting to single-workstation scan is sensible.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Electron with Node.js 22 is the right choice for a desktop tool requiring filesystem access and PDF generation. SQLite with SQLCipher handles the local storage and encryption requirement without network dependencies. IPC channels between renderer and main process should be typed via a preload API to avoid contextBridge security issues.
reasoner_reviews.security: SQLCipher encryption for the local database is appropriate for confidential license data. License key fields should have an additional application-layer encryption using a key derived from the admin password. JWT tokens should be stored in Electron safeStorage (OS keychain) rather than plain localStorage.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Offline-first constraint is well-addressed by SQLite. macOS code signing and Windows Authenticode signing via GitHub Actions Electron Builder are required for installer trust; unsigned installers will trigger security warnings that block enterprise deployment.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The DAW plugin scanner needs a concrete strategy for locating VST3/AU plugin bundles across DAW-specific registry locations (VST3 standard paths, Logic AU Component Registry, Pro Tools VENUE plugins). A generic filesystem scan will produce false positives from non-DAW software.

backend.service_design: Electron main process services: PluginScannerService (traverses VST3/AU standard paths and DAW-specific registries), LicenseRegistryService (CRUD on SQLite licenses and plugins tables), ComplianceEngineService (cross-references scanned plugins against license registry), ReportGeneratorService (PDF generation via PDFKit).
backend.api_patterns: Typed Electron IPC channels via preload script contextBridge: 'plugin:scan', 'plugin:list', 'license:create', 'license:update', 'license:list', 'compliance:run', 'report:generate'. Each channel uses TypeScript discriminated union request/response types.
backend.failure_handling: Plugin scan errors logged to local log file with plugin path and error type; scan continues on individual failures. SQLite write failures trigger Electron dialog with option to retry. PDF generation failures show in-app error toast with log file path.

frontend.app_structure: Electron renderer SPA with React Router v6. Main window: WorkstationDashboard, PluginInventory, LicenseRegistry, ComplianceReport, Settings. Preload script exposes typed IPC API to renderer. No Node.js access from renderer process (contextIsolation: true).
frontend.pages_and_flows: WorkstationDashboard: scan trigger button, compliance summary badges (licensed/unlicensed/expired counts). PluginInventory: table of discovered plugins with license status indicator per row. LicenseRegistry: CRUD form for adding/editing licenses with vendor, expiry date, seat count. ComplianceReport: flagged violations grouped by workstation and plugin, PDF export button.
frontend.state_management: Zustand for local UI state (selected workstation, scan progress). React Query for IPC-backed data with staleTime=0 (always fresh from SQLite on focus).
frontend.frontend_security_notes: contextIsolation: true, nodeIntegration: false, sandbox: true in BrowserWindow config. All Node.js access via preload contextBridge API. CSP header set to default-src 'self' in renderer.

security.auth_design: Admin logs in with username/password on app launch. Credentials hashed with bcrypt and stored in SQLite users table. JWT signed with HS256 and stored in Electron safeStorage (OS keychain). Token validated on every IPC call in main process middleware. Engineer role token scoped to own workstation_id.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: admin role sees all workstations and can manage license registry; engineer role read-only access to own machine plugin list. IPC handler checks jwt.role before executing privileged operations.
security.secrets_management: SQLCipher database key derived via PBKDF2 from admin master password with 100K iterations. License key field values encrypted with AES-256-GCM using a separate derived key. Keys never written to disk in plaintext.

data.entities: plugins (id INT PK, name, vendor, version, format ENUM vst3/au/aax, plugin_path, scan_checksum, workstation_id FK, discovered_at). licenses (id INT PK, plugin_id FK NULLABLE, vendor, product_name, license_key_encrypted, seat_count, expiry_date, license_type ENUM perpetual/subscription/trial, notes, created_at, updated_at). workstations (id INT PK, hostname, mac_address, os_version, last_scan_at, assigned_engineer_id FK). license_assignments (id INT PK, license_id FK, workstation_id FK, assigned_at, assigned_by). audit_reports (id INT PK, generated_at, generated_by, report_path, violations_count). vendors (id INT PK, name, contact_email, website, support_phone).
data.storage_design: Single SQLite file at ~/Library/Application Support/PluginManager/db.sqlite (macOS) or %APPDATA%/PluginManager/db.sqlite (Windows), encrypted with SQLCipher. Daily backup copy written to backup_{date}.sqlite in same directory.
data.retention_and_deletion: Audit reports and compliance history retained indefinitely. Decommissioned plugins flagged is_archived=true; hard-deleted after 3 years per retention policy.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions: lint (ESLint + Prettier), test (Jest + Playwright desktop), Electron Builder for macOS (DMG with code signing via Apple Developer certificate) and Windows (MSI with Authenticode signing). Artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases.
devops.ci_cd_design: PR checks: ESLint, TypeScript strict compile, Jest unit tests (80% coverage gate), Electron Builder dry-run. Release tag triggers full build with code signing and GitHub Release upload.
devops.observability_stack: Sentry SDK (opt-in, no PII) for crash reports. Local structured JSON log to application log directory with levels info/warn/error. Log rotation: keep last 30 days.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an offline-first Electron desktop app for VST/AU plugin license compliance. Key decisions: SQLite with SQLCipher for confidential local storage, contextBridge IPC pattern for renderer-to-main security boundary, RBAC JWT stored in OS keychain via Electron safeStorage. PDF audit report generation via PDFKit in main process. Plugin discovery must cover VST3 standard paths (/Library/Audio/Plug-Ins/VST3 on macOS, C:/Program Files/Common Files/VST3 on Windows) and AU Component Registry on macOS.
target_output.title: DAW Plugin License Compliance Manager
target_output.executive_summary: An Electron 30 desktop application for professional music studios that inventories installed VST3 and AU plugins across workstations, cross-references each against a local license registry, flags expired or unlicensed instruments, and exports PDF compliance audit reports. Built with React 18 and TypeScript in the renderer, Node.js 22 in the main process, and SQLite encrypted with SQLCipher for confidential license data. Fully offline; no internet dependency.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single Electron process pair: renderer (React 18 SPA with Tailwind CSS, Zustand state, React Query for IPC calls) communicates exclusively via contextBridge preload API to the main process (Node.js 22 with Express-style IPC router). Main process hosts four services: PluginScannerService traverses VST3/AU standard OS paths and DAW-specific registries; LicenseRegistryService manages the SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite database; ComplianceEngineService cross-references scan results against license records; ReportGeneratorService produces PDF reports via PDFKit. RBAC JWT auth with admin and engineer roles controls IPC access. All data stays on-device; no network egress outside studio LAN.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Electron 30, React 18, TypeScript 5.4, Tailwind CSS 3, Zustand 4, React Query 5, React Router 6, PDFKit 0.14 (main process). Backend: Node.js 22 in Electron main process, better-sqlite3 with SQLCipher extension, bcrypt for password hashing, jsonwebtoken for JWT, Electron safeStorage for keychain token storage. Build: Electron Builder 24 for macOS DMG and Windows MSI with code signing. Testing: Jest, Playwright for Electron. CI: GitHub Actions with Apple Developer certificate and Authenticode secrets.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Plugin Scanner: detects installed VST3 plugins at /Library/Audio/Plug-Ins/VST3 (macOS) and C:/Program Files/Common Files/VST3 (Windows), AU plugins via macOS Component Registry, and AAX via Pro Tools plugin folder. License Registry: admin CRUD for vendor, product name, license key (encrypted), seat count, expiry date, and license type (perpetual/subscription/trial). Compliance Engine: for each discovered plugin, looks up matching license by vendor+product name; flags as unlicensed (no matching license), expired (expiry_date < today), or over-seat (plugin count > seat_count). Audit Report PDF: lists all flagged violations grouped by workstation and plugin, with license details and recommended remediation. License Expiry Alerts: on app launch, checks licenses expiring within 30 days and displays dismissable notification.
target_output.system_components: Electron Main Process (Node.js 22): IPC router with JWT middleware, PluginScannerService, LicenseRegistryService, ComplianceEngineService, ReportGeneratorService (PDFKit), SQLite connection pool (better-sqlite3 + SQLCipher). Electron Renderer Process (React 18 SPA): WorkstationDashboard, PluginInventory table, LicenseRegistry CRUD forms, ComplianceReport view with PDF export trigger, Settings page. Preload Script: contextBridge exposing typed IPC channels (plugin:scan, license:*, compliance:run, report:generate). SQLite Database: SQLCipher-encrypted single file with plugins, licenses, workstations, license_assignments, audit_reports, vendors, users tables. Local Log File: structured JSON at OS application support directory.
target_output.workflows: Plugin Scan and Compliance Check: admin clicks 'Scan Workstation' on WorkstationDashboard. Renderer calls plugin:scan IPC channel. Main process PluginScannerService enumerates VST3/AU/AAX paths, reads plugin bundle metadata (name, vendor, version), computes SHA256 checksum for change detection, upserts to plugins table with workstation_id. ComplianceEngineService runs for the workstation: SELECT all plugins WHERE workstation_id=X, LEFT JOIN licenses on vendor+product_name, evaluate expired/unlicensed/over-seat flags, write compliance_flags to plugins rows. WorkstationDashboard updates with violation count badges. Audit Report Export: admin navigates to ComplianceReport view, selects workstations and date range, clicks 'Export PDF'. Renderer calls report:generate IPC. Main process ReportGeneratorService queries flagged plugins grouped by workstation, generates multi-page PDF via PDFKit with studio logo header, violation table per workstation, license detail appendix, saves to ~/Documents/PluginAudit_{date}.pdf, opens in system PDF viewer.
target_output.data_model: plugins (id INTEGER PK, name TEXT NOT NULL, vendor TEXT NOT NULL, version TEXT, format TEXT CHECK(format IN ('vst3','au','aax')), plugin_path TEXT UNIQUE, scan_checksum TEXT, compliance_status TEXT CHECK(compliance_status IN ('licensed','unlicensed','expired','over_seat')), workstation_id INTEGER FK, discovered_at TEXT). licenses (id INTEGER PK, vendor TEXT NOT NULL, product_name TEXT NOT NULL, license_key_encrypted BLOB, seat_count INTEGER DEFAULT 1, expiry_date TEXT, license_type TEXT CHECK(license_type IN ('perpetual','subscription','trial')), notes TEXT, plugin_id INTEGER FK NULLABLE, created_at TEXT, updated_at TEXT). workstations (id INTEGER PK, hostname TEXT UNIQUE, mac_address TEXT, os_version TEXT, last_scan_at TEXT, assigned_engineer_id INTEGER FK). license_assignments (id INTEGER PK, license_id INTEGER FK, workstation_id INTEGER FK, assigned_at TEXT, assigned_by INTEGER FK). audit_reports (id INTEGER PK, generated_at TEXT, generated_by INTEGER FK, report_path TEXT, violations_count INTEGER). vendors (id INTEGER PK, name TEXT UNIQUE, contact_email TEXT, website TEXT). users (id INTEGER PK, username TEXT UNIQUE, password_hash TEXT, role TEXT CHECK(role IN ('admin','engineer')), workstation_id INTEGER FK NULLABLE).
target_output.api_design: Typed Electron IPC channels via contextBridge preload API. plugin:scan {workstation_id} -> {plugins_found: int, violations: int, duration_ms: int}. plugin:list {workstation_id, compliance_status?} -> Plugin[]. license:create {vendor, product_name, license_key, seat_count, expiry_date, license_type} -> License. license:update {id, ...partial} -> License. license:list {vendor?, expiry_before?} -> License[]. license:delete {id} -> {success: bool}. compliance:run {workstation_id} -> {licensed: int, unlicensed: int, expired: int, over_seat: int}. report:generate {workstation_ids: int[], date_range?: {from, to}} -> {report_path: string}. All handlers validate JWT role; admin-only handlers reject engineer tokens with IPC error code 403.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SQLCipher encrypts the entire SQLite database with AES-256-CBC using a key derived via PBKDF2 (100K iterations, SHA-256) from the admin master password entered on first launch. License key field values are additionally encrypted with AES-256-GCM before storage. JWT tokens signed with HS256 and stored in Electron safeStorage (OS keychain/Credential Manager) rather than localStorage. Every IPC handler validates JWT and checks role before execution. contextIsolation: true and nodeIntegration: false enforced in BrowserWindow config; renderer has no direct Node.js access. App is signed with Apple Developer ID (macOS) and Authenticode certificate (Windows) to pass Gatekeeper and SmartScreen. No network calls outside studio LAN; all vendor API integrations are Phase 2 with explicit user consent.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI pipeline: ESLint + Prettier lint check, TypeScript strict compile (tsc --noEmit), Jest unit tests with 80% coverage gate, Playwright Electron integration tests for scan and report flows. Release workflow triggered by semver tags: Electron Builder generates macOS DMG (signed with Apple Developer certificate stored as GitHub Actions secret) and Windows MSI (Authenticode signed). Artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases with SHA-256 checksums. Distribution: studio admin downloads signed installer from GitHub Releases and deploys via studio asset management system. Update mechanism: manual installer re-run per release; auto-update via Electron Updater deferred to Phase 2.
target_output.observability: Sentry Electron SDK for crash and unhandled exception reporting; opt-in during first-launch setup wizard; no PII or license data included in Sentry payloads (breadcrumbs filtered). Local structured JSON log at ~/Library/Logs/PluginManager/app.log (macOS) or %APPDATA%/PluginManager/logs/app.log (Windows), rotated daily, retaining 30 days. Log fields: timestamp, level, service, message, error_code. Studio admin can export log bundle from Settings for support escalations.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero ongoing infrastructure cost; entirely on-premise. One-time development cost of ~8 engineering weeks for MVP. Electron Builder signing requires $99/year Apple Developer account and a Digicert Authenticode certificate (~$200/year). Plugin scan performance: filesystem traversal of standard VST3/AU paths scans ~500 plugins in under 3 seconds on modern SSDs. SQLite handles up to 50 concurrent workstation records comfortably. Future Phase 2 network scan adds complexity but not infrastructure cost.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Single-workstation plugin scan (VST3/AU paths on macOS and Windows), manual license entry CRUD, compliance flag generation (unlicensed/expired/over_seat), basic PDF audit report export, admin and engineer RBAC, SQLCipher database, signed installer for macOS and Windows. Phase 2 (5 weeks): Network-wide multi-workstation scan via SSH/WMI probe agents on LAN, automatic license expiry email reminders, iLok license API read integration for automated license validation. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Waves Central and Native Instruments Service Center API integrations, bulk license import via CSV, dashboard analytics for license utilization by vendor.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode with no-any rule. ESLint with eslint-plugin-react and @typescript-eslint. All IPC channel types defined in shared/ipc-types.ts imported by both preload and renderer. Main process services must not import renderer code; renderer must not import main process modules. Jest for unit tests on scanner, compliance engine, and report generator. Playwright for Electron E2E tests covering scan flow and PDF generation. No license keys or credentials in version control; use .env.local for development secrets with .gitignore entry.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: VST3/AU plugin path conventions differ by DAW (Pro Tools uses AAX paths outside standard VST3 folder; Logic uses AU Component Registry that requires macOS API calls not filesystem scan). Mitigation: implement DAW-specific scan adapters with fallback to standard paths; document unsupported DAWs in Phase 1 release notes. Risk: SQLCipher key derivation ties database access to admin master password; if password is forgotten, database is unrecoverable. Mitigation: offer secure backup export on password change. Tradeoff: Electron app size (~150MB) is near the 200MB constraint; tree-shaking and asar optimization required. Tradeoff: local-only architecture avoids network complexity but prevents multi-admin concurrent access; acceptable for studio use case.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed SQLite over PostgreSQL as no server infrastructure is available or desired. Selected PDFKit over Puppeteer for PDF generation to avoid Chromium bundle size overhead (~180MB). Chose Electron safeStorage over electron-keytar for JWT persistence as safeStorage is built-in since Electron 15 and covers both macOS Keychain and Windows Credential Manager. VST3 standard paths confirmed as primary scan targets for MVP; DAW-specific AAX/AU registry scanning added to Phase 2 scope.

metadata.notes: First-pass desktop app for DAW plugin license compliance; offline-first SQLite + SQLCipher with Electron contextBridge security pattern.

project_goal.value: Desktop application for field recording engineers that securely stores and organizes location audio sessions in encrypted project vaults, with role-based access for remote post-production teams to decrypt and review sessions
project_goal.rationale: Unreleased commercial audio content requires NDA-grade protection both on-device and during remote collaboration

target_users.value: Field recording engineers on location and remote post-production editors at studios
target_users.rationale: Two distinct roles with different access needs: engineers create/own vaults, editors get read-only remote access

project_class.rationale: Tauri application running on location laptops; no browser UI; native filesystem and hardware access required

capabilities.rationale: SvelteKit frontend, Rust backend, SQLCipher data layer, Keycloak auth

complexity_level.rationale: Vault encryption, RBAC, and remote access sync are moderately complex; bounded scope without distributed systems

risk_level.rationale: Unreleased commercial recordings subject to studio NDAs; unauthorized access could cause major financial and reputational harm

data_sensitivity.rationale: Unreleased music and film audio under active NDA agreements

external_exposure.rationale: Remote post-production editors access vaults over authenticated channel; not public internet facing

access_model.value: Keycloak OIDC with engineer and editor roles; engineer owns vault and issues time-limited access grants to editors; mandatory re-authentication before raw audio export
access_model.rationale: Vault ownership model aligns with NDA terms assigning responsibility to the recording engineer

feature_scope.value: Encrypted project vault creation and management, session metadata capture, RBAC access grants to remote editors, automatic vault locking on idle timeout, export authorization with re-auth gate, audit log of all access events
feature_scope.rationale: Full security feature set for NDA-compliant field recording workflow

mvp_scope.value: Vault creation with SQLCipher encryption, session metadata entry, local engineer access, idle timeout auto-lock, basic audit log
mvp_scope.rationale: Remote editor access and export gate deferred to Phase 2 after local vault security validated

security_baseline.value: SQLCipher AES-256 for vault database, vault auto-lock after 10-minute idle, mandatory Keycloak re-authentication before export, mTLS for remote editor access sync, audit log signed with engineer private key
security_baseline.rationale: High risk level and confidential data require defense-in-depth controls

frontend_stack.rationale: Tauri provides native desktop with smaller binary than Electron; SvelteKit for reactive UI; Rust backend for cryptographic operations

backend_stack.value: Rust with Axum for Tauri commands and optional sync HTTP server
backend_stack.rationale: Rust's memory safety properties align with high-security requirements; Axum for optional local sync endpoint

data_platform.value: SQLite encrypted via SQLCipher, embedded in Tauri app data directory
data_platform.rationale: Offline-capable encrypted local storage appropriate for field conditions

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal on location laptops (macOS and Windows); no cloud hosting
hosting_target.rationale: Field recordings captured in locations without reliable internet; fully local required

privacy_retention_policy.value: Vault contents retained until project owner explicitly deletes; audit logs retained 5 years per studio NDA terms; access grants expire after 30 days maximum
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Studio NDA audit trail requirements

future_scope.value: P2P encrypted vault sync between engineer and editor machines, automatic NDA watermark embedding in exported audio, integration with Avid Pro Tools session import
future_scope.rationale: Phase 3 roadmap items requiring additional infrastructure

constraints.value: Must operate fully offline in field; binary size under 50MB (Tauri target); macOS 13+ and Windows 11 support
constraints.rationale: Field locations have no internet; Tauri binary budget is stricter than Electron

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for crash reporting (no audio data in payloads); local audit log signed with engineer private key; Tauri log plugin for structured app logs
observability_baseline.rationale: Desktop app; signed audit log is the primary compliance artifact

execution_preference.value: Security-first milestone delivery; each milestone independently auditable
execution_preference.rationale: High-risk classification requires security review at each milestone

compliance_context.value: Studio NDA agreements require tamper-evident audit logs, vault encryption at rest, and time-limited access grants; unauthorized access must be detectable and attributable
compliance_context.rationale: Commercial audio NDA enforcement requires technical controls, not just contractual language

project.goal: Encrypted project vault management for field audio with RBAC remote access
project.target_users: Field recording engineers and remote post-production editors
project.timeline: Security milestone delivery; MVP in 10 weeks

frontend.state: Svelte stores for local UI; Tauri invoke for backend commands

backend.framework: Tauri commands + Axum HTTP server for optional sync
backend.api: Tauri invoke commands typed via serde; local Axum endpoints for sync

security.auth: Keycloak OIDC with engineer/editor roles
security.encryption: SQLCipher AES-256 for vault DB; vault key derived from Keycloak token + device key
security.transport: mTLS for remote editor sync channel

devops.ci: GitLab CI: cargo test, Tauri build (macOS and Windows), codesigning
devops.hosting: Bare-metal laptop deployment via signed installer

open_questions.key_derivation: Whether vault key should be derived from Keycloak token alone or combined with device TPM/Secure Enclave

reasoner_reviews.product: The encrypted vault model is well-matched to field recording NDA requirements. Engineer-owned vaults with time-limited editor grants match how studios actually distribute access rights. Auto-lock and export re-auth are essential security controls for portable devices.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Tauri 2 with Rust backend is the right choice: Rust memory safety reduces cryptographic implementation risk, Tauri binary budget is met, and Axum provides a clean local sync HTTP server if needed. The vault key derivation strategy is the critical architectural decision — it must survive Keycloak session expiry in offline field conditions.
reasoner_reviews.security: Round 1 plan did not specify how the vault decryption key persists between offline sessions. If the key is derived purely from the Keycloak OIDC token, the vault becomes inaccessible when the token expires in the field. A device-bound key component stored in OS Secure Enclave (macOS) or TPM (Windows) combined with the Keycloak credential is required for offline resilience with strong binding.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Tauri 2 binary with SvelteKit frontend and Rust SQLCipher bindings is estimated at 35-40MB, within the 50MB budget. GitLab CI code signing requires Apple Developer certificate and Windows Authenticode secret stored as CI variables.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The audit log signing mechanism was mentioned but not specified in round 1. A tamper-evident log requires append-only storage and cryptographic chaining — either HMAC-chained entries or Ed25519 signatures per entry with the engineer's key. Without this, the audit log cannot serve as a compliance artifact under NDA terms.

backend.service_design: Rust Tauri commands: vault_create (generates vault ID, derives SQLCipher key, creates encrypted DB), vault_open (prompts re-auth if idle timeout exceeded), vault_lock (zeroes in-memory key), session_create (inserts recording_sessions row), access_grant_create (issues time-limited editor grant with encrypted key share), export_authorize (requires Keycloak re-auth, writes audit event, returns signed export token).
backend.failure_handling: Vault open failure on wrong key logged and locked after 3 attempts with 30-second backoff. SQLCipher error on DB open triggers vault corruption detection dialog. Network unavailability for Keycloak re-auth during export falls back to offline re-auth via cached credential hash with expiry check.
backend.scaling_notes: Single-user local app; no scaling concerns. SQLite handles up to 10K session records per vault without performance issues.

frontend.app_structure: Tauri 2 SvelteKit SPA. Pages: VaultDashboard, SessionEntry, AccessGrants, ExportCenter, AuditLog, Settings. Tauri invoke calls wrapped in typed service modules. Auto-lock overlay triggered by idle timer in Svelte store.
frontend.pages_and_flows: VaultDashboard: list of sessions with status badges (locked/open/exported). SessionEntry: form for location, date, microphone config, take notes, file paths. AccessGrants: table of active grants with expiry countdown and revoke action. ExportCenter: select sessions, trigger export_authorize (re-auth modal), download signed export package.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: Tauri CSP: default-src 'self'; no external URLs. All Tauri commands use allowlist to prevent untrusted invoke calls. Auto-lock overlay rendered at z-index 9999 on idle timeout event from Rust side.

security.auth_design: Keycloak OIDC PKCE flow via Tauri shell plugin for browser-based auth. ID token parsed for role (engineer/editor). Vault key = PBKDF2(keycloak_sub + device_key, salt, 200K iterations) where device_key is retrieved from macOS Secure Enclave or Windows TPM via Tauri plugin. Key held only in Rust memory; never written to disk.
security.authorization_model: Engineer role: full vault CRUD, access grant issuance, export with re-auth. Editor role: read-only session metadata and audio file access within grant window; no export. IPC command handlers enforce role check before execution.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Audit log entries stored in audit_events table; each entry includes event_type, user_id, vault_id, timestamp, and Ed25519 signature signed with engineer private key stored in OS keychain. Log entries are append-only (no UPDATE/DELETE on audit_events). Compliance export produces a JSON-L audit file with chained signatures.

data.entities: project_vaults (id UUID PK, name, owner_id, created_at, last_locked_at). recording_sessions (id UUID PK, vault_id FK, location_name, recorded_date, duration_seconds, microphone_config_json, take_notes, file_paths_json, created_at). audio_files (id UUID PK, session_id FK, relative_path, format, sample_rate, bit_depth, file_size_bytes, checksum_sha256). team_members (id UUID PK, keycloak_sub, display_name, role ENUM engineer/editor, created_at). access_grants (id UUID PK, vault_id FK, grantee_id FK, granted_by FK, encrypted_key_share BLOB, expires_at, revoked_at). audit_events (id UUID PK, event_type, vault_id FK, actor_id FK, resource_id, timestamp, signature_ed25519 BLOB).
data.storage_design: SQLite with SQLCipher in Tauri app data directory. Audio files stored on filesystem with paths referenced in audio_files table. Vault key never persisted; derived on each unlock.
data.retention_and_deletion: Vault data retained until engineer explicitly deletes. Access grants auto-expired at expires_at; soft-deleted (revoked_at set). Audit events append-only; never deleted.

devops.deployment_topology: GitLab CI pipeline: cargo test + clippy, Tauri build for macOS (DMG, Apple Developer code signed) and Windows (MSI, Authenticode signed). Artifacts stored in GitLab Package Registry.
devops.ci_cd_design: MR checks: cargo clippy, cargo test (including SQLCipher integration tests), Tauri build dry-run. Release pipeline triggered by semver tag: full Tauri build with code signing secrets injected from GitLab CI variables.
devops.observability_stack: Sentry Rust SDK for panics and unhandled errors (no audio data in payloads). Tauri log plugin for structured app logs at info/warn/error levels. Audit log as primary compliance artifact.
