previous_plan.title: Field Recording Vault Manager Architecture v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Tauri 2 desktop app with SvelteKit UI and Rust backend managing SQLCipher-encrypted vaults for field audio sessions. Keycloak OIDC auth, auto-lock, and export re-auth gate. Audit log of all vault access events.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Tauri 2 SPA with SvelteKit renderer, Rust Tauri commands for vault operations, SQLite + SQLCipher for encrypted storage. Keycloak OIDC for auth. Vault key derived from Keycloak token via PBKDF2.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Keycloak OIDC with engineer/editor roles. SQLCipher AES-256 vault encryption. Auto-lock after 10-minute idle. Export requires Keycloak re-auth. Audit log of all vault access and export events.
previous_plan.data_model: project_vaults, recording_sessions, audio_files, team_members, access_grants, audit_events tables in SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve two blocking security issues: offline vault key derivation (ISS-001) and audit log tamper-evidence (ISS-002). For ISS-001: vault key = PBKDF2(keycloak_sub + device_key, salt, 200K) where device_key is retrieved from macOS Secure Enclave or Windows TPM — this survives token expiry because keycloak_sub is stable and device_key is hardware-bound, not session-dependent. For ISS-002: Ed25519 per-entry signatures using engineer private key stored in OS keychain, with append-only enforcement via SQLite trigger blocking DELETE and UPDATE on audit_events.

fix_report.action_taken: Revised vault key derivation to PBKDF2(keycloak_sub + device_key, vault_salt, 200K iterations, SHA-256) where keycloak_sub is the stable user identifier from the ID token and device_key is a 32-byte random key stored in macOS Secure Enclave (via CryptoKit SecureEnclave) or Windows TPM (via Microsoft Platform Crypto Provider). Vault key is held only in Rust memory (Zeroize-on-drop), never written to disk. Offline re-unlock uses cached keycloak_sub from last successful auth combined with device_key; no live Keycloak session required.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Vault remains unlockable during multi-day offline field sessions after initial authentication; keycloak_sub stable identifier survives token expiry

fix_report.action_taken: Added Ed25519 per-entry audit log signing: engineer Ed25519 private key generated at account setup and stored in macOS Keychain or Windows DPAPI-protected store. Each audit_events row includes signature_ed25519 = Ed25519Sign(private_key, event_type || vault_id || actor_id || timestamp). SQLite trigger BEFORE DELETE/UPDATE ON audit_events raises error preventing modification. Compliance export generates JSON-L file of all events with signatures for third-party verification.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Audit log entries are cryptographically attributable to engineer and cannot be silently deleted or modified; NDA compliance artifact requirement met

target_output.title: Field Recording Vault Manager Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Tauri 2 desktop application for field recording engineers that stores location audio sessions in SQLCipher AES-256 encrypted project vaults with device-bound key derivation (Secure Enclave/TPM) that survives Keycloak token expiry in offline field conditions. Supports time-limited RBAC access grants to remote post-production editors, mandatory export re-authentication, and an Ed25519-signed append-only audit log that satisfies studio NDA tamper-evidence requirements. Built with SvelteKit TypeScript frontend and Rust Axum backend; runs offline on location laptops under 50MB.
target_output.architecture_overview: Tauri 2 application with SvelteKit TypeScript renderer communicating to Rust backend via typed Tauri invoke commands. Rust backend manages vault operations: SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite database for session metadata and access grants; audio files stored on filesystem with SHA-256 checksums in DB. Vault decryption key = PBKDF2(keycloak_sub + device_key, vault_salt, 200K, SHA-256) — device_key is a hardware-bound 32-byte secret from macOS Secure Enclave or Windows TPM, ensuring vault accessibility offline after initial auth. Keycloak OIDC PKCE flow via Tauri shell plugin for auth; stable keycloak_sub used for key derivation, not the expiring token itself. Audit events table is append-only with Ed25519 per-entry signatures using engineer private key from OS keychain. Auto-lock zeroes the in-memory vault key after 10-minute idle. Export requires live Keycloak re-authentication.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Tauri 2, SvelteKit, TypeScript 5.4, Tailwind CSS 3, Svelte stores. Backend: Rust 1.78, Axum 0.7, SQLx with SQLCipher, ring (AES-256, PBKDF2, Ed25519), Zeroize for key memory clearing, Tauri plugins (log, shell, keychain). Auth: Keycloak OIDC 24 via Tauri shell browser. Security: macOS Secure Enclave via CryptoKit (Swift bridge), Windows TPM via Microsoft Platform Crypto Provider (C FFI). Build: Tauri CLI 2, GitLab CI with Apple Developer and Authenticode signing secrets. Testing: cargo test, Playwright Tauri.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Vault Creation: engineer creates named vault; Rust generates vault_id, retrieves or creates device_key from Secure Enclave/TPM, derives SQLCipher key via PBKDF2(keycloak_sub+device_key, salt), initializes encrypted SQLite DB. Session Entry: form for location, date, microphone configuration, take notes, and audio file path references; SHA-256 checksum computed per file. Access Grants: engineer issues time-limited (max 30 days) encrypted key share to editor's Keycloak account; editor decrypts using their Keycloak sub + their device key. Export Authorization: export triggers Keycloak re-auth modal; on success, Rust signs export manifest with engineer Ed25519 key and packages selected sessions. Audit Log: every vault open, session create, access grant, and export event written to append-only audit_events table with Ed25519 signature. Auto-Lock: idle timer in Rust zeroes vault key after 10 minutes; UI shows locked overlay.
target_output.system_components: Tauri 2 Runtime: Rust process hosting vault commands, SQLCipher connection, crypto operations (PBKDF2, Ed25519, AES-256-GCM). SvelteKit Renderer: VaultDashboard, SessionEntry, AccessGrants, ExportCenter, AuditLog, Settings pages; communicates via typed Tauri invoke. Keycloak OIDC: self-hosted or studio-managed Keycloak instance for auth; accessed via Tauri shell plugin browser during online sessions. SQLCipher Database: project_vaults, recording_sessions, audio_files, team_members, access_grants, audit_events; encrypted with PBKDF2-derived key. OS Keychain Integration: macOS Secure Enclave (device_key) and Keychain (Ed25519 private key); Windows TPM (device_key) and DPAPI (Ed25519 private key). Sentry SDK: crash reporting without audio data.
target_output.workflows: Vault Unlock (offline-capable): engineer launches app, enters Keycloak credentials (or uses cached keycloak_sub if offline). Rust retrieves device_key from Secure Enclave/TPM, derives vault key = PBKDF2(keycloak_sub + device_key, vault_salt, 200K, SHA-256), opens SQLCipher DB. If Keycloak is unreachable and cached keycloak_sub matches last authenticated user, offline unlock proceeds using cached sub + device_key without live token validation. Vault re-locks after 10-minute idle by zeroing key in Rust memory. Session Recording Entry: engineer opens unlocked vault, creates session via SessionEntry form (location, date, microphone_config_json, take_notes). Rust computes SHA-256 for each referenced audio file path, inserts recording_sessions and audio_files rows, writes audit_events entry (event_type='session_create', Ed25519 signed). Access Grant Issuance: engineer opens AccessGrants, selects editor from team_members, sets expiry (max 30 days), clicks grant. Rust generates encrypted_key_share = AES-256-GCM(editor_keycloak_sub + editor_device_key_hash, vault_key), inserts access_grants row, writes signed audit event. Export Authorization: engineer selects sessions in ExportCenter, clicks Export. Rust triggers Keycloak re-auth via Tauri shell browser. On confirmed re-auth, Rust writes audit_events entry (event_type='export_authorized', signed), packages session metadata and audio file paths into a signed JSON manifest, zips with selected audio files, writes to output path.
target_output.data_model: project_vaults (id UUID PK, name TEXT NOT NULL, owner_id UUID FK, vault_salt BLOB NOT NULL, created_at TEXT, last_locked_at TEXT). recording_sessions (id UUID PK, vault_id UUID FK, location_name TEXT, recorded_date TEXT, duration_seconds INTEGER, microphone_config_json TEXT, take_notes TEXT, created_at TEXT). audio_files (id UUID PK, session_id UUID FK, relative_path TEXT NOT NULL, format TEXT, sample_rate INTEGER, bit_depth INTEGER, file_size_bytes INTEGER, checksum_sha256 TEXT NOT NULL). team_members (id UUID PK, keycloak_sub TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, display_name TEXT, role TEXT CHECK(role IN ('engineer','editor')), created_at TEXT). access_grants (id UUID PK, vault_id UUID FK, grantee_id UUID FK, granted_by UUID FK, encrypted_key_share BLOB NOT NULL, expires_at TEXT NOT NULL, revoked_at TEXT). audit_events (id UUID PK, event_type TEXT NOT NULL, vault_id UUID FK, actor_id UUID FK, resource_id TEXT, timestamp TEXT NOT NULL, signature_ed25519 BLOB NOT NULL). SQLite trigger: BEFORE DELETE OR UPDATE ON audit_events BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'audit log is append-only'); END.
target_output.api_design: Typed Tauri invoke commands (Rust handlers). vault_create {name: String} -> VaultInfo. vault_open {vault_id: String, offline: bool} -> VaultStatus. vault_lock {vault_id: String} -> (). session_create {vault_id: String, location: String, date: String, mic_config: Value, notes: String, file_paths: Vec<String>} -> SessionInfo. access_grant_create {vault_id: String, grantee_keycloak_sub: String, expires_in_days: u32} -> GrantInfo. export_authorize {vault_id: String, session_ids: Vec<String>, output_path: String} -> ExportManifest. audit_log_export {vault_id: String} -> String (JSON-L file path). All commands return Result<T, VaultError>; error types include VaultLocked, AuthRequired, InsufficientRole, CryptoFailure.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Vault key derivation: PBKDF2(keycloak_sub + device_key, vault_salt, 200K iterations, SHA-256) producing 32-byte SQLCipher key. device_key is a hardware-bound 32-byte secret in macOS Secure Enclave (CryptoKit SecureEnclave.P256 derived symmetric key) or Windows TPM (Microsoft Platform Crypto Provider ECDH key). Vault key held only in Rust zeroized memory (Zeroize trait on drop); never serialized to disk. Ed25519 engineer private key stored in macOS Keychain (kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly) or Windows DPAPI-protected store; public key stored in team_members row. All audit_events rows have Ed25519 signature; append-only enforcement via SQLite trigger. Export requires live Keycloak re-authentication; export manifest signed with engineer key. Studio NDA compliance: vault never accessible without device_key (hardware binding), audit log tamper-evident, access grants expire automatically.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: cargo clippy (deny warnings), cargo test including SQLCipher integration tests and Ed25519 signing unit tests, Tauri build for macOS (DMG, Apple Developer ID signed, notarized) and Windows (MSI, Authenticode signed). Code-signing secrets injected from GitLab CI protected variables. Release artifacts published to GitLab Package Registry. Distribution: field engineers receive installer via secure studio asset portal, not public download. No auto-update mechanism (offline use case); manual installer re-run per release. Binary size target: 38MB (Tauri 2 + SvelteKit SSR + SQLCipher + ring crate), within 50MB constraint.
target_output.observability: Sentry Rust SDK for panics and unhandled errors; beforeSend filter strips any file path or session metadata from Sentry payloads. Tauri log plugin writes structured JSON logs to ~/Library/Logs/VaultManager/app.log (macOS) or %APPDATA%/VaultManager/logs/app.log (Windows), retained 30 days, rotated daily. Audit log in audit_events table is the primary compliance observability artifact; JSON-L export with Ed25519 signatures enables third-party NDA audit verification. Domain metrics relevant to operations: vault unlock latency (PBKDF2 derivation target <200ms), audit event write latency, access grant expiry countdown alerts on VaultDashboard.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost; fully local deployment. Development: approximately 10 engineering weeks for Phase 1+2 milestones. Apple Developer account ($99/year) and Authenticode certificate (~$200/year) for code signing. Rust PBKDF2 at 200K iterations completes in ~180ms on modern i7; acceptable for vault unlock UX. SQLite handles up to 100K session records per vault without performance issues. Tauri binary estimated at 38MB with Brotli-compressed SvelteKit assets.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Vault creation with SQLCipher + device-bound key derivation, session metadata entry, local engineer RBAC, auto-lock after 10-minute idle, Ed25519 append-only audit log, signed macOS and Windows installers. Phase 2 (5 weeks): Remote editor access grants with encrypted key share, export authorization with Keycloak re-auth, audit log JSON-L compliance export, Keycloak instance setup guide for studios. Phase 3 (4 weeks): P2P encrypted vault sync between engineer and editor machines via libp2p, automatic NDA watermark embedding in export audio via FFmpeg metadata injection, Pro Tools session import integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust: clippy deny(warnings) in CI, cargo audit for dependency CVEs, Zeroize on all key material structs. No raw unsafe blocks outside crypto FFI bridging. Tauri CSP: default-src 'self' only. All Tauri commands return typed Result<T, VaultError>; no panic in command handlers. TypeScript: strict mode, no-any rule. IPC command types defined in src-tauri/bindings/ auto-generated via ts-rs. cargo test must include SQLCipher open/close cycle and Ed25519 sign/verify round-trip. GitLab MR requires security review approval for any change to vault key derivation or audit log schema.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Secure Enclave/TPM device_key is hardware-bound; if engineer's laptop is lost or replaced, vault is inaccessible even with valid Keycloak credentials. Mitigation: on-boarding flow requires engineer to create an encrypted backup of device_key wrapped with a recovery passphrase, stored in a separate encrypted file. Risk: PBKDF2 200K iterations adds ~180ms to vault unlock; acceptable for interactive use but noticeable on low-end hardware. Mitigation: Argon2id with lower iteration count as fallback option documented for older devices. Tradeoff: Ed25519 private key in OS keychain means vault audit log is only signable on the engineer's registered device; by design for NDA attribution. Tradeoff: offline Keycloak fallback using cached keycloak_sub reduces security posture slightly vs. live auth; mitigated by hardware-bound device_key requirement.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Vault key derivation confirmed as PBKDF2(keycloak_sub + device_key) with device_key in Secure Enclave/TPM — resolves ISS-001. Ed25519 per-entry signing with append-only SQLite trigger confirmed for audit log — resolves ISS-002. Keycloak instance responsibility assigned to studio IT team per Phase 2 setup guide. P2P sync via libp2p chosen over SFTP for Phase 3 to avoid server infrastructure dependency.

metadata.notes: Round 2 of field recording vault manager; resolves offline key derivation (ISS-001) with Secure Enclave/TPM binding and audit log tamper-evidence (ISS-002) with Ed25519 per-entry signing.

reasoner_reviews.product: The landing page scope is appropriately minimal for a 3-day waitlist launch. Hero, audio demo clips, and waitlist CTA are the only conversion-critical sections. Pricing and FAQ can be added post-launch based on waitlist questions.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 with static export on Vercel is the correct choice. No server-side runtime needed. Audio demo clips should be hosted on Vercel's CDN as static assets rather than embedded YouTube iframes to avoid third-party cookie issues.
reasoner_reviews.security: Static page with no first-party cookies or auth is very low risk. CSP header blocking inline scripts prevents basic XSS via third-party embeds. Typeform embed will set third-party cookies; confirm with legal whether this requires GDPR notice.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 3-day timeline is achievable with Tailwind and pre-built Next.js components. Audio demo files should be compressed to MP3 at 128kbps to meet the 2-second load budget on 3G.

backend.service_design: No backend service; fully static. Waitlist form handled by Typeform embed. Analytics via Vercel Analytics script tag.
backend.api_patterns: No API. Typeform embed submits directly to Typeform's servers.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 static export SPA. Single-page layout with anchor-linked sections: Hero, Demo, Features, Waitlist, Pricing, FAQ.
frontend.pages_and_flows: index.tsx: HeroSection (tagline, CTA button), DemoSection (3 AudioDemoCard components with play/pause), FeaturesSection (3-column feature grid), WaitlistSection (Typeform embed), PricingSection (free/pro tier cards), FAQSection (accordion).
frontend.performance_notes: Next.js Image component with WebP for all images. Audio demo files: MP3 128kbps max 30s clips hosted as /public/demo/ static assets. Font: system-ui stack to eliminate font load. Tailwind CSS purge removes unused classes. Target: LCP under 1.5s on mobile.

security.auth_design: No authentication required.
security.abuse_prevention: Vercel rate limiting on deployment. Typeform handles form spam protection.

devops.observability_stack: Vercel Analytics dashboard for page views, referrers, and custom events. No additional monitoring.

target_output.thinking_summary: Static marketing landing page for a chord detection SaaS. Next.js 14 static export on Vercel with no backend; all interactivity is client-side React. Audio demo clips hosted as static MP3 assets. Vercel Analytics for cookieless tracking. Main concern is meeting the 2s load budget on 3G given audio assets.
target_output.title: Chord Detection Tool Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A static Next.js 14 landing page for a browser-based chord detection SaaS targeting songwriters who hum melodies. Deployed on Vercel with no backend; audio demo clips hosted as compressed MP3 static assets; waitlist capture via Typeform embed; cookieless analytics via Vercel Analytics.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Next.js 14 application exported as HTML/CSS/JS and served from Vercel's CDN with automatic HTTPS. No server-side runtime, no database, no authentication. React components for UI interactions (audio playback, FAQ accordion). Waitlist email capture handled by embedded Typeform form submitting to Typeform's servers. Vercel Analytics tracks page engagement events. CSP headers configured in vercel.json to block inline script execution from third-party embeds.
target_output.technology_stack: Framework: Next.js 14 with static export (output: 'export' in next.config). Language: TypeScript 5. Styling: Tailwind CSS 3 with purge enabled. UI interactions: React 18 hooks for accordion and audio player state. Analytics: Vercel Analytics (@vercel/analytics). Audio: HTML5 Audio API with MP3 128kbps demo clips. Waitlist: Typeform embed script. Hosting: Vercel with automatic CDN and TLS. CI: GitHub Actions with next build check.
target_output.functional_feature_map: HeroSection: product tagline 'Hum it. Know it. Play it.', primary CTA button scrolling to WaitlistSection. DemoSection: three AudioDemoCard components each with a 15-30 second MP3 clip of a hummed melody and the detected chord name result displayed as overlay text. FeaturesSection: three feature cards targeting songwriters — 'No music theory needed', 'Works with any hummed melody', 'Get chord names instantly'. WaitlistSection: Typeform iframe embed for email and songwriter use-case collection. PricingSection: Free tier (5 detections/day) and Pro tier ($9/month, unlimited). FAQSection: accordion with questions like 'Do I need to know music theory?'
target_output.system_components: Next.js static build output (HTML, CSS, JS bundles) served from Vercel CDN edge nodes. Audio demo MP3 files in /public/demo/ served as static assets with Cache-Control max-age=31536000. Typeform embed iframe (third-party). Vercel Analytics script (@vercel/analytics/react SpeedInsights + Analytics components). vercel.json: headers config with Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff.
target_output.workflows: Visitor Landing and Demo Interaction: visitor arrives at page, HeroSection loads with LCP target under 1.5s. Visitor scrolls to DemoSection, clicks play on AudioDemoCard. HTML5 Audio API plays MP3 clip. Vercel Analytics custom event 'demo_play' fires with demo_id property. Visitor continues scrolling, Vercel Analytics tracks scroll_depth_50 and scroll_depth_100 events. Waitlist Conversion: visitor clicks CTA in HeroSection, page smooth-scrolls to WaitlistSection. Typeform embed loads in iframe. Visitor fills songwriters use-case field and email, submits directly to Typeform. Vercel Analytics custom event 'waitlist_submitted' fires on Typeform embed postMessage callback.
target_output.data_model: No first-party data model. Static content entities: demo_clips (3 items: title, mp3_path, detected_chord_display), feature_cards (3 items: icon, headline, body), pricing_tiers (free/pro: name, price, features[]), faq_items (question, answer). All content hardcoded in TypeScript component props or JSON files imported at build time; no runtime data fetching.
target_output.api_design: No API endpoints. Single external integration: Typeform embed URL https://form.typeform.com/to/{form_id} embedded as iframe. Vercel Analytics API: client-side track() calls for custom events (demo_play, waitlist_cta_click, waitlist_submitted). No server-side API routes in Next.js config.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel; no HTTP fallback. CSP header in vercel.json: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://a.typeform.com https://va.vercel-scripts.com; frame-src https://form.typeform.com; img-src 'self' data:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'. X-Frame-Options: DENY to prevent clickjacking. No first-party cookies set; Vercel Analytics is cookieless. Typeform sets third-party cookies in its iframe; isolated from first-party origin by iframe sandbox. No user PII stored in first-party systems.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Vercel project connected to GitHub repository. Production deployment on push to main branch. PR preview deployments for every pull request with unique Vercel preview URL. GitHub Actions CI: eslint check, tsc --noEmit TypeScript compile, next build (verifies static export succeeds). next build output size target: under 300KB JS bundle (gzipped). No manual deployment steps; all automated via Vercel GitHub integration. Custom domain configured in Vercel DNS with automatic TLS certificate renewal.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics dashboard: daily unique visitors, page views, referrer breakdown, device type split. Custom events tracked via @vercel/analytics track(): demo_play (demo_id: string), waitlist_cta_click (section: string), scroll_depth (depth: 25|50|75|100). Vercel Speed Insights for Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FID) monitoring per deployment. No server logs; no custom alerting needed for static page.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Hobby tier ($0/month) sufficient for waitlist phase; scales to Pro ($20/month) if traffic exceeds 100GB bandwidth or 1000 serverless function invocations (none expected). CDN edge caching eliminates origin load at any traffic level. Audio demo files (~3MB total at 128kbps MP3) served from CDN with long-lived Cache-Control headers.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (3 days): HeroSection, DemoSection with 3 audio clips, WaitlistSection with Typeform embed, mobile-responsive Tailwind layout, Vercel deployment, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (post-waitlist, 2 weeks): FAQSection, PricingSection, blog section stub, Open Graph meta tags for social sharing. Phase 3 (when ML model ready): Replace static audio demo clips with live in-browser chord detection using TensorFlow.js or ONNX Runtime Web; user microphone input via Web Audio API.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode. ESLint with next/core-web-vitals ruleset. All images use next/image with explicit width/height to prevent CLS. Audio demo files must be MP3 128kbps maximum; no lossless audio in static assets. No inline styles; Tailwind classes only. Typeform form_id stored in environment variable NEXT_PUBLIC_TYPEFORM_ID for easy swap between staging and production forms.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Typeform embed adds third-party cookie in waitlist iframe; may require GDPR consent notice for EU visitors depending on legal review. Mitigation: evaluate Mailchimp hosted form (no cookie) as alternative. Risk: audio demo files increase page weight; 3x 1MB MP3 files add 3MB to initial page load budget. Mitigation: lazy-load audio elements; only fetch MP3 src on first play interaction. Tradeoff: Next.js static export vs. plain HTML; Next.js adds build complexity but gives TypeScript type checking and component reuse for maintainability.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Vercel Analytics over Google Analytics to avoid GDPR consent banner (cookieless). Demo audio clips will be hosted as /public/demo/ static assets rather than YouTube embeds to avoid third-party cookie from youtube.com. Typeform selected over Mailchimp for waitlist form due to conditional logic on songwriter use-case field. Static export (output: 'export') confirmed over Next.js serverless to eliminate server-side execution on Vercel.

project_goal.value: Batch automation tool for record labels that ingests thousands of audio tracks per day from GCS, extracts acoustic features (BPM, musical key, spectral energy, loudness LUFS) using Essentia or Librosa, and writes enriched metadata to a ClickHouse central catalog database
project_goal.rationale: Label catalog managers need acoustic metadata for playlist curation, sync licensing, and A&R analysis; manual tagging at current ingestion volume is infeasible

target_users.value: Record label catalog managers, A&R analysts, and sync licensing teams
target_users.rationale: Internal users who query catalog database for track discovery and licensing decisions

project_class.rationale: GCS-triggered batch pipeline with no browser UI; all value in automated feature extraction and ClickHouse ingestion

capabilities.rationale: No frontend needed; batch extraction pipeline with GCS integration and ClickHouse data sink

complexity_level.rationale: High-throughput GCS trigger ingestion, parallel audio DSP at scale, ClickHouse bulk insert optimization, and daily reconciliation jobs create significant data engineering complexity

risk_level.rationale: Proprietary audio assets and catalog metadata are business-sensitive; data loss or extraction errors would corrupt licensing decisions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Audio catalog metadata is proprietary business data; not personal or financial data but commercially sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: Batch pipeline triggered by internal GCS events; catalog DB accessed only from internal label systems

access_model.value: Custom JWT for Cloud Run Job invocation API; service accounts for GCS and ClickHouse access; no human-interactive auth flow
access_model.rationale: Machine-to-machine auth appropriate for automated batch pipeline

feature_scope.value: GCS bucket trigger on new audio file upload, parallel feature extraction workers (BPM/key/loudness/spectral), ClickHouse bulk insert of acoustic_features, catalog_entries upsert, ingestion job tracking, daily reconciliation comparing extracted vs expected counts, alert on missing extractions
feature_scope.rationale: Full production feature set for catalog acoustic enrichment

mvp_scope.value: GCS trigger on new uploads, single-worker sequential extraction (BPM and key only), ClickHouse insert, basic ingestion_jobs tracking table
mvp_scope.rationale: Parallel multi-feature extraction deferred to Wave 2 after single-feature pipeline validated at scale

security_baseline.value: GCP service accounts with least-privilege IAM roles, TLS 1.3 for all GCP API calls, customer-managed encryption key (CMEK) for GCS bucket, audit logging to Cloud Audit Logs
security_baseline.rationale: Internal tool handling proprietary audio assets; CMEK required by label content security policy

frontend_stack.rationale: Batch automation tool with no UI; catalog data queried directly from ClickHouse by existing internal tools

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for trigger API; Essentia and Librosa for audio DSP
backend_stack.rationale: Python DSP ecosystem (Essentia, Librosa) has no equivalent in other languages; FastAPI for Cloud Run trigger endpoint

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for acoustic feature catalog; Redis 7 for ingestion job queue and deduplication; GCS for audio asset storage
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse's columnar storage and vectorized aggregation queries match catalog analytics access patterns; Redis for lightweight job coordination

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run Jobs for extraction workers; Cloud Run service for trigger API
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run Jobs provides serverless batch execution with per-job parallelism; no cluster management

privacy_retention_policy.value: Acoustic feature data retained indefinitely as catalog asset; raw audio files in GCS retained per label rights agreements; ingestion_jobs logs retained 180 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Catalog acoustic metadata has indefinite business value; ingestion logs are operational data

future_scope.value: Mood and genre classification via fine-tuned audio ML model, API endpoint for catalog managers to query features, Looker Studio dashboard for A&R analytics
future_scope.rationale: Phase 3 roadmap items building on enriched catalog data

constraints.value: Extract features for 5000 tracks per day within a 4-hour processing window; ClickHouse cluster budget under $500/month; extraction errors must not silently corrupt catalog
constraints.rationale: Label catalog team SLA requires fresh acoustic data daily by 6am; budget constraint from infrastructure team

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards with Prometheus metrics; Cloud Logging for structured job logs; PagerDuty alert if daily reconciliation detects >1% missing extractions
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing GCP observability stack

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints; extraction worker features added incrementally per sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Incremental feature rollout reduces risk of introducing DSP regression

compliance_context.value: Proprietary audio assets subject to label content security policy; CMEK encryption required for GCS; catalog metadata must not leave internal GCP project boundary
compliance_context.rationale: Record label content agreements restrict audio asset handling to label-controlled infrastructure

project.goal: GCS-triggered batch pipeline extracting BPM, key, loudness, and spectral features from audio tracks and writing to ClickHouse catalog
project.target_users: Internal catalog managers and A&R analysts

backend.framework: FastAPI trigger API; Essentia + Librosa for DSP
backend.api: Cloud Run trigger endpoint; GCS Pub/Sub notification trigger

security.auth: GCP service accounts with least-privilege IAM
security.encryption: CMEK on GCS bucket; TLS 1.3 for all API calls
security.audit: Cloud Audit Logs for GCS and ClickHouse access

data.cache: Redis 7 for job queue and track deduplication
data.storage: GCS for audio assets with CMEK

devops.ci: Cloud Build: pytest, Docker build, Cloud Run Job deploy
devops.hosting: Cloud Run Jobs (workers) + Cloud Run service (trigger API)

constraints.error_handling: No silent catalog corruption on extraction failure

open_questions.deduplication: Redis-based track deduplication key strategy for GCS re-uploads of same track

reasoner_reviews.product: The acoustic feature extraction scope is well-defined and high-value for catalog operations. BPM, key, loudness, and spectral energy cover the core features needed for playlist curation and sync licensing. The daily reconciliation job is essential to maintain catalog integrity given the scale.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Cloud Run Jobs with parallel task indices is the right hosting model for batch audio processing. Each job invocation processes a partition of the daily track list, with parallelism scaled to meet the 4-hour window SLA. ClickHouse's ReplacingMergeTree engine handles upserts for re-extracted tracks without duplication. The round 2 addition of Redis-based deduplication was correct but the deduplication TTL window was not specified.
reasoner_reviews.security: GCP service accounts with IAM least privilege (roles/storage.objectViewer on source bucket, ClickHouse connection via Private Service Connect) are appropriate. CMEK on GCS bucket satisfies label content security policy. Cloud Audit Logs capture all GCS object reads and Cloud Run Job invocations.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 5000 tracks/day in 4-hour window requires approximately 2.88 seconds per track. Cloud Run Job with 50 parallel task instances processing 100 tracks each in ~144 seconds (2.4 seconds/track via Essentia BPM+key extraction on 2 vCPU) is achievable. ClickHouse bulk insert in batches of 1000 rows satisfies throughput.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan added Redis deduplication but did not specify the deduplication key or TTL. GCS re-uploads of the same track (e.g., remasters) could either skip extraction (wrong) or re-extract and upsert (correct). The plan must specify that deduplication key = GCS object generation number, not just the filename, so remasters trigger fresh extraction.

backend.service_design: FastAPI Cloud Run service: POST /extract-batch (receives GCS Pub/Sub notification payload, validates track list, enqueues to Redis job queue, triggers Cloud Run Job). ExtractionWorker Cloud Run Job: reads track batch from Redis queue, downloads audio from GCS via signed URL, runs Essentia BPM + key extraction, Librosa RMS and spectral centroid, writes feature_extractions dict, bulk inserts to ClickHouse acoustic_features. ReconciliationJob: daily Cloud Scheduler trigger, queries ClickHouse ingestion_jobs vs GCS object count, emits missing_extractions metric.
backend.api_patterns: GCS Pub/Sub notification -> Cloud Run trigger POST /extract-batch. Cloud Run Job tasks read batch assignments from Redis LPOP on job_queue:{date} key. ClickHouse writes via clickhouse-connect bulk insert with ReplacingMergeTree upsert on (track_id, extraction_version).
backend.failure_handling: Extraction failure: worker catches exception, writes ingestion_jobs.status='failed' and error_detail. Track re-queued up to 3 times via Redis job retry counter. After 3 failures, track added to failed_extractions ClickHouse table for manual review. No silent failure: all errors written to Cloud Logging with track GCS URI and extraction stage.
backend.background_jobs: Daily reconciliation job (Cloud Scheduler 5am UTC): counts tracks in GCS bucket prefix vs acoustic_features rows in ClickHouse for same ingestion date; emits missing_count metric to Prometheus; PagerDuty alert if missing_count/total > 0.01.

security.auth_design: ExtractionWorker Cloud Run Job runs as GCP service account extract-worker@{project}.iam.gserviceaccount.com with roles/storage.objectViewer on audio-assets bucket and ClickHouse connection via Private Service Connect. FastAPI trigger service runs as trigger-api@{project}.iam.gserviceaccount.com with roles/run.invoker to trigger Cloud Run Jobs and roles/redis.editor for Redis.
security.secrets_management: ClickHouse connection credentials and Redis auth token in Secret Manager, mounted as environment variables in Cloud Run Job task container via secretEnv. Rotated every 60 days.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Cloud Audit Logs: DATA_READ logs for all GCS object accesses by extract-worker service account. Cloud Logging: structured JSON from each extraction worker task including track_id, gcs_uri, gcs_generation, features_extracted, duration_ms, and error if applicable.

data.entities: tracks (track_id UUID, gcs_uri TEXT, gcs_generation BIGINT, label_release_id UUID, title TEXT, artist TEXT, duration_seconds FLOAT, ingested_at DATETIME). acoustic_features (track_id UUID, extraction_version UInt32, bpm FLOAT, musical_key String, loudness_lufs FLOAT, spectral_centroid_hz FLOAT, rms_energy FLOAT, extracted_at DATETIME, extractor_version String). catalog_entries (track_id UUID, title TEXT, artist TEXT, label_release_id UUID, bpm FLOAT, musical_key String, loudness_lufs FLOAT, updated_at DATETIME). ingestion_jobs (job_id UUID, ingestion_date DATE, track_id UUID, gcs_uri TEXT, gcs_generation BIGINT, status ENUM, attempts UInt8, error_detail TEXT, started_at DATETIME, finished_at DATETIME). label_releases (release_id UUID, title TEXT, artist TEXT, release_date DATE, territory TEXT, ingested_track_count UInt32). feature_extractions (id UUID, job_id UUID, track_id UUID, extractor String, parameters_json String, raw_output_json String, created_at DATETIME).
data.storage_design: ClickHouse cluster (3 shards, 1 replica each) on GCP. acoustic_features table uses ReplacingMergeTree(extracted_at) partitioned by toYYYYMM(extracted_at) ordered by (track_id, extraction_version). catalog_entries uses ReplacingMergeTree(updated_at). Bulk inserts in batches of 1000 rows. GCS bucket with CMEK for audio files; lifecycle policy deletes temporary extraction working files after 7 days.
data.consistency_notes: Redis deduplication key = SHA256(gcs_uri + ':' + gcs_generation); TTL 48 hours. Re-uploads with new GCS generation number trigger fresh extraction and upsert via ReplacingMergeTree. Re-uploads with same generation number are deduplicated by Redis and skipped.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service (trigger-api): min 0, max 3 instances, 1 vCPU/512MB. Cloud Run Job (extraction-worker): parallelism 50, max task retries 3, 2 vCPU/4GB per task, timeout 300s per task. Cloud Scheduler for daily reconciliation job at 5am UTC.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build trigger on main branch push: pytest (90% coverage gate), ruff lint, Docker build, Artifact Registry push, Cloud Run service deploy (trigger-api), Cloud Run Job update (extraction-worker). Integration tests spin up a ClickHouse container and Redis via Cloud Build substitution vars.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus metrics exported from trigger-api (/metrics): tracks_enqueued_total, tracks_extracted_total, tracks_failed_total, extraction_duration_seconds (histogram by extractor). Grafana dashboard: daily ingestion throughput, extraction success rate, p95 extraction latency per extractor, reconciliation missing_count trend. PagerDuty: reconciliation missing rate >1%, extraction failure rate >5% over 30 minutes.
devops.cost_controls: Cloud Run Job billed per vCPU-second; at 5000 tracks/day x 2.4s/task x 2 vCPU = 24,000 vCPU-seconds/day = ~$0.60/day. ClickHouse cluster on e2-standard-4 nodes: ~$280/month for 3 nodes. Redis managed on Memorystore: ~$40/month. Total ~$360/month under $500 budget.

ISS-001.title: ClickHouse insertion errors silently discarded tracks from catalog
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 used fire-and-forget ClickHouse inserts; failed inserts were not retried and tracks were never recorded as failed in ingestion_jobs

ISS-002.title: GCS re-upload deduplication key not specified
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 added Redis deduplication but used gcs_uri as the dedup key; re-uploads of remasters with same filename but new GCS generation would be incorrectly skipped

ISS-003.title: Daily reconciliation timing window not defined
ISS-003.detail: Reconciliation job timing relative to ingestion window end not specified; running reconciliation before all workers complete produces false missing counts

focus_issues.title: GCS re-upload deduplication key not specified
focus_issues.detail: Redis dedup key is gcs_uri only; re-uploads with new GCS generation number incorrectly skipped as duplicates

focus_issues.title: Daily reconciliation timing window not defined
focus_issues.detail: Reconciliation job running before extraction window closes produces false missing-extraction alerts

latest_recommendations: Change Redis deduplication key from gcs_uri to SHA256(gcs_uri + ':' + gcs_generation) so re-uploads with new generation number trigger fresh extraction
Schedule reconciliation job at 5am UTC (1 hour after 4am end-of-extraction-window) to ensure all Cloud Run Job tasks have completed before counting
