previous_plan.title: Record Label Acoustic Feature Extraction Pipeline v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: GCS-triggered Cloud Run Jobs pipeline extracting BPM, key, loudness, and spectral features from 5000 audio tracks/day using Essentia and Librosa, with ClickHouse catalog ingestion, Redis job queue, ingestion_jobs retry tracking, and daily reconciliation.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Cloud Run trigger API receives GCS Pub/Sub notifications, enqueues track batches to Redis, triggers Cloud Run Job with 50 parallel tasks. Each task downloads audio from GCS, extracts features via Essentia/Librosa, bulk inserts to ClickHouse. ingestion_jobs table tracks status and retries. Daily reconciliation at midnight compares ClickHouse counts vs GCS.
previous_plan.data_model: tracks, acoustic_features (ReplacingMergeTree), catalog_entries, ingestion_jobs, label_releases tables in ClickHouse. Redis job queue with gcs_uri dedup key.
previous_plan.workflows: GCS upload triggers Pub/Sub notification. Trigger API validates, deduplicates (Redis key=gcs_uri), enqueues to Redis, triggers Cloud Run Job. Workers extract BPM/key/loudness/spectral, bulk insert to ClickHouse, update ingestion_jobs. Reconciliation at midnight counts mismatches.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 resolves two remaining issues: ISS-002 (dedup key must include GCS generation number so remasters trigger fresh extraction) and ISS-003 (reconciliation must run 1 hour after the extraction window closes, not concurrently with it). Both fixes are straightforward and improve data integrity. The core architecture — Cloud Run Jobs with Essentia/Librosa, ClickHouse ReplacingMergeTree, Redis queue — is solid and unchanged from round 2.

fix_report.action_taken: Changed Redis deduplication key from SHA256(gcs_uri) to SHA256(gcs_uri + ':' + str(gcs_generation)) where gcs_generation is the GCS object generation number from the Pub/Sub notification payload. GCS generation numbers monotonically increase on each overwrite. Re-uploads of the same filename with a new generation produce a different dedup key and trigger fresh extraction and ClickHouse upsert via ReplacingMergeTree. Same file re-submitted (same generation) is deduplicated as before. TTL set to 48 hours.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Remasters uploaded as new GCS object generations are correctly re-extracted and upserted to catalog; same-file duplicate submissions within 48 hours continue to be deduplicated

fix_report.action_taken: Rescheduled daily reconciliation Cloud Scheduler job to 5:00am UTC, which is 1 hour after the nominal 4:00am extraction window close (5000 tracks starting midnight, ~2.4s/track with 50 parallel workers = ~240s = 4 minutes actual, but scheduled 4-hour window accounts for retries and failures). Added a pre-reconciliation check: reconciliation job queries Cloud Run Job execution status via GCP API and waits up to 30 minutes if any job tasks are still running before counting.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Reconciliation runs only after all extraction tasks are confirmed complete; false missing-extraction alerts eliminated

target_output.title: Record Label Acoustic Feature Extraction Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 GCS-triggered batch automation pipeline for record labels that ingests thousands of audio tracks per day via Cloud Pub/Sub notifications, extracts acoustic features (BPM, musical key, loudness LUFS, spectral centroid) using Essentia and Librosa on Cloud Run Jobs with 50 parallel tasks, and writes enriched metadata to a ClickHouse catalog via bulk ReplacingMergeTree upserts. Redis 7 handles job queuing with generation-aware deduplication; a daily reconciliation job at 5am UTC verifies extraction completeness with PagerDuty alerting on missing rates above 1%.
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI Cloud Run service (trigger-api) receives GCS Pub/Sub push notifications on new audio file uploads. For each notification, trigger-api extracts gcs_uri and gcs_generation, computes dedup key SHA256(gcs_uri+':'+gcs_generation), checks Redis SET NX with 48h TTL, and enqueues non-duplicate tracks to Redis job_queue:{date} list. Trigger-api then invokes a Cloud Run Job (extraction-worker) with parallelism=50. Each Cloud Run Job task LPOPs a batch of 100 tracks from Redis, downloads audio from GCS via signed URLs, runs Essentia BPM and musical key extraction and Librosa RMS + spectral centroid, writes acoustic_features dict, and bulk inserts to ClickHouse acoustic_features (ReplacingMergeTree) and updates catalog_entries. ingestion_jobs table tracks per-track status with retry counter; failed tracks are retried up to 3 times before being written to failed_extractions for manual review. Daily Cloud Scheduler at 5am UTC triggers ReconciliationJob which verifies Cloud Run Job completion before counting ClickHouse acoustic_features rows against GCS prefix object counts.
target_output.technology_stack: API and Workers: Python 3.12, FastAPI, clickhouse-connect, redis-py, google-cloud-storage, Essentia 2.1 (BPM via RhythmExtractor2013, key via KeyExtractor), Librosa 0.10 (RMS, spectral_centroid), structlog for JSON logging, prometheus-client for /metrics. Infrastructure: GCP Cloud Run (trigger-api service), Cloud Run Jobs (extraction-worker), Cloud Pub/Sub, GCS with CMEK, ClickHouse on GCE (3-shard cluster), Memorystore Redis 7, Secret Manager, Cloud Scheduler. Build: Cloud Build, Artifact Registry, Docker. Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, Cloud Logging, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: GCS Trigger Ingestion: Cloud Pub/Sub push notification to trigger-api on new object finalize event in audio-assets bucket. Generation-Aware Deduplication: Redis dedup key SHA256(gcs_uri+':'+gcs_generation) prevents re-extraction of same file while allowing remasters (new generation) to trigger fresh extraction. Parallel Feature Extraction: 50 Cloud Run Job tasks each processing 100-track batches; Essentia BPM/key and Librosa RMS/spectral_centroid extracted per track. ClickHouse Catalog Upsert: bulk insert to acoustic_features and catalog_entries using ReplacingMergeTree; remasters automatically supersede previous extractions. Extraction Retry: up to 3 retries per track with error detail logged to ingestion_jobs; permanent failures written to failed_extractions ClickHouse table. Daily Reconciliation: Cloud Scheduler at 5am UTC, waits for Cloud Run Job completion, counts ClickHouse rows vs GCS object count, emits missing_count metric, PagerDuty alert if >1% missing.
target_output.system_components: FastAPI Trigger API (Cloud Run service, min 0/max 3 instances, 1 vCPU/512MB): receives Pub/Sub push notifications, validates HMAC, computes dedup key, enqueues to Redis, triggers Cloud Run Job. ExtractionWorker (Cloud Run Job, 50 parallel tasks, 2 vCPU/4GB, 300s timeout): LPOP from Redis job queue, GCS signed URL download, Essentia BPM+key extraction, Librosa RMS+spectral, ClickHouse bulk insert, ingestion_jobs update. ReconciliationJob (Cloud Run Job, 1 task, daily): Cloud Run execution status check, GCS prefix count query, ClickHouse count query, missing_count metric emit, PagerDuty alert trigger if threshold exceeded. ClickHouse Cluster (3 shards, e2-standard-4): acoustic_features, catalog_entries, ingestion_jobs, tracks, label_releases, failed_extractions tables. Memorystore Redis 7: job_queue:{date} lists, dedup SET NX keys. GCS audio-assets bucket: CMEK encrypted, Pub/Sub notification on OBJECT_FINALIZE. Secret Manager: ClickHouse credentials and Redis auth token.
target_output.workflows: Track Ingestion Trigger: label upload pipeline writes audio file to gs://label-audio-assets/{release_id}/{track_id}.wav. GCS emits OBJECT_FINALIZE Pub/Sub message to trigger-api Cloud Run service. Trigger-api validates Pub/Sub HMAC, extracts gcs_uri and gcs_generation from message attributes. Computes dedup_key = SHA256(gcs_uri + ':' + str(gcs_generation)). Calls Redis SET dedup_key 1 NX EX 172800 (48h). If SET returns nil (duplicate), logs dedup_skipped event and returns 200. If SET returns 1, pushes track metadata JSON to Redis LPOP key job_queue:{today_date}. If job_queue length == 1 (first enqueue of day), triggers Cloud Run Job extraction-worker with job_id and date args. Feature Extraction: ExtractionWorker task_index=N LPOPs 100 tracks from Redis. For each track: downloads audio via GCS signed URL (15-min expiry), runs Essentia RhythmExtractor2013 (BPM) and KeyExtractor (musical key + scale), runs Librosa spectral_centroid and rms, assembles feature_extractions dict {bpm, musical_key, loudness_lufs via pyloudnorm, spectral_centroid_hz, rms_energy}. Bulk inserts 100-row batch to ClickHouse acoustic_features and catalog_entries. Updates ingestion_jobs.status='completed'. On exception: increments ingestion_jobs.attempts, status='failed', logs error. After 3 attempts: writes to failed_extractions table, logs permanent_failure event to Cloud Logging. Daily Reconciliation: Cloud Scheduler fires at 5:00am UTC. ReconciliationJob queries GCP Cloud Run API for extraction-worker job status; if any tasks still running, waits up to 30 minutes polling every 2 minutes. Once confirmed complete, queries SELECT count() FROM acoustic_features WHERE toDate(extracted_at) = today(). Queries GCS list_objects prefix for today's ingestion date to get expected count. Computes missing_count = expected - extracted. Emits Prometheus gauge reconciliation_missing_tracks. If missing_count / expected > 0.01, triggers PagerDuty alert with missing track IDs from ingestion_jobs WHERE status='failed'.
target_output.data_model: ClickHouse tables. tracks (track_id UUID, gcs_uri String, gcs_generation Int64, label_release_id UUID, title String, artist String, duration_seconds Float32, ingested_at DateTime). acoustic_features ENGINE ReplacingMergeTree(extracted_at) PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(extracted_at) ORDER BY (track_id, extraction_version): track_id UUID, extraction_version UInt32, bpm Float32, musical_key String, musical_scale String ENUM('major','minor'), loudness_lufs Float32, spectral_centroid_hz Float32, rms_energy Float32, extractor_version String, extracted_at DateTime. catalog_entries ENGINE ReplacingMergeTree(updated_at) ORDER BY track_id: track_id UUID, title String, artist String, label_release_id UUID, bpm Float32, musical_key String, loudness_lufs Float32, updated_at DateTime. ingestion_jobs (job_id UUID, ingestion_date Date, track_id UUID, gcs_uri String, gcs_generation Int64, status ENUM('queued','running','completed','failed','permanent_failure'), attempts UInt8, error_detail String, started_at DateTime, finished_at DateTime). label_releases (release_id UUID, title String, artist String, release_date Date, territory String). feature_extractions (id UUID, job_id UUID, track_id UUID, extractor String, parameters_json String, raw_output_json String, created_at DateTime). failed_extractions (track_id UUID, gcs_uri String, gcs_generation Int64, error_detail String, failed_at DateTime).
target_output.api_design: FastAPI trigger-api endpoints. POST /ingest (Pub/Sub push body: {message: {data: base64, attributes: {bucketId, objectId, eventType, objectGeneration}}}) validates HMAC token in Authorization header, extracts gcs_uri and gcs_generation, runs deduplication, enqueues to Redis, returns {status: 'enqueued'|'deduplicated'}. GET /health returns {status: 'ok', redis_connected: bool, queue_depth: int}. GET /metrics returns Prometheus text format (tracks_enqueued_total, tracks_deduplicated_total). Internal Cloud Run Job invocation via GCP Cloud Run Jobs API (not HTTP); job args: --date YYYY-MM-DD --batch-size 100. No external API; all endpoints are internal Cloud Run service.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GCP IAM: trigger-api service account with roles/run.invoker (to trigger Cloud Run Jobs), roles/redis.editor (Memorystore), roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor. ExtractionWorker service account with roles/storage.objectViewer on audio-assets bucket only, roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor for ClickHouse/Redis credentials. ClickHouse accessible only via Private Service Connect (no public IP). GCS bucket CMEK with Cloud KMS key in same project; satisfies label content security policy. Cloud Audit Logs: DATA_READ log for all GCS object reads by extraction-worker service account. Pub/Sub push HMAC token validated by trigger-api to prevent spoofed ingest triggers. TLS 1.3 on all Cloud Run and GCS API calls. Secrets rotated every 60 days via Secret Manager version rotation.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Build trigger on main branch push to Artifact Registry. Pipeline: ruff lint, pytest (90% coverage gate including Essentia mock tests and ClickHouse container integration tests), Docker build, push to Artifact Registry with SHA tag. Cloud Run service update (trigger-api): gcloud run deploy with --image and --service-account flags. Cloud Run Job update (extraction-worker): gcloud run jobs update --image. Environments: dev (single Cloud Run Job task, local Redis and ClickHouse containers in docker-compose), staging (5-task parallelism, staging ClickHouse cluster, real GCS bucket), production (50-task parallelism, 3-shard ClickHouse cluster, CMEK GCS bucket). Rollback: Cloud Run service revision rollback via gcloud run services update-traffic on failed health check. No stateful migration needed for ClickHouse (ReplacingMergeTree is additive).
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics from trigger-api /metrics endpoint: tracks_enqueued_total (counter), tracks_deduplicated_total (counter), tracks_extracted_total by extractor (counter), extraction_duration_seconds histogram by extractor (essentia_bpm, essentia_key, librosa_rms, librosa_spectral), clickhouse_insert_duration_seconds histogram. Grafana dashboards: daily ingestion throughput (tracks/hour), extraction success rate by extractor, p95 extraction latency per feature, ClickHouse insert batch size distribution, reconciliation missing_count trend over 30 days. Cloud Logging: structured JSON from each extraction task with fields track_id, gcs_uri, gcs_generation, job_id, task_index, features_extracted (list), duration_ms, error (if any). PagerDuty alerts: reconciliation missing rate >1% (critical), extraction failure rate >5% over 30 minutes (high), trigger-api error rate >2% over 10 minutes (medium).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $360/month: ClickHouse cluster 3x e2-standard-4 ~$280/month (sustained use discount applied); Memorystore Redis 7 m1-red-medium ~$40/month; Cloud Run Jobs at 5000 tracks x 2.4s x 2 vCPU = 24,000 vCPU-seconds/day x $0.000024/vCPU-second = ~$0.58/day = ~$17/month; GCS storage ~$15/month; Cloud Build and Pub/Sub negligible. Total ~$352/month under $500 budget. Scaling: Cloud Run Job parallelism can increase from 50 to 200 tasks to handle 20K tracks/day without ClickHouse cluster change; ClickHouse ReplacingMergeTree scales horizontally with additional shards.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): GCS Pub/Sub trigger, single-worker sequential extraction (BPM and key via Essentia only), ClickHouse acoustic_features insert, ingestion_jobs status tracking, Cloud Build CI. Phase 2 (4 weeks): 50-task parallel Cloud Run Job, generation-aware Redis deduplication (SHA256 key with gcs_generation), Librosa RMS and spectral centroid extraction, catalog_entries upsert, Cloud Logging structured output. Phase 3 (3 weeks): Daily reconciliation job at 5am UTC with Cloud Run Job completion check, failed_extractions table, Grafana dashboards, PagerDuty integration, staging environment with real GCS bucket.
target_output.development_guidelines: ruff + black enforced in Cloud Build CI with zero-tolerance failures. pytest at 90% coverage gate; Essentia mocked via pytest-mock in unit tests; ClickHouse tested against real clickhouse/clickhouse-server Docker container in Cloud Build. ExtractionWorker implements BaseExtractor abstract class with extract(gcs_uri: str, gcs_generation: int) -> FeatureDict interface. All ClickHouse inserts use clickhouse-connect insert_dataframe with batch size 1000; never row-by-row inserts. dedup_key = SHA256(gcs_uri + ':' + str(gcs_generation)) — this exact formula is a contract; changing it without a Redis cache flush causes duplicate extractions during TTL window. ADR required for any change to ClickHouse table engine or partition key.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Essentia extraction on corrupted or unusually encoded audio files raises unhandled exception crashing the batch task. Mitigation: wrap each track extraction in try/except, log to ingestion_jobs, continue processing remaining tracks in batch. Risk: ClickHouse ReplacingMergeTree deduplication is eventual (merge happens asynchronously); a query immediately after insert may see duplicate rows. Mitigation: queries use FINAL modifier (SELECT ... FROM acoustic_features FINAL WHERE ...) which forces synchronous dedup at query time. Tradeoff: 90% pytest coverage gate requires mocking Essentia C++ bindings; adds test infrastructure complexity but essential for CI reliability. Tradeoff: dedup TTL of 48 hours means re-uploads within 48 hours of same generation are skipped; label ops team must be aware that corrected re-uploads need to increment GCS generation (overwrite file) not just re-trigger notification.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Dedup key confirmed as SHA256(gcs_uri+':'+gcs_generation) — resolves ISS-002. Reconciliation schedule confirmed as 5am UTC with Cloud Run Job completion check — resolves ISS-003. pyloudnorm selected for LUFS measurement (ITU-R BS.1770) over Essentia LoudnessEBUR128 due to more predictable behavior on short clips. ClickHouse FINAL modifier accepted as query overhead trade-off given analytics access patterns.

metadata.notes: Round 3 of record label acoustic feature extraction; resolves ISS-002 generation-aware dedup key and ISS-003 reconciliation timing offset.

project_goal.value: Migrate legacy on-premise music distribution system to a cloud-native catalog API exposing ISRCs, metadata, and audio file delivery to streaming platform partners
project_goal.rationale: Core business need to replace aging on-premise infrastructure with scalable cloud-native endpoints

target_users.value: Streaming platform partners and internal distribution team engineers
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are B2B partners accessing catalog data and audio deliveries

project_class.rationale: No frontend required; pure backend API service exposing catalog data to external partners

capabilities.rationale: Backend processing with partner-facing API, data migration, and legacy system integration

complexity_level.rationale: Dual-write migration strategy, partner auth, ISRC management, and phased cutover add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Proprietary catalog data under label agreements, GDPR on artist data, and partner SLAs create high risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Music catalog data governed by label distribution agreements; artist PII subject to GDPR

external_exposure.rationale: API endpoints exposed exclusively to authenticated streaming platform partners via OAuth2

access_model.value: OAuth2 client credentials for machine-to-machine partner access; internal staff use Auth0 management portal with admin scope
access_model.rationale: Partners require automated M2M access; staff need human-facing management UI

feature_scope.value: ISRC lookup and registration, release and track metadata CRUD, signed S3 URL generation for audio delivery, rights holder management, metadata versioning, dual-write migration adapter
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope required to replace all legacy system capabilities

mvp_scope.value: ISRC catalog query, release and track metadata read endpoints, signed audio delivery URLs, and dual-write adapter for phased cutover
mvp_scope.rationale: Partners need read access and audio delivery first; write endpoints follow in phase 2

security_baseline.value: OAuth2 client credentials with 15-minute JWTs, mTLS for partner connections, TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest, audit logging of all catalog access
security_baseline.rationale: Partner-facing API with confidential catalog data requires strong transport and access controls

frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI; partners consume API endpoints directly

backend_stack.rationale: Go provides low-latency throughput for high-volume catalog queries and S3 pre-signed URL generation

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for relational catalog data; Redis 7 for ISRC lookup cache and rate limiting
data_platform.rationale: Relational model fits hierarchical release/track/rights structure; Redis caches hot ISRC queries

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 and eu-west-1 for data residency
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with multi-region support for partner latency and GDPR data residency

privacy_retention_policy.value: Catalog metadata retained indefinitely; artist PII retained for contract duration plus 7 years; access logs retained 2 years per GDPR Article 17
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Distribution agreements and GDPR retention rules govern catalog and personal data lifecycle

future_scope.value: Write endpoints for partner metadata submissions, automated royalty reporting feeds, and GraphQL API layer for flexible catalog queries
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after successful read-only migration cutover

constraints.value: Must maintain 100% uptime for existing partner integrations during migration; legacy system decommission target is 6 months post-launch
constraints.rationale: Partner SLAs prohibit downtime; legacy decommission has cost and licensing drivers

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with distributed tracing, structured JSON logs, per-partner request metrics, PagerDuty for SLA breach alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Partner SLAs require per-partner visibility and rapid incident response

execution_preference.value: Phased migration with traffic shadowing and incremental partner onboarding
execution_preference.rationale: Risk-averse cutover strategy required given partner SLA obligations

compliance_context.value: GDPR for artist personal data; label distribution agreement confidentiality obligations; partner data processing agreements required under GDPR Article 28
compliance_context.rationale: GDPR applies to artist PII; commercial agreements govern catalog data handling

project.goal: Replace legacy on-premise music catalog system with cloud-native API for partner consumption
project.target_users: Streaming platform partners and internal catalog managers

backend.framework: Gin with Auth0 JWT middleware and mTLS enforcement

security.auth: Auth0 OAuth2 client credentials M2M; mTLS at ALB listener
security.transport: TLS 1.3; mTLS enforced per partner endpoint
security.audit: audit_events append-only table for all catalog reads and writes

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security by label_id
data.cache: Redis 7 for ISRC hot-path cache TTL 5min and rate limit counters

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: go test, golangci-lint, govulncheck, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy

constraints.uptime: zero-downtime migration; dual-write throughout cutover period

reasoner_reviews.product: The dual-write adapter strategy is the correct approach for zero-downtime migration. Partners must be onboarded incrementally to the new API while the legacy system continues serving, with explicit per-partner cutover gates gated on smoke test passage and drift confirmation below 0.01% for 14 consecutive days.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with Gin is well-suited for high-throughput ISRC lookups and S3 pre-signed URL generation. Redis ISRC cache TTL must align with metadata update frequency — 5 minutes with explicit cache invalidation on write balances freshness and cache efficiency. eu-west-1 standby cluster adds operational overhead; confirm whether it is required day one or can be a phase 2 addition to reduce initial migration scope.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS enforcement at the ALB listener is non-negotiable for partner-facing endpoints handling confidential catalog data. OAuth2 client credentials must use 15-minute JWT TTL. Row-level security in PostgreSQL by label_id is a critical control preventing cross-label data leakage in the event of a compromised partner credential.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Six-month decommission is aggressive for a catalog of this size. The dual-write reconciliation job must detect drift above 0.1% and halt cutover approval automatically. Budget additional engineer bandwidth during the high-risk cutover phase.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The current plan defers eu-west-1 to phase 2, but GDPR data residency for EU partner traffic must be addressed in architecture design now even if deployment is phased. Confirm that us-east-1 adequately serves all launch partners before deferring the EU region.

backend.service_design: Catalog service with domain modules: releases, tracks, isrc_registry, audio_delivery, rights_holders, migration_adapter. Each module owns its repository layer and exposes handler functions to the Gin router with no cross-module direct calls.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful v1 under /v1/catalog. All responses include X-Request-ID and X-Catalog-Version headers. Pagination via cursor-based keyset on release_id. Audio delivery URLs generated with 15-minute expiry using aws-sdk-go-v2 S3 pre-signer.
backend.background_jobs: Dual-write reconciliation job runs every 15 minutes comparing MD5 checksums of migrated releases between legacy and new PostgreSQL. Alerts Datadog metric dual_write_drift_pct; triggers PagerDuty if drift exceeds 0.1%.
backend.failure_handling: Circuit breaker on legacy adapter with fallback to read-only mode when legacy is unreachable. Redis cache unavailability degrades gracefully with direct PostgreSQL ISRC lookup at 3x latency cost, logged as cache_miss event.

security.auth_design: Auth0 machine-to-machine application per partner. JWTs validated at Gin middleware with claim extraction for partner_id and allowed_label_ids. Token introspection cache in Redis for 5-minute reuse.
security.authorization_model: Partner access scoped to allowed_label_ids JWT claim enforced by PostgreSQL RLS policy: CREATE POLICY label_isolation ON tracks USING (label_id = ANY(current_setting('app.allowed_label_ids')::uuid[])). Internal staff use Auth0 management app with admin scope.
security.secrets_management: Auth0 client secrets and DB credentials in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation. S3 pre-signing uses short-lived IAM role assumed per request via STS AssumeRole with condition restricting bucket access to catalog prefix.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_events table records partner_id, resource_type, resource_id, action, timestamp for every catalog read and write. Table has INSERT-only grants for API role; no UPDATE or DELETE. Daily S3 backup of audit_events.

data.entities: releases (id UUID PK, label_id UUID FK, title TEXT, upc CHAR(13), release_date DATE, status ENUM draft/published/withdrawn). tracks (id UUID PK, release_id FK, isrc CHAR(12), title TEXT, duration_ms INT, audio_s3_key TEXT, metadata_version INT). isrc_codes (isrc CHAR(12) PK, track_id FK, registered_at, registrant_label_id). rights_holders (id UUID PK, name TEXT, ipi_number CHAR(11), label_id FK, share_pct NUMERIC). metadata_versions (id UUID PK, track_id FK, version_num INT, diff_json JSONB, changed_by TEXT, changed_at). distribution_partners (id UUID PK, name TEXT, auth0_client_id TEXT, allowed_label_ids UUID[]). audit_events (id UUID PK, partner_id UUID, action TEXT, resource_type TEXT, resource_id TEXT, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.schema_notes: isrc_codes uses CHAR(12) with CHECK constraint enforcing ISO 3901 format. metadata_versions stores diffs as JSONB for efficient changelog queries. Partial index on tracks(isrc) for fast ISRC lookups on cache miss. RLS policies applied to releases, tracks, and isrc_codes tables.
data.retention_and_deletion: Catalog metadata retained indefinitely. Artist PII in rights_holders purged via scheduled job 7 years after contract end. audit_events retained 2 years then archived to S3 Glacier via lifecycle rule.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate service in us-east-1a and us-east-1c behind ALB with mTLS listener. API container: 2-8 tasks auto-scaling on CPU 65% target and ALB RequestCountPerTarget. Reconciliation worker as ECS scheduled task every 15 minutes.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions pipeline: go test ./..., golangci-lint with errcheck/staticcheck/gosec, govulncheck, docker build, ECR push, integration tests against testcontainers PostgreSQL, ECS rolling update with deployment circuit breaker enabled.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS deployment circuit breaker rolls back on failed health checks within 5 minutes. Dual-write ensures legacy system is always current as fallback target during entire migration window.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM ddtrace/go auto-instrumentation. Custom metrics: isrc_cache_hit_rate (alert <85%), partner_request_latency_p99_ms (alert >300ms), dual_write_drift_pct (alert >0.1%), audio_url_generation_latency_ms, audit_event_write_failure_count.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a cloud-native catalog API to replace a legacy on-premise music distribution system. Key decisions: Go with Gin for low-latency ISRC lookups and S3 pre-signed URL generation, dual-write adapter for zero-downtime migration with automated reconciliation, mTLS enforced at ALB for partner-facing endpoints, PostgreSQL row-level security by label_id to prevent cross-label data leakage during the migration window.
target_output.title: Cloud-Native Music Catalog API — Legacy Migration Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22/Gin REST API service on AWS ECS Fargate replacing a legacy on-premise music distribution system. The service exposes ISRC registry lookup, release and track metadata, and signed S3 audio delivery URLs to authenticated streaming platform partners via OAuth2 M2M client credentials and mTLS. A dual-write adapter maintains consistency with the legacy system throughout the phased cutover, with automated 15-minute reconciliation drift checks ensuring zero data loss before decommission.
target_output.architecture_overview: Partner-facing REST API on ECS Fargate behind an ALB with mTLS listener policies enforcing client certificate validation for all partner requests. The Go Gin service connects to PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security policies scoped by label_id, and Redis 7 for ISRC hot-path caching with 5-minute TTL. During the migration window, a dual-write adapter module simultaneously writes catalog updates to the legacy on-premise SOAP endpoint and the new PostgreSQL catalog. A background reconciliation worker runs every 15 minutes comparing MD5 checksums between both systems and alerts on drift above 0.1%. Auth0 machine-to-machine applications issue 15-minute JWTs with partner_id and allowed_label_ids claims. Audio file delivery uses per-request S3 pre-signed URLs generated via STS-assumed IAM role, never exposing raw S3 credentials to partners.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22 with Gin, golang-jwt for JWT validation, aws-sdk-go-v2 for S3 pre-signing and Secrets Manager, pgx/v5 for PostgreSQL. Cache: Redis 7 via go-redis for ISRC cache and rate limit counters. Auth: Auth0 M2M OAuth2 client credentials. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate us-east-1, ALB with mTLS certificate validation, ECR, S3 with SSE-AES256 for audio masters, Secrets Manager for credential rotation. Observability: Datadog APM ddtrace/go, zerolog structured JSON logs.
target_output.functional_feature_map: ISRC Registry: GET by ISRC returning track and release metadata; bulk ISRC validation for partner ingestion batches up to 500 codes. Release Catalog: paginated release listing by label_id using keyset cursor on release_id; release detail with embedded track array. Track Metadata: track detail with current metadata and versioned diff log via metadata_versions JSONB. Audio Delivery: per-request signed S3 URLs with 15-minute expiry, access audited to audit_events. Rights Holders: listing and detail endpoints scoped by label_id for royalty attribution. Migration Adapter: dual-write to legacy system with drift reconciliation job and cutover approval gating.
target_output.system_components: ALB with mTLS listener (partner certificate validation via ACM private CA), ECS Fargate cluster (Gin API containers 2-8 tasks), ECS scheduled task (reconciliation worker every 15 minutes), PostgreSQL 16 primary with read replica and RLS policies, Redis 7 cluster (ISRC cache TTL 5min, rate limit counters), S3 bucket (audio master files SSE-AES256), Auth0 tenant (M2M app per partner), Secrets Manager (Auth0 client secrets 90-day rotation, DB credentials), Legacy adapter connector (SOAP client to on-premise system), Datadog agent sidecars on each Fargate task.
target_output.workflows: Partner Audio Delivery Workflow: Partner POSTs to Auth0 /oauth/token with client_id and client_secret, receives JWT with allowed_label_ids claim (15-min TTL). Partner calls GET /v1/catalog/tracks/:isrc/audio-url with Bearer JWT and mTLS client cert. Gin middleware validates JWT, extracts allowed_label_ids, sets PostgreSQL session variable app.allowed_label_ids, queries tracks table (RLS filters by label), generates S3 pre-signed URL via STS-assumed IAM role, writes to audit_events, returns URL. Partner downloads audio directly from S3. Dual-Write Migration Workflow: Internal catalog manager PATCHes track metadata via admin endpoint. Gin handler writes to PostgreSQL, migration_adapter module forwards identical update to legacy SOAP endpoint synchronously. Reconciliation worker ECS task fetches checksum manifests from both systems, computes drift_pct metric, emits to Datadog; if drift_pct > 0.1%, PagerDuty P1 alert fires and cutover approval flag is locked until resolved.
target_output.data_model: releases (id UUID PK, label_id UUID FK, title TEXT, upc CHAR(13), release_date DATE, status ENUM draft/published/withdrawn, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). tracks (id UUID PK, release_id UUID FK, isrc CHAR(12) CHECK iso_3901, title TEXT, duration_ms INT, audio_s3_key TEXT, metadata_version INT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). isrc_codes (isrc CHAR(12) PK, track_id UUID FK, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, registrant_label_id UUID FK). rights_holders (id UUID PK, name TEXT, ipi_number CHAR(11), label_id UUID FK, share_pct NUMERIC(5,2)). metadata_versions (id UUID PK, track_id UUID FK, version_num INT, diff_json JSONB, changed_by TEXT, changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). distribution_partners (id UUID PK, name TEXT, auth0_client_id TEXT UNIQUE, allowed_label_ids UUID[], created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). audit_events (id UUID PK, partner_id UUID, action TEXT, resource_type TEXT, resource_id TEXT, ip TEXT, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ) -- INSERT-only grants.
target_output.api_design: GET /v1/catalog/isrc/:isrc — ISRC lookup with track and release metadata. GET /v1/catalog/releases?label_id=&after_cursor=&limit= — keyset-paginated release listing. GET /v1/catalog/releases/:id — release detail with embedded tracks. GET /v1/catalog/tracks/:id/audio-url — signed S3 URL generation, 15-min expiry, audited. POST /v1/catalog/isrc/validate-bulk — validate up to 500 ISRCs, returns per-ISRC status. GET /v1/catalog/rights-holders?label_id= — rights holder listing. GET /v1/internal/reconciliation/status — drift report for ops (internal scope only). All partner endpoints require Authorization: Bearer JWT plus valid mTLS client certificate.
target_output.security_and_compliance: mTLS enforced at ALB listener using ACM private CA; partner client certificates issued per partner and rotated annually with 30-day overlap. OAuth2 client credentials with 15-minute JWT TTL; claims include partner_id and allowed_label_ids. PostgreSQL RLS policy: CREATE POLICY label_isolation ON tracks USING (label_id = ANY(current_setting('app.allowed_label_ids')::uuid[])). Auth0 client secrets and DB credentials in Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation Lambda trigger. S3 audio bucket denies direct access; all downloads via STS-assumed IAM role pre-signed URLs. GDPR compliance: rights_holders PII purged 7 years post contract-end via scheduled cleanup job. audit_events INSERT-only (no UPDATE/DELETE grants to API role). Partner data processing agreements in place per GDPR Article 28.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: go test ./..., golangci-lint (errcheck, staticcheck, gosec), govulncheck, docker build, ECR push, integration tests via testcontainers-go against real PostgreSQL with RLS enabled, ECS rolling update with deployment circuit breaker. Environments: dev (single AZ, shared RDS dev), staging (multi-AZ, production-equivalent RLS policies and Auth0 test M2M apps), production (us-east-1 multi-AZ, 2-8 Fargate tasks auto-scaling CPU 65% target). DB migrations via golang-migrate as ECS one-off task pre-deployment. Secrets rotation: Secrets Manager Lambda rotates Auth0 client secrets and DB passwords every 90 days. Legacy decommission gate: drift_pct < 0.01% for 14 consecutive days confirmed before partner cutover sign-off.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM ddtrace/go auto-instrumentation on all Gin handlers with partner_id and label_id injected into trace tags. Custom metrics: isrc_cache_hit_rate (alert if below 85% for 10 min), partner_request_latency_p99_ms (alert >300ms), dual_write_drift_pct (alert >0.1%), audio_url_generation_latency_ms (p99), audit_event_write_failure_count. Zerolog structured JSON logs include partner_id, label_id, isrc, trace_id on every request. PagerDuty: P1 on drift breach and auth failure rate >5% per partner over 10 minutes.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate reserved baseline 2 tasks (~$130/month) with burst to 8 tasks during peak partner batch queries. Redis cache absorbs ~90% of ISRC reads, reducing PostgreSQL load. S3 Intelligent-Tiering for audio masters older than 90 days. Estimated $700-1000/month at current catalog size excluding inter-region data transfer fees. eu-west-1 standby adds ~$300/month when activated in phase 2.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Go API with ISRC lookup, release and track read endpoints, signed audio delivery URLs, Auth0 M2M per partner, mTLS at ALB, PostgreSQL with RLS, dual-write adapter for 3 pilot label catalogs, Datadog APM and reconciliation alerting. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Write endpoints for metadata submissions, full catalog migration for all labels, bulk ISRC validation, eu-west-1 standby cluster activation, GDPR PII purge job. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Legacy decommission gate evaluation, automated drift sign-off workflow, rights holder management endpoints, metadata versioning diff API, GraphQL evaluation.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go modules with go.sum committed. golangci-lint with errcheck, staticcheck, gosec enabled in CI gate. Table-driven tests for ISRC validation, RLS policy assertions, and JWT claim parsing. Integration tests use testcontainers-go with real PostgreSQL including RLS enabled. No raw SQL strings outside repository layer; use pgx named queries. OpenAPI 3.1 spec generated via swaggo annotations; spec published to partner developer portal on each production release.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Dual-write adapter adds write latency during migration window. Mitigation: async legacy write with reconciliation; legacy write failures logged but do not block new system response. Risk: mTLS certificate rotation causes partner outage if not coordinated. Mitigation: 30-day certificate overlap with automated expiry alerts at 60 and 30 days. Risk: PostgreSQL RLS policy performance on high-volume ISRC queries. Mitigation: Redis cache absorbs >90% of ISRC reads; RLS only evaluated on cache miss. Tradeoff: keyset pagination over offset avoids count query overhead on million-row catalog at cost of no random page access.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: mTLS selected over API key auth to meet confidential catalog data security requirements. Keyset pagination chosen over offset due to catalog scale. eu-west-1 deferred to phase 2 to reduce initial migration scope. Redis ISRC TTL set to 5 minutes with explicit write-through invalidation. GDPR Article 28 DPAs confirmed required before partner onboarding.

metadata.notes: Cloud-native catalog API migration from legacy on-premise music distribution with dual-write strategy, mTLS partner auth, and PostgreSQL RLS by label.

project_goal.value: Replace a legacy rules-based music recommendation engine with an LLM-powered assistant that generates personalized playlist rationales and mood-based curation for a streaming platform
project_goal.rationale: Legacy engine produces generic recommendations; LLM-powered assistant enables natural language mood input and narrative playlist explanations

target_users.value: Streaming platform subscribers who want mood-driven, explainable playlist curation
target_users.rationale: End users interact with the AI assistant via the streaming app; platform engineers consume the recommendation API

project_class.rationale: LLM-powered recommendation engine with real-time inference, listener personalization, and retraining pipeline defines this as an AI system

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, analytics, realtime
capabilities.rationale: Full-stack AI system with Next.js UI, FastAPI inference backend, PostgreSQL listener data, Cognito auth, OpenAI LLM, Kinesis analytics pipeline, and real-time WebSocket events

complexity_level.rationale: High concurrent listener base, real-time inference latency requirements, engagement signal pipeline, and prompt retraining pipeline define high-scale complexity

risk_level.rationale: Personal listening history and preference data used for personalization; GDPR applies; LLM output quality risk managed via fallback to catalog-based ranking

data_sensitivity.rationale: Listener profiles, mood inputs, and listening history constitute personal data under GDPR

external_exposure.rationale: All recommendation endpoints require Cognito JWT; no unauthenticated access

access_model.value: Cognito user pools with OAuth2 PKCE for streaming app; service-to-service JWT for internal recommendation API calls
access_model.rationale: Streaming app requires PKCE for browser security; internal services use M2M JWT

feature_scope.value: Mood-input playlist generation with LLM narrative rationale, listener preference profile management, real-time engagement signal ingestion, prompt template versioning, A/B testing framework for model variants, GDPR data export and deletion
feature_scope.rationale: Core features required to replace legacy engine and add explainability capability

mvp_scope.value: Mood-input playlist generation with LLM rationale, listener profile storage, Cognito auth, and basic engagement signal logging
mvp_scope.rationale: Core AI capability first; A/B testing and advanced analytics follow in phase 2

security_baseline.value: Cognito JWT for all endpoints, TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest, PII minimization in LLM prompts, prompt injection detection
security_baseline.rationale: Personal data requires encryption and access controls; LLM inputs must sanitize listener PII before sending to external model API

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; SSR for fast initial page load; React ecosystem for component reuse with existing streaming app

backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI with async I/O suits LLM inference latency; Python ecosystem for ML tooling and OpenAI SDK

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for listener profiles and curation_sessions; Redis 7 for recommendation caching and session state
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for listener profile relationships; Redis caches recent recommendations to reduce LLM calls

hosting_target.value: AWS EKS in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Kubernetes provides flexible scaling for inference workloads and retraining jobs

privacy_retention_policy.value: Listener profiles and engagement history retained while account is active plus 2 years; LLM prompt logs retained 30 days; deleted on GDPR erasure request within 30 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR Articles 17 and 20 govern listener data retention and portability

future_scope.value: Fine-tuned open-source model to reduce OpenAI dependency, social playlist sharing with collaborative LLM curation, integration with label promotional campaigns
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 and 3 roadmap items contingent on LLM quality baseline established in phase 1

constraints.value: LLM inference p50 latency must be below 2 seconds; OpenAI API spend capped at $8000/month
constraints.rationale: User experience requires sub-2s playlist generation; LLM cost must be contained via caching and prompt optimization

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM, LLM call latency and token metrics, engagement funnel events in Kinesis, PagerDuty for inference latency SLA breaches
observability_baseline.rationale: LLM inference requires dedicated latency monitoring; engagement signals drive A/B test evaluation

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with model quality evaluation gates between phases
execution_preference.rationale: LLM quality must meet relevance score threshold before scaling to full user base

compliance_context.value: GDPR for listener personal data; PII must be stripped from LLM prompts before sending to OpenAI; data processing agreement with OpenAI in place
compliance_context.rationale: Listener names, emails, and listening history must not appear in LLM prompt payloads sent to external APIs

llm_integration.value: OpenAI GPT-4o for playlist rationale generation and mood-to-genre mapping; prompt templates versioned in prompt_registry table; Redis caches identical mood+genre responses for 30 minutes to reduce API calls
llm_integration.rationale: GPT-4o balances quality and latency; prompt versioning enables A/B testing; Redis caching controls OpenAI spend

project.goal: LLM-powered mood-based playlist curation replacing legacy rules engine
project.target_users: Streaming platform subscribers

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for mood input session state
frontend.realtime: WebSocket connection for live playlist update events

backend.framework: FastAPI with async handlers and Pydantic v2
backend.llm: OpenAI SDK with retry and timeout middleware

security.auth: Cognito user pools with PKCE
security.pii_controls: listener name and email stripped from all LLM prompt payloads

data.cache: Redis 7 for recommendation cache TTL 30min and session state

constraints.llm_spend: OpenAI capped at $8000/month via Redis caching and prompt token optimization

open_questions.fine_tuning: evaluate open-source model fine-tuning in phase 2 to reduce OpenAI dependency

reasoner_reviews.product: The LLM rationale feature directly addresses the core user complaint about opaque recommendations from the legacy engine. Mood-to-playlist in under 2 seconds will define the user experience bar. Ensure the fallback to catalog-based ranking is invisible to users — LLM failures must silently degrade to high-quality rule-based results, not error states.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI async is the right choice for LLM inference with OpenAI SDK since calls are I/O-bound. Redis caching of identical mood+genre prompts will be critical to staying within the $8000/month OpenAI cap. Prompt template versioning in the database enables A/B testing without deploys. EKS adds operational overhead over Fargate; confirm whether the retraining pipeline workloads justify Kubernetes or if a simpler Fargate setup suffices for phase 1.
reasoner_reviews.security: PII stripping from LLM prompts is the most critical control here — listener names, emails, and specific track history must never appear in payloads sent to OpenAI. Prompt injection from mood input fields is a real attack surface; implement a sanitization layer that rejects prompts containing instructions or jailbreak patterns before sending to GPT-4o.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: OpenAI spend at $8000/month is achievable if Redis cache hit rate stays above 60% on repeated mood queries. Monitor token counts per request in Datadog and set hard limits in the OpenAI SDK client. LLM p50 latency under 2 seconds requires stream=True on OpenAI calls and progressive playlist rendering on the frontend.

backend.service_design: Recommendation service with modules: curation_engine (mood parsing, LLM dispatch, track selection), listener_profiles (preferences, history), prompt_registry (versioned templates, A/B config), engagement_sink (Kinesis event writer), fallback_ranker (rule-based fallback when LLM unavailable).
backend.llm_integration_design: OpenAI GPT-4o called via openai-python SDK with async client. Prompt template fetched from prompt_registry by version_id. Listener mood input sanitized through PII-stripping middleware before template injection. Redis checks for cache hit on sha256(prompt_hash+genre_context) before LLM call. stream=True for progressive token delivery. Circuit breaker activates fallback_ranker on 3 consecutive OpenAI timeouts.
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/recommend/playlist — mood input, returns playlist with track list and LLM narrative. GET /v1/listener/profile — preference summary. POST /v1/engage/event — engagement signal ingest. GET /v1/prompts/:version_id — prompt template inspection for A/B monitoring.
backend.failure_handling: Circuit breaker on OpenAI client: 3 failures in 30 seconds activates fallback_ranker. Fallback returns catalog-based ranked playlist without LLM narrative, indistinguishable to frontend. Redis TTL expiry forces LLM refresh; stale-while-revalidate pattern for low-latency UX.

frontend.pages_and_flows: MoodInput page: natural language text field with mood suggestions, submit triggers POST /v1/recommend/playlist with streaming response. PlaylistView: progressive track list render as LLM tokens arrive via SSE, narrative explanation below playlist. ListenerProfile: preference summary with override sliders for genre weight.
frontend.state_management: React Query manages server state for playlist and profile fetches with stale-while-revalidate. Zustand holds ephemeral mood session state (current mood input, streaming tokens, playback position).
frontend.performance_notes: Next.js 15 streaming SSR for MoodInput page initial load under 1 second. OpenAI response streamed via SSE from FastAPI to Next.js backend route to browser — no full response buffering. React Suspense boundaries prevent blank states during LLM stream.
frontend.frontend_risks: LLM stream drops on mobile network interruption — implement SSE reconnect with last-event-id to resume partial playlist without re-triggering LLM call.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool with OAuth2 PKCE for streaming app. Cognito JWT validated at FastAPI middleware via JWKS endpoint caching. Service-to-service calls use Cognito M2M client credentials.
security.privacy_controls: PII stripping middleware intercepts mood input before LLM dispatch: regex and NLP patterns remove email addresses, personal names, and explicit track identifiers from prompt payload. LLM prompt logs stored for 30 days then purged. GDPR erasure request triggers deletion of listener_profiles, curation_sessions, recommendation_events, and Kinesis-derived S3 analytics within 30 days.
security.abuse_prevention: Prompt injection detection: keyword blocklist and LLM-as-judge classifier evaluates mood inputs for jailbreak patterns before forwarding to GPT-4o. Rate limit: 10 playlist requests per listener per minute via Redis token bucket.
security.security_risks: Risk of PII leakage to OpenAI via mood inputs referencing other users by name. Mitigation: named entity recognition pass on all inputs before LLM dispatch.

data.entities: listener_profiles (id UUID PK, cognito_sub UNIQUE, genre_weights JSONB, mood_history TEXT[], created_at). playlists (id UUID PK, listener_id FK, mood_input TEXT, llm_narrative TEXT, track_ids UUID[], prompt_version_id FK, created_at). tracks (id UUID PK, catalog_id, title, artist, genre, bpm, energy_score, embedding VECTOR(768)). mood_contexts (id UUID PK, label TEXT, genre_affinities JSONB, seed_track_ids UUID[]). recommendation_events (id UUID PK, listener_id FK, playlist_id FK, action ENUM play/skip/save, track_id FK, event_at). prompt_registry (id UUID PK, version TEXT UNIQUE, template TEXT, model TEXT, token_budget INT, active BOOL). curation_sessions (id UUID PK, listener_id FK, mood_input TEXT, llm_response_cached BOOL, latency_ms INT, created_at).
data.analytics_events: Engagement events (play, skip, save, share) written to Kinesis Firehose partitioned by listener_id date. Delivered to S3 and queried via Athena for A/B test evaluation of prompt versions.
data.consistency_notes: recommendation_events written async to Kinesis; curation_sessions written sync to PostgreSQL for latency tracking. Eventual consistency acceptable for engagement analytics; strong consistency required for GDPR erasure verification.

devops.deployment_topology: EKS cluster with node groups: inference-workers (c6g.xlarge, 4-20 nodes auto-scaling), retraining-jobs (m6i.2xlarge spot instances). FastAPI deployment as EKS Deployment with HPA on p95 inference latency. Next.js on Vercel with API routes proxying to EKS inference service.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest with coverage >85%, ruff linter, mypy type checks, docker build, ECR push, Helm chart upgrade to EKS with canary rollout. Prompt template changes deployed via migration without app redeploy.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on FastAPI pods. Custom metrics: llm_call_latency_p50_ms (alert >2000ms), openai_tokens_per_request (alert >2000 tokens), recommendation_cache_hit_rate, fallback_ranker_activation_count, gdpr_erasure_pending_count.
devops.cost_controls: Redis cache TTL 30 minutes on identical mood+genre hash. OpenAI SDK hard token limit 1500 per request. EKS inference nodes scale to zero overnight via Karpenter. Spot instances for retraining jobs.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an LLM-powered music recommendation system replacing a legacy rules engine. Key decisions: FastAPI async for OpenAI I/O-bound inference, Redis prompt hash caching to stay within $8000/month OpenAI spend cap, PII-stripping middleware as the primary GDPR control before LLM dispatch, SSE streaming from FastAPI through Next.js to browser for sub-2s perceived latency, PostgreSQL prompt_registry for versioned template A/B testing without deploys.
target_output.title: LLM-Powered Mood-Based Music Recommendation System
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale AI system replacing a legacy rules-based music recommendation engine with an LLM-powered assistant on AWS EKS. Listeners describe their mood in natural language; the FastAPI inference service dispatches sanitized prompts to GPT-4o, which selects tracks from the catalog and generates a narrative playlist rationale. Redis caches identical mood+genre prompt hashes for 30 minutes to stay within the $8000/month OpenAI budget. GDPR compliance enforced through PII-stripping middleware, 30-day prompt log retention, and automated erasure on account deletion.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 15 streaming frontend communicates with a FastAPI inference service on EKS via SSE for progressive playlist rendering. The inference service dispatches sanitized mood prompts to GPT-4o via OpenAI SDK (stream=True), selecting tracks from the PostgreSQL catalog using embedding similarity and returning LLM-generated narrative rationale. Redis 7 caches prompt responses keyed on sha256(mood_hash+genre_context) with 30-minute TTL. A circuit breaker activates a catalog-based fallback_ranker on 3 consecutive OpenAI timeouts, producing a seamless fallback invisible to listeners. Engagement events (play, skip, save) stream to Kinesis Firehose, landing in S3 for Athena-based A/B evaluation of prompt versions stored in the prompt_registry PostgreSQL table. Cognito PKCE handles listener authentication; PII stripping middleware intercepts mood inputs before LLM dispatch to enforce GDPR controls on external API calls.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 15 TypeScript, React Query, Zustand, SSE for streaming. Backend: Python 3.12 FastAPI async, openai-python SDK, Pydantic v2, pgvector for embedding similarity. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension for track embeddings. Cache: Redis 7 for prompt hash cache and token-bucket rate limiting. Auth: Cognito user pools with PKCE. ML: GPT-4o via OpenAI API; fallback rule-based ranker. Infrastructure: EKS us-east-1, Karpenter for node autoscaling, ECR, Kinesis Firehose, S3, Athena. Observability: Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Mood Playlist Generation: listener submits natural language mood input; system strips PII, checks Redis cache, dispatches to GPT-4o with versioned prompt template, returns track list with narrative rationale via SSE stream. Listener Profile Management: stores genre_weights, mood_history, and explicit preference overrides; updated by engagement signals. Engagement Signal Pipeline: play, skip, save events written to Kinesis Firehose, partitioned to S3, evaluated via Athena for prompt version A/B testing. Prompt Registry: versioned templates stored in PostgreSQL, activated via feature flag without app redeploy. GDPR Controls: data export endpoint, erasure endpoint deleting all listener data within 30 days. Fallback Ranker: catalog-based energy/genre ranking activates silently on LLM unavailability.
target_output.system_components: EKS inference-worker node group (c6g.xlarge 4-20 nodes, HPA on p95 latency), FastAPI inference pods, Next.js on Vercel (API routes proxy to EKS), PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector (listener profiles, playlists, prompt_registry, tracks with embeddings), Redis 7 (prompt hash cache TTL 30min, rate limit counters), Cognito user pool (PKCE for streaming app), Kinesis Firehose (engagement events to S3), S3 + Athena (A/B evaluation queries), OpenAI API (GPT-4o), Karpenter (node autoscaling with overnight scale-to-zero), Datadog agents.
target_output.workflows: Mood Playlist Generation Workflow: Listener types mood input ('mellow Sunday morning with rain') into Next.js MoodInput component. Frontend POSTs to FastAPI POST /v1/recommend/playlist with Cognito JWT. FastAPI middleware validates JWT, extracts listener_id, runs PII-stripping NER pass on mood input. Computes sha256(stripped_mood+genre_context), checks Redis cache. On cache miss, fetches active prompt template from prompt_registry, injects mood and top-5 listener genre_weights, dispatches to GPT-4o stream=True with 1500-token budget. Tokens streamed back via SSE to Next.js; frontend renders tracks progressively as they arrive. curation_sessions record written to PostgreSQL with latency_ms. Engagement Signal Workflow: Listener skips track in playlist. Frontend fires POST /v1/engage/event with track_id and action='skip'. FastAPI writes engagement event to Kinesis Firehose. Nightly Glue job aggregates skip rates by prompt_version_id into Athena table. A/B test dashboard in Datadog queries Athena for prompt version relevance scores, enabling ML team to promote winning template in prompt_registry.
target_output.data_model: listener_profiles (id UUID PK, cognito_sub TEXT UNIQUE, genre_weights JSONB, mood_history TEXT[], created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). playlists (id UUID PK, listener_id UUID FK, mood_input TEXT, llm_narrative TEXT, track_ids UUID[], prompt_version_id UUID FK, cache_hit BOOL, latency_ms INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). tracks (id UUID PK, title TEXT, artist TEXT, genre TEXT, bpm SMALLINT, energy_score FLOAT, embedding VECTOR(768), catalog_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). mood_contexts (id UUID PK, label TEXT, genre_affinities JSONB, seed_track_ids UUID[]). recommendation_events (id UUID PK, listener_id UUID FK, playlist_id UUID FK, track_id UUID FK, action ENUM play/skip/save/share, event_at TIMESTAMPTZ). prompt_registry (id UUID PK, version TEXT UNIQUE, template TEXT, model TEXT, token_budget INT, active BOOL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). curation_sessions (id UUID PK, listener_id UUID FK, mood_input_hash TEXT, llm_response_cached BOOL, latency_ms INT, openai_tokens_used INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/recommend/playlist — body: {mood_input, listener_id}, SSE stream response with track_ids and narrative tokens. GET /v1/listener/profile/:id — genre weights and mood history summary. PATCH /v1/listener/profile/:id/preferences — update genre weight overrides. POST /v1/engage/event — body: {listener_id, playlist_id, track_id, action}. GET /v1/prompts/:version_id — prompt template detail for monitoring. GET /v1/gdpr/export/:listener_id — GDPR Article 20 data export. DELETE /v1/gdpr/erasure/:listener_id — GDPR Article 17 erasure request. All endpoints require Cognito JWT; /v1/prompts and /v1/gdpr/erasure require admin scope.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito PKCE enforced for all listener-facing endpoints; JWKS cached in FastAPI middleware with 5-minute TTL. PII-stripping middleware: spaCy NER pass removes PERSON, EMAIL, and PHONE entities from mood inputs before LLM dispatch. Prompt injection detection: keyword blocklist (system prompt overrides, role injection strings) plus rate limit 10 requests/listener/minute via Redis token bucket. LLM prompt logs (curation_sessions.mood_input_hash only — no raw text) retained 30 days then purged by scheduled job. GDPR erasure: DELETE cascade on listener_profiles removes playlists, recommendation_events, curation_sessions within 30 days; Kinesis-derived S3 events anonymized via Glue job. Data processing agreement with OpenAI in place; no listener PII in prompt payloads enforced by architecture.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: pytest coverage >85%, ruff, mypy, docker build, ECR push, Helm upgrade to EKS canary (10% traffic first 30 minutes, full rollout on <1% error rate). EKS node groups: inference-workers c6g.xlarge HPA on p95 latency target 1800ms (4-20 nodes), retraining-jobs m6i.2xlarge spot. Karpenter scales inference nodes to minimum 2 overnight. Database migrations via Alembic run as EKS Job pre-deployment. Next.js deployed to Vercel with API routes proxying to EKS ALB. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager injected as EKS pod environment variables via External Secrets Operator.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM auto-instrumentation on FastAPI pods with listener_id and prompt_version_id trace tags. Custom metrics: llm_call_latency_p50_ms (alert >2000ms sustained 5min), openai_tokens_per_request (alert >1800), recommendation_cache_hit_rate (alert <60%), fallback_ranker_activation_count (alert >10/min signals LLM degradation), gdpr_erasure_completion_hours (SLA 720h). Kinesis Firehose delivery latency monitored via CloudWatch. A/B prompt version relevance scores queried from Athena nightly into Datadog dashboard.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: OpenAI cost controlled via Redis cache hit rate target >60% (estimated $5000-7000/month at scale), 1500-token hard budget per request, and stream=True avoiding buffering charges. EKS inference nodes scale 4-20 on HPA; overnight Karpenter scale-down saves ~40% compute cost. Spot instances for retraining jobs save 70% over on-demand. Estimated total infrastructure: $4000-6000/month including EKS, RDS, Redis, and Kinesis at 500K daily playlist requests.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Mood playlist generation with GPT-4o, PII-stripping middleware, Cognito PKCE auth, listener_profiles and playlists in PostgreSQL, Redis prompt cache, fallback_ranker, SSE streaming frontend, basic Datadog LLM latency and cost metrics. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Prompt registry A/B testing framework, Kinesis engagement pipeline with Athena evaluation, GDPR export and erasure endpoints, genre weight preference overrides, pgvector embedding similarity for track selection improvement. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Open-source model fine-tuning evaluation to reduce OpenAI dependency, collaborative playlist sharing with LLM curation, label promotional campaign integration with mood context seeding.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python ruff and mypy in CI with strict type annotations on all FastAPI handlers. Pydantic v2 models for all request/response schemas with explicit PII fields marked for stripping. pytest-asyncio for async handler tests; coverage gate at 85%. Prompt templates reviewed in code review before merge to prompt_registry. No listener PII in application logs — use listener_id only. OpenAI SDK configured with timeout=10s and max_retries=2; never allow default retry behavior to bypass circuit breaker.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: OpenAI API cost overrun if cache hit rate falls below 60% on diverse mood inputs. Mitigation: semantic clustering of mood embeddings to expand cache hit surface beyond exact string match. Risk: GPT-4o hallucinating track titles not in catalog. Mitigation: post-processing validates all returned track_ids against PostgreSQL before returning playlist; invalid IDs replaced with catalog-ranked alternatives. Risk: PII leakage via indirect references in mood inputs ('play something like my friend Sarah likes'). Mitigation: NER pass plus fuzzy name matching against listener contact list as additional stripping layer. Tradeoff: Redis cache returns identical playlist for same mood hash — add listener_id salt to cache key to maintain personalization at cost of lower cache hit rate.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: GPT-4o selected over GPT-3.5 for narrative quality; cost controlled via caching rather than model downgrade. stream=True chosen for SSE progressive rendering over batch response to meet 2s perceived latency target. Redis prompt cache key uses sha256(stripped_mood+genre_context) without listener_id salt in phase 1; personalized salt evaluated in phase 2 after measuring cache hit rate impact. Cognito PKCE confirmed over API key auth for browser security compliance.

metadata.notes: LLM-powered streaming music recommendation replacing rules engine; GPT-4o with PII-stripping, Redis prompt caching, SSE streaming, and GDPR controls.

reasoner_reviews.product: The evaluation framework comparing neural outputs against the legacy rule-based baseline using harmonic coherence scoring is the right research gate. Ensure generated_patterns are always exported as standard MIDI files so the researcher can load them in a DAW for perceptual evaluation alongside quantitative metrics.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: PyTorch transformer with music21 tokenization is a sensible choice for MIDI sequence modeling. DuckDB is ideal for querying corpus metadata and evaluation results without server overhead. The key unresolved architecture issue from round 2 is the checkpoint management strategy — running 10 training experiments without a structured checkpoint naming convention will make reproducibility difficult. Add a checkpoint_manifest table to DuckDB.
reasoner_reviews.security: Local-only prototype with no network listeners presents minimal security surface. No changes needed from security perspective.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: RTX 4090 with 24GB VRAM can train a 6-layer transformer on 10K MIDI compositions within 8 hours at batch size 32. Confirm sequence length truncation at 512 tokens to avoid OOM. Gradient checkpointing may be needed if model depth increases beyond phase 1 configuration.

backend.service_design: Modules: corpus_loader (MIDI file ingestion via music21, tokenization, DuckDB metadata writes), model_trainer (PyTorch training loop, gradient checkpointing, checkpoint saves), pattern_generator (temperature-sampled autoregressive generation), evaluator (harmonic coherence scoring against rule-based baseline), experiment_manager (MLflow run lifecycle).
backend.api_patterns: FastAPI mounts Gradio app at /ui. Internal REST endpoints: POST /train (launch training run with hyperparams), POST /generate (pattern generation with checkpoint_id and temperature), GET /experiments (MLflow run list from DuckDB), GET /evaluate/:run_id (harmonic coherence score comparison).
backend.background_jobs: Training runs execute in a Python ThreadPoolExecutor to avoid blocking the Gradio UI. Corpus preprocessing (tokenization) runs as a one-time batch job on first corpus upload, with progress tracked in DuckDB corpus_preprocessing_status table.
backend.failure_handling: Training run OOM error caught and logged to DuckDB training_runs.error_message; checkpoint saved at last successful epoch before OOM. Gradio UI shows run status from DuckDB polling every 5 seconds.

frontend.app_structure: Single Gradio Blocks app with tabs: Corpus Management (upload, preprocessing status, corpus stats), Training (hyperparameter sliders, launch button, live loss curve), Generation (checkpoint selector, temperature slider, MIDI file output), Evaluation (side-by-side neural vs rule-based metrics table, MLflow run link).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Researcher uploads MIDI corpus zip in Corpus tab; preprocessing job tokenizes files and writes metadata to DuckDB. Researcher sets layer depth, learning rate, batch size in Training tab and clicks Run — FastAPI dispatches training in background thread. On completion, researcher selects checkpoint in Generation tab, sets temperature (0.5-1.5), generates 8-bar MIDI pattern for DAW review. Evaluation tab compares harmonic_coherence_score, note_density, and interval_diversity against legacy baseline.
frontend.performance_notes: Gradio polling for training progress uses 5-second interval via gr.State update. Generated MIDI files offered as download links via Gradio File component.

data.entities: midi_compositions (id INT PK, filename TEXT, source_corpus TEXT, composer TEXT, time_signature TEXT, key TEXT, token_count INT, tokenized_path TEXT, ingested_at). training_runs (id UUID PK, run_name TEXT, corpus_id INT FK, model_depth INT, learning_rate FLOAT, batch_size INT, max_epochs INT, best_val_loss FLOAT, status ENUM pending/running/completed/failed, error_message TEXT, started_at, completed_at). model_checkpoints (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK, epoch INT, val_loss FLOAT, checkpoint_path TEXT, saved_at). generated_patterns (id UUID PK, checkpoint_id UUID FK, temperature FLOAT, output_midi_path TEXT, bar_count INT, generated_at). evaluation_metrics (id UUID PK, generated_pattern_id UUID FK, harmonic_coherence_score FLOAT, note_density FLOAT, interval_diversity FLOAT, baseline_coherence_score FLOAT, eval_at). harmony_rules (id INT PK, rule_name TEXT, description TEXT, weight FLOAT) -- legacy rules preserved for baseline comparisons.
data.schema_notes: DuckDB analytical queries group evaluation_metrics by checkpoint temperature to produce quality-vs-creativity tradeoff curves. training_runs joined with model_checkpoints for reproducibility report.
data.migration_notes: Legacy harmony_rules table populated from static JSON export of legacy rule engine on first init. No ongoing sync needed.

devops.deployment_topology: Single local Python process: Uvicorn server running FastAPI + Gradio on localhost:7860. PyTorch training dispatched to background thread using GPU device cuda:0. MLflow tracking server runs on localhost:5000 as separate process started by launch script.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest for corpus_loader tokenization, evaluator harmonic coherence calculation, and model_trainer epoch logic with small synthetic MIDI fixtures. ruff linter. No Docker — local workstation install via uv environment.
devops.observability_stack: MLflow logs: training loss per epoch, validation loss, sample generated MIDI files as artifacts, hyperparameter dict, best checkpoint path. DuckDB training_runs and evaluation_metrics queryable from Gradio Evaluation tab or DuckDB CLI.

ISS-001.title: No checkpoint naming convention causes reproducibility failures
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan saved checkpoints as epoch_N.pt without embedding run_id or hyperparameters in filename, making it impossible to trace which checkpoint corresponds to which experiment after running multiple training jobs.

ISS-002.title: Evaluation metric not comparing against legacy baseline
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 plan added harmonic_coherence_score but only measured neural model outputs without computing the baseline legacy rule-based score for the same prompts, making quality comparison impossible.

ISS-003.title: Training OOM risk not addressed for long MIDI sequences
ISS-003.detail: The plan does not specify sequence length truncation or gradient checkpointing strategy, leaving the training loop vulnerable to CUDA OOM on compositions longer than 512 tokens on the RTX 4090.
