target_output.title: Neural MIDI Pattern Generator — Legacy Modernization Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A local research prototype on a Linux GPU workstation replacing a hardcoded harmony rule engine with a PyTorch transformer sequence model trained on a MIDI composition corpus. Researchers interact via a Gradio UI to upload corpora, configure hyperparameters, launch training runs, generate MIDI patterns at variable temperature, and evaluate harmonic coherence against the legacy rule-based baseline. DuckDB stores corpus metadata, training run records, and evaluation metrics; MLflow tracks experiment loss curves and generated artifacts. This round resolves the OOM risk by enforcing 512-token sequence truncation and gradient checkpointing for deep model configurations.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process local system: Uvicorn serves FastAPI with Gradio mounted at /ui on localhost:7860. PyTorch training dispatched to a background thread using CUDA device cuda:0 (RTX 4090 24GB). Corpus preprocessing tokenizes MIDI events via music21 event vocabulary and truncates sequences to max_length=512 tokens using tail-truncation to preserve harmonic resolution endings. For model configurations with depth >6 transformer layers, torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint_sequential activates gradient checkpointing, trading ~25% training throughput for VRAM headroom. OOM exceptions caught in training loop, saving the most recent checkpoint to DuckDB before surfacing error status in the Gradio UI. DuckDB stores corpus metadata, training_runs, model_checkpoints (with run_id in filename for reproducibility), generated_patterns, and evaluation_metrics comparing neural harmonic_coherence_score against rule-based baseline. MLflow tracking server runs on localhost:5000 logging epoch losses, hyperparameters, and generated MIDI artifacts per run.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12, PyTorch 2.3 with CUDA 12, music21 for MIDI tokenization, Gradio 4.x for researcher UI, FastAPI for inference and training dispatch API, DuckDB for analytical corpus and experiment queries, MLflow for experiment tracking, uv for local environment management. Hardware: NVIDIA RTX 4090 24GB VRAM, Linux workstation. Evaluation: custom harmonic_coherence_scorer comparing neural and rule-based outputs using music21 chord analysis.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Corpus Management: upload MIDI zip, tokenize with music21 event vocabulary, truncate sequences at 512 tokens (tail strategy), write metadata to DuckDB midi_compositions. Training: configure depth (2-12 layers), learning rate, batch size via Gradio sliders; launch background training with gradient checkpointing auto-enabled for depth >6; poll status from DuckDB every 5 seconds. Pattern Generation: select checkpoint by run_id+epoch, set temperature (0.5-1.5), generate 4-16 bar autoregressive MIDI sequence, download as .mid file. Evaluation: compute harmonic_coherence_score on generated patterns and rule-based baseline for same prompts; compare note_density and interval_diversity; log to evaluation_metrics. Experiment History: query DuckDB evaluation_metrics by temperature and checkpoint for quality-vs-creativity tradeoff curves; link to MLflow run artifacts.
target_output.system_components: Uvicorn server (FastAPI + Gradio localhost:7860), PyTorch transformer model (6-layer default, gradient checkpointing >6), music21 MIDI tokenizer (event vocabulary, 512-token truncation), corpus_loader module, model_trainer module (OOM exception handler, epoch checkpoint saves), pattern_generator module (temperature sampling), evaluator module (harmonic_coherence_scorer vs rule-based baseline), DuckDB local database file (corpus metadata + experiment results), MLflow tracking server (localhost:5000), checkpoint file store (local filesystem, run_id-prefixed filenames).
target_output.workflows: Training Run Workflow: Researcher uploads MIDI corpus zip in Gradio Corpus tab. corpus_loader extracts files, runs music21 tokenization, truncates each sequence at 512 tokens (tail strategy), writes midi_compositions record to DuckDB including token_count and tokenized_path. Researcher sets hyperparameters in Training tab: depth=6, lr=0.0001, batch_size=32. Clicks Run — FastAPI POST /train spawns background thread, creates training_runs record with status=running. Training loop runs with gradient checkpointing disabled at depth=6; OOM handler wraps each epoch, saving checkpoint on exception. MLflow logs val_loss per epoch. On completion, training_runs updated to status=completed with best_val_loss. Evaluation Workflow: Researcher selects completed checkpoint in Evaluation tab. FastAPI POST /evaluate/:run_id generates 20 patterns at temperature=1.0 using the checkpoint. evaluator module runs harmonic_coherence_scorer on each generated pattern and on 20 rule-based baseline outputs for the same seed prompts. Scores written to evaluation_metrics. Gradio Evaluation tab renders side-by-side violin plot of neural vs baseline coherence distributions and exports DuckDB query results as CSV.
target_output.data_model: midi_compositions (id INT PK, filename TEXT, source_corpus TEXT, composer TEXT, time_signature TEXT, key TEXT, token_count INT, sequence_truncated BOOL, tokenized_path TEXT, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ). training_runs (id UUID PK, run_name TEXT, corpus_filter TEXT, model_depth INT, learning_rate FLOAT, batch_size INT, sequence_max_length INT DEFAULT 512, gradient_checkpointing BOOL, max_epochs INT, best_val_loss FLOAT, best_checkpoint_id UUID FK, status ENUM pending/running/completed/failed, error_message TEXT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). model_checkpoints (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK, epoch INT, val_loss FLOAT, checkpoint_path TEXT, saved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). generated_patterns (id UUID PK, checkpoint_id UUID FK, temperature FLOAT, seed_prompt TEXT, output_midi_path TEXT, bar_count INT, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). evaluation_metrics (id UUID PK, generated_pattern_id UUID FK, harmonic_coherence_score FLOAT, note_density FLOAT, interval_diversity FLOAT, baseline_coherence_score FLOAT, eval_method TEXT, eval_at TIMESTAMPTZ). harmony_rules (id INT PK, rule_name TEXT, description TEXT, weight FLOAT).
target_output.api_design: POST /train — body: {corpus_filter, model_depth, learning_rate, batch_size, max_epochs}, returns run_id. GET /train/:run_id/status — training_runs record with current epoch and val_loss. POST /generate — body: {checkpoint_id, temperature, bar_count}, returns generated MIDI file path. POST /evaluate/:run_id — triggers evaluation of all checkpoints in run, returns evaluation_metrics summary. GET /experiments — DuckDB query of training_runs joined with best evaluation_metrics, sorted by harmonic_coherence_score. All endpoints localhost only; no auth.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Local-only system bound to 127.0.0.1; no external network listeners. No credentials stored — DuckDB file and MIDI checkpoints on local filesystem with OS-level access controls. No PII in corpus or evaluation data. MLflow tracking server bound to localhost:5000. No security hardening required beyond standard local process isolation.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Launch script starts MLflow server on localhost:5000 and Uvicorn on localhost:7860 via uv run. GitHub Actions CI: pytest for corpus_loader tokenization and truncation logic, evaluator harmonic coherence calculation, and training_runs OOM exception handler with synthetic MIDI fixtures. ruff linter. No Docker — uv environment pinned in pyproject.toml. Model checkpoints backed up manually by researcher to external drive or NAS.
target_output.observability: MLflow experiment tracking: training loss and validation loss per epoch, best_val_loss, hyperparameter dict, generated MIDI artifacts per run. DuckDB evaluation_metrics queryable for quality-vs-creativity curves by temperature. Gradio Training tab polls DuckDB training_runs.status every 5 seconds and renders live loss curve from MLflow run data. OOM events logged to training_runs.error_message for post-mortem analysis.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud cost — local GPU workstation only. Electricity cost of 8-hour RTX 4090 training run approximately $0.60 at local rates. DuckDB and MLflow consume minimal additional resources. Corpus storage scales with MIDI file count; 10K compositions average 50KB each = ~500MB total.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (completed prior rounds): MIDI corpus ingestion, 6-layer transformer training, pattern generation, MLflow logging, harmonic coherence evaluation vs rule-based baseline, OOM handling with 512-token truncation. Phase 2 (next iteration): Genre-specific corpus filtering for sub-genre model fine-tuning, MIDI-to-audio rendering via FluidSynth for perceptual listening evaluation, temperature sweep experiments across 0.5-1.5 range with automated evaluation_metrics export. Phase 3: Web-based Gradio demo deployment for peer review, larger model configuration (12-layer) with gradient checkpointing, corpus expansion to 50K compositions.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python ruff linter and type annotations enforced in CI. pytest fixtures use synthetic 16-token MIDI sequences to keep CI runtime under 60 seconds — do not use full corpus in tests. Checkpoint filenames must follow convention: {run_id}_{epoch:03d}_valloss{val_loss:.4f}.pt. DuckDB accessed via context manager; never hold connection open across request boundaries in FastAPI. Gradient checkpointing flag set automatically in model_trainer when model_depth > 6 — do not require researcher to enable manually. sequence_max_length=512 is a hardcoded constant in corpus_loader; changing it requires reprocessing the entire corpus.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: sequence tail-truncation discards harmonic context from the opening bars of long compositions. Mitigation: evaluate truncated vs full-sequence subset quality in phase 2 to determine whether sliding window tokenization improves coherence. Risk: gradient checkpointing at depth >6 slows training by ~25%; 8-hour budget may require reducing epochs. Mitigation: monitor val_loss convergence curve and early-stop if plateau reached. Risk: music21 tokenization vocabulary does not capture continuous controller data (pitch bend, velocity curves), limiting expressivity of generated patterns. Accepted for research prototype scope. Tradeoff: DuckDB over SQLite for analytical query support on evaluation_metrics at cost of slightly higher binary size.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved in round 2: checkpoint naming uses run_id prefix for reproducibility. ISS-002 resolved in round 2: evaluation_metrics now computes baseline_coherence_score from legacy rule engine for same seed prompts. ISS-003 resolved this round: sequence_max_length=512 enforced in corpus_loader with tail-truncation; gradient checkpointing auto-enabled for depth >6 layers; OOM exception handler saves checkpoint and updates training_runs.error_message.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision resolving OOM risk for neural MIDI generator; adds 512-token truncation and gradient checkpointing specification.

project_goal.value: Migrate a music publisher's legacy metadata catalog from a flat-file CSV system to a structured PostgreSQL database, deduplicating musical works, resolving conflicting ISWC codes, and enriching records via a third-party rights database API
project_goal.rationale: Legacy CSV catalog has accumulated duplicate works and ISWC conflicts over 20 years; structured database migration enables reliable rights management

target_users.value: Music publisher catalog operations team running migration batches on an internal VPS
target_users.rationale: Internal operations staff trigger and monitor migrations; not a user-facing application

project_class.rationale: Batch CLI tool executing data ingestion, deduplication, conflict resolution, and API enrichment pipelines

capabilities.rationale: Python CLI backend, PostgreSQL data platform, third-party rights API integration, and batch processing pipeline

complexity_level.rationale: CSV parsing, deduplication logic, ISWC conflict resolution rules, and external API enrichment define moderate complexity

risk_level.rationale: Confidential publishing rights data; incorrect deduplication or ISWC assignment could cause royalty disputes

data_sensitivity.rationale: Music publishing rights data is commercially sensitive under confidentiality terms with rights societies

external_exposure.rationale: CLI tool runs on internal VPS with no public-facing endpoints; rights API calls are outbound only

access_model.value: VPS OS-level access control; CLI requires sudo for DB operations; rights API key stored in environment variable
access_model.rationale: Internal tool with no user authentication layer; access controlled at OS level

feature_scope.value: CSV ingestion with schema validation, deduplication by canonical work title and ISWC, ISWC conflict flagging and resolution queue, third-party rights API enrichment for society registration data, migration run audit log, conflict_reports for manual review
feature_scope.rationale: Full migration pipeline scope required to produce clean, deduplicated catalog

mvp_scope.value: CSV ingestion, deduplication logic, ISWC conflict flagging to conflict_reports table, basic audit log
mvp_scope.rationale: Deduplication and conflict flagging first; rights API enrichment follows in phase 2 once base migration is validated

security_baseline.value: PostgreSQL credentials in environment variables; rights API key rotated quarterly; migration run logs retained for audit; no plaintext credentials in codebase
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential data requires credential hygiene and audit trail

backend_stack.rationale: Click provides ergonomic CLI command structure; Python ecosystem for CSV processing and HTTP client calls

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Hetzner VPS
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for musical works, ISWC codes, rights holders, and conflict tracking; Hetzner VPS matches existing infrastructure

hosting_target.value: Hetzner bare-metal VPS
hosting_target.rationale: Existing infrastructure; migration tool runs as CLI invocations, not a persistent service

privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Rights society audit obligations require migration audit trail retention

future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after base migration pipeline validated

constraints.rationale: Publisher catalog size and rights API rate limit define throughput requirements

observability_baseline.value: Structured JSON logs to file; Grafana + Prometheus for VPS resource monitoring; migration_runs table for run-level audit
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing Grafana stack on VPS; migration audit needs structured per-run records

execution_preference.rationale: Catalog team requires dry-run validation before committing migration to production database

compliance_context.value: Music publishing rights data subject to confidentiality terms with ASCAP, BMI, SESAC; ISWC assignments must be verifiable against rights society registrations; migration audit trail required for dispute resolution
compliance_context.rationale: Rights society confidentiality terms and potential royalty dispute audit needs require strict data handling and audit logging

project.goal: Migrate legacy CSV music catalog to structured PostgreSQL with deduplication and ISWC conflict resolution
project.target_users: Catalog operations team on internal VPS
project.timeline: Single overnight migration run after phase 1 validation

backend.framework: Click CLI with modular pipeline commands
backend.api_client: httpx for rights API calls with retry and rate limit backoff

security.auth: OS-level VPS access; API key in environment variable
security.credentials: No plaintext credentials in codebase; .env file excluded from git

data.orm: psycopg3 with transaction management per batch

devops.ci: GitLab CI for unit tests and linting

constraints.api_rate: rights API max 100 req/min with exponential backoff

open_questions.ml_dedup: ML similarity deduplication deferred to phase 2; phase 1 uses deterministic title normalization and ISWC matching

confirmed_decisions.dedup_strategy: deterministic title normalization + ISWC exact match

reasoner_reviews.product: The dry-run mode is critical for operator confidence — catalog team must be able to preview deduplication decisions and ISWC conflict flags without touching the production database. Ensure the conflict_reports output is human-readable (CSV export) so catalog managers can review and annotate before committing the run.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Python Click with psycopg3 is the right lightweight choice for a batch migration CLI. Chunked batch inserts of 1000 records at a time with explicit transaction boundaries will handle 500K records within an overnight window. The rights API rate limit of 100 req/min requires a token-bucket throttle in the httpx client — use a leaky bucket implementation to avoid 429 errors during enrichment phase.
reasoner_reviews.security: Credentials must not appear in process arguments or shell history. Rights API key must be read from environment variable only — not from CLI flag. Migration audit log must capture run start/end times, record counts, and operator username for dispute resolution. Conflict_reports table must not allow DELETE to preserve audit integrity.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 500K records at 1000 per batch = 500 batches. At 2 seconds per batch including deduplication hash computation, the ingestion phase completes in under 20 minutes. Rights API enrichment at 100 req/min for 500K records would take 83 hours — confirm that enrichment is scoped to records missing society data only, not all records.

backend.service_design: Click CLI with commands: migrate ingest (CSV parsing, schema validation, batch insert), migrate deduplicate (title normalization, ISWC matching, duplicate flagging), migrate enrich (rights API calls for missing society data), migrate report (conflict_reports CSV export), migrate status (migration_runs summary). Each command is idempotent — re-runnable without creating duplicate records.
backend.api_patterns: No inbound API. Outbound: httpx client to rights database REST API with token-bucket rate limiter (100 req/min), exponential backoff on 429 and 5xx, 3 retries max. Enrichment responses cached in rights_api_cache table to avoid redundant calls on re-run.
backend.background_jobs: Migration commands run as foreground CLI processes, not background jobs. Long-running deduplicate and enrich commands display Click progress bars. Designed for cron scheduling: 0 2 * * 1 python migrate.py ingest --source /data/catalog.csv >> /var/log/migrate.log 2>&1.
backend.failure_handling: Ingestion phase: row-level validation errors written to ingestion_errors table and skipped; run continues. Deduplication phase: duplicate groups with >50% title similarity flagged to conflict_reports; not auto-resolved. Enrichment phase: API failures after 3 retries written to enrichment_failures; run continues with partial enrichment.

data.entities: musical_works (id UUID PK, canonical_title TEXT, normalized_title TEXT, iswc CHAR(15), status ENUM active/duplicate/conflict, migration_run_id UUID FK, source_row_hash TEXT, created_at). iswc_codes (iswc CHAR(15) PK, work_id UUID FK, assigned_at, registrant_society TEXT). rights_holders (id UUID PK, name TEXT, ipi_number CHAR(11), publisher_share_pct NUMERIC(5,2), work_id UUID FK). publisher_shares (id UUID PK, work_id UUID FK, rights_holder_id UUID FK, share_pct NUMERIC(5,2), territory TEXT, effective_date DATE). conflict_reports (id UUID PK, work_id_a UUID FK, work_id_b UUID FK, conflict_type ENUM iswc_collision/title_duplicate/rights_overlap, detail TEXT, status ENUM open/resolved/dismissed, created_at -- no DELETE grant). migration_runs (id UUID PK, run_name TEXT, source_csv TEXT, total_rows INT, ingested INT, deduplicated INT, conflicts_flagged INT, enriched INT, status ENUM running/completed/failed, started_by TEXT, started_at, completed_at). ingestion_errors (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK, source_row_num INT, error_type TEXT, raw_row TEXT, created_at). rights_api_cache (iswc CHAR(15) PK, api_response JSONB, fetched_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.schema_notes: musical_works.normalized_title generated by lowercasing, stripping punctuation, and collapsing whitespace — used as deduplication key. source_row_hash is MD5 of raw CSV row for idempotent re-ingestion detection. conflict_reports.status uses INSERT-only audit; updates via UPDATE but no DELETE.
data.migration_notes: Alembic migrations manage schema versioning. Initial migration creates all tables and indexes. Rollback migration drops only non-catalog tables (migration_runs, conflict_reports, ingestion_errors) to allow re-migration without losing catalog data.

devops.deployment_topology: CLI installed on Hetzner VPS via uv pip install. PostgreSQL 16 on same VPS. No containerization for production; Docker Compose used for dev environment with PostgreSQL 16 container.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI pipeline: pytest with 80% coverage gate, ruff linter, mypy, integration tests using pytest-postgresql with test fixtures of 1000 synthetic MIDI work records. CI validates deduplication logic and ISWC conflict detection rules.
devops.observability_stack: Structured JSON logs via structlog to /var/log/migrate.log with run_id, command, record_count, and duration fields per batch. migration_runs table provides run-level audit queryable via psql. Grafana + Prometheus monitors VPS CPU and PostgreSQL connection count during overnight runs.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a batch CLI migration tool for a music publisher's legacy CSV catalog. Key decisions: Click CLI with idempotent commands for safe re-runs, psycopg3 chunked batch inserts at 1000 records/batch for 500K throughput within overnight window, token-bucket rate limiter for rights API (100 req/min) with enrichment scoped to records missing society data only, conflict_reports table with no DELETE grants for audit integrity, dry-run mode for pre-validation.
target_output.title: Music Publisher Legacy Catalog Migration CLI — Architecture Plan
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 Click CLI automation tool migrating a music publisher's 500K-record legacy CSV catalog to PostgreSQL 16 on Hetzner VPS. The pipeline handles CSV ingestion with schema validation, deterministic deduplication by normalized title and ISWC matching, ISWC conflict flagging to a human-reviewable conflict_reports table, and optional rights API enrichment for missing society registration data at a rate-limited 100 req/min. All commands are idempotent and support dry-run mode. An append-only migration_runs audit table records every run for rights society dispute resolution.
target_output.architecture_overview: CLI-only system with no persistent service process. Python Click commands run as foreground processes on a Hetzner VPS, reading CSV source files and writing to a local PostgreSQL 16 database. The ingest command validates CSV rows against a Pydantic schema, computes source_row_hash for idempotent re-run detection, and inserts in 1000-record psycopg3 batches within explicit transactions. The deduplicate command computes normalized_title (lowercase, stripped punctuation) for each musical_work and groups by normalized_title or exact ISWC match to identify duplicates; flagging them to conflict_reports without auto-resolution. The enrich command calls a third-party rights database REST API via httpx with a token-bucket rate limiter enforcing 100 req/min; enrichment scoped to works missing registrant_society to avoid processing all 500K records. Responses cached in rights_api_cache by ISWC. The report command exports conflict_reports as CSV for catalog manager review. All runs recorded in migration_runs with row counts and operator identity for audit.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12, Click 8.x for CLI command structure, psycopg3 for PostgreSQL with explicit transaction and batch copy support, Pydantic v2 for CSV row schema validation, httpx for async rights API calls with token-bucket rate limiter, structlog for structured JSON log output, Alembic for schema migrations, uv for VPS environment management. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Hetzner VPS. CI: GitLab CI with pytest-postgresql for integration tests.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Ingest Command: parse legacy CSV export, validate each row against musical_works Pydantic schema, compute MD5 source_row_hash for idempotent detection, batch insert 1000 rows/transaction with row-level errors written to ingestion_errors and skipped. Deduplicate Command: normalize all musical_works.titles, group by normalized_title and ISWC, flag duplicate pairs to conflict_reports with conflict_type=title_duplicate or iswc_collision. Enrich Command: query works with NULL registrant_society, batch-call rights API at 100 req/min with token-bucket, cache responses in rights_api_cache, update works with society registration data. Report Command: export open conflict_reports as CSV with work titles, ISWCs, and conflict details for manual catalog team review. Status Command: print migration_runs summary with record counts and completion status.
target_output.system_components: Click CLI entrypoint (migrate.py), CSV ingestion module (Pydantic validation, psycopg3 batch inserts), deduplication module (title normalizer, ISWC conflict detector, conflict_reports writer), enrichment module (httpx rights API client, token-bucket rate limiter, rights_api_cache writer), report exporter (conflict CSV generator), migration_runs recorder (audit log per run), PostgreSQL 16 on Hetzner VPS, structlog JSON log file, GitLab CI pipeline.
target_output.workflows: Overnight Migration Workflow: Catalog operations team receives monthly CSV export from legacy system. Operator runs python migrate.py ingest --source /data/catalog_2026_03.csv --dry-run to preview validation errors without DB writes. After reviewing ingestion_errors preview, operator runs without --dry-run; 500K records insert in ~500 batches with progress bar. Next, operator runs python migrate.py deduplicate — tool normalizes titles, groups duplicates, writes iswc_collision and title_duplicate records to conflict_reports. Operator runs python migrate.py report --output /data/conflicts_2026_03.csv and shares CSV with catalog manager for manual review. After managers annotate conflicts, operator updates conflict_reports.status to resolved or dismissed. Enrichment Workflow: Operator runs python migrate.py enrich --scope missing-society — tool queries musical_works WHERE registrant_society IS NULL, batches ISWCs in groups of 10, calls rights API at 100 req/min with token-bucket throttle. For each hit, updates musical_works.registrant_society and caches response in rights_api_cache. Enrichment failures written to enrichment_failures table for manual follow-up. Run completes in under 6 hours for 36K records missing society data.
target_output.data_model: musical_works (id UUID PK, canonical_title TEXT, normalized_title TEXT, iswc CHAR(15), registrant_society TEXT, status ENUM active/duplicate/conflict, migration_run_id UUID FK, source_row_hash TEXT UNIQUE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). iswc_codes (iswc CHAR(15) PK, work_id UUID FK, assigned_at TIMESTAMPTZ, registrant_society TEXT). rights_holders (id UUID PK, name TEXT, ipi_number CHAR(11), publisher_share_pct NUMERIC(5,2), work_id UUID FK). publisher_shares (id UUID PK, work_id UUID FK, rights_holder_id UUID FK, share_pct NUMERIC(5,2), territory CHAR(2), effective_date DATE). conflict_reports (id UUID PK, work_id_a UUID FK, work_id_b UUID FK, conflict_type ENUM iswc_collision/title_duplicate/rights_overlap, detail TEXT, status ENUM open/resolved/dismissed, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ -- INSERT-only + UPDATE; no DELETE grants). migration_runs (id UUID PK, run_name TEXT, source_csv TEXT, total_rows INT, ingested INT, deduplicated INT, conflicts_flagged INT, enriched INT, status TEXT, started_by TEXT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). ingestion_errors (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK, source_row_num INT, error_type TEXT, raw_row TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). rights_api_cache (iswc CHAR(15) PK, api_response JSONB, fetched_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: No inbound API endpoints. Outbound rights API: GET https://api.rightsdb.example/v1/works?iswc={code} — returns society registration data. httpx client configured: timeout=30s, max_retries=3, exponential backoff on 429 and 5xx. Token-bucket rate limiter: 100 tokens per 60 seconds, refill rate 1.67/second. Batch ISWC queries: POST /v1/works/batch with up to 10 ISRCs per request to reduce call count by 10x. Rights API key read from RIGHTS_API_KEY environment variable only.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PostgreSQL credentials in DATABASE_URL environment variable; rights API key in RIGHTS_API_KEY environment variable. Neither stored in codebase or passed as CLI flags — operator sources .env file excluded from git via .gitignore. conflict_reports table has no DELETE grant for API role; only INSERT and UPDATE allowed to preserve dispute resolution audit trail. migration_runs.started_by records OS username of operator via os.getlogin(). Structured logs written to /var/log/migrate.log with restricted read permissions (chmod 640). Rights society confidentiality: musical_works data not transmitted to any external service except ISWCs to the contracted rights API. Alembic migration scripts reviewed before production apply.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Installation: uv pip install on Hetzner VPS into isolated virtual environment. Alembic alembic upgrade head run before first migration. Cron entry for monthly run: 0 2 1 * * cd /opt/migrate && source .env && python migrate.py ingest --source /data/latest.csv >> /var/log/migrate.log 2>&1. GitLab CI: pytest with pytest-postgresql fixtures creating test PostgreSQL database, 80% coverage gate, ruff linter, mypy type checks. Dev environment: Docker Compose with PostgreSQL 16 and pgAdmin for schema inspection. Backup: pg_dump of PostgreSQL to Hetzner Storage Box nightly via cron.
target_output.observability: structlog structured JSON logs: every batch records run_id, command, batch_num, rows_processed, duration_ms, errors_count. migration_runs table queryable for run-level summaries: total_rows, ingested, conflicts_flagged, enriched, status. Grafana + Prometheus monitors VPS CPU load and PostgreSQL active connections during overnight runs with alert on CPU >80% for 15 minutes. enrichment_failures table inspected manually after each enrichment run.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No ongoing infrastructure cost beyond existing Hetzner VPS (~$20/month). Rights API enrichment cost depends on vendor pricing per call — batch endpoint reduces call count by 10x. Tool designed for periodic batch runs, not continuous operation. 500K records processed in single overnight run with no scaling requirements.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): CSV ingestion with Pydantic validation and source_row_hash idempotency, deterministic deduplication by normalized title and ISWC match, conflict_reports flagging with CSV export, migration_runs audit log, dry-run mode, GitLab CI with pytest-postgresql. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Rights API enrichment with token-bucket rate limiter and rights_api_cache, enrichment_failures tracking, Alembic rollback migrations, scheduled cron automation. Phase 3 (4 weeks): ML fuzzy title similarity for improved deduplication, web-based conflict review UI, automated ISWC conflict resolution for high-confidence matches.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Click commands must be idempotent — re-running the same command on the same data must produce identical results without creating duplicates. Use source_row_hash for ingestion idempotency and rights_api_cache for enrichment idempotency. psycopg3 transactions committed per 1000-row batch; rollback on batch error, log error, continue with next batch. No credentials in CLI arguments or log output — structlog sanitizer strips DATABASE_URL and RIGHTS_API_KEY from log records. Pydantic v2 models for all CSV row schemas with explicit field validators for ISWC format (T-XXXXXXXXXX-Y). pytest fixtures use pytest-postgresql with 1000 synthetic works for integration tests; never use production database in CI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Deterministic normalized_title deduplication produces false positives for works with identical titles but different composers. Mitigation: conflict_reports flags for human review rather than auto-merging; operators resolve before committing. Risk: Rights API enrichment scoped to missing-society records may not catch stale society data on existing records. Mitigation: --force-refresh flag re-queries all records regardless of current society value. Risk: Source CSV encoding issues (Latin-1, Windows-1252) cause ingestion errors on non-ASCII artist names. Mitigation: charset detection via chardet on CSV open with fallback to UTF-8 replace mode and error logging. Tradeoff: ISWC CHAR(15) enforces ISO 15707 format at DB level; malformed legacy ISWCs written to ingestion_errors rather than truncated.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ML similarity deduplication deferred to phase 2; phase 1 uses deterministic normalized title and exact ISWC match for safety. Enrichment scoped to records missing registrant_society to avoid 83-hour full enrichment run against 100 req/min API limit. conflict_reports uses INSERT+UPDATE pattern with no DELETE to satisfy rights society audit requirements. Dry-run mode implemented by wrapping each command in a database transaction that is always rolled back.

metadata.notes: Music publisher CSV-to-PostgreSQL catalog migration CLI with deterministic deduplication, ISWC conflict flagging, and rights API enrichment.

profile.domain: Real-Time Audio Transcription and Lyrics Sync API

project_goal.value: Build a real-time audio transcription and timestamped lyrics synchronization API for podcast and music platforms, ingesting live audio streams and emitting word-level timing events over WebSockets
project_goal.rationale: Platform partners need sub-second word-level timestamps to display synchronized lyrics and captions during live and on-demand playback

target_users.value: Podcast and music platform engineering teams consuming the API to power synchronized lyrics and caption display features
target_users.rationale: B2B partner API; end users are listeners on partner platforms who see the synchronized captions

project_class.rationale: Backend API service with WebSocket streaming and REST endpoints; no browser UI

capabilities.rationale: Go WebSocket backend, TimescaleDB for time-series session data, Auth0 partner auth, partner-facing API, and real-time event streaming

complexity_level.rationale: Sub-second transcription latency, concurrent WebSocket session management, chunked PCM audio processing, and word-timestamp persistence define advanced complexity

risk_level.rationale: Partner SLAs on transcription latency; audio content is partner-proprietary; no PII in audio streams

data_sensitivity.rationale: Audio session data and transcription segments are partner-proprietary internal content; no listener PII stored

external_exposure.rationale: API endpoints exposed to authenticated platform partners only; no public unauthenticated access

access_model.value: Auth0 machine-to-machine client credentials per partner platform; JWT required to open WebSocket sessions
access_model.rationale: Automated M2M access for platform backend integration; JWT enforced on WebSocket upgrade

feature_scope.value: WebSocket session management for live PCM audio streaming, sub-second ASR transcription with word-level timestamps, timestamped lyric-sync event emission, session metadata and word_timestamps persistence in TimescaleDB, REST endpoints for post-session transcript retrieval
feature_scope.rationale: Core real-time and persistence features required for partner integration

mvp_scope.value: WebSocket session with PCM audio chunking, word-level timestamp emission, session_tokens and transcription_segments persistence
mvp_scope.rationale: Core real-time transcription loop first; on-demand retrieval and lyric-sync formatting follow in phase 2

security_baseline.value: Auth0 JWT for WebSocket upgrade, TLS 1.3 for all connections, partner session isolation, audio chunk data discarded after transcription
security_baseline.rationale: Partner audio content must not leak across sessions; audio buffers must not be persisted beyond transcription window

frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI; partner platforms consume WebSocket and REST endpoints directly

backend_stack.rationale: Go provides efficient concurrent WebSocket session management and low-latency PCM processing; Gorilla WebSocket for robust connection lifecycle handling

data_platform.value: TimescaleDB for time-series transcription segments and word timestamps; Redis 7 for active session state and rate limiting
data_platform.rationale: TimescaleDB time-series compression suits high-frequency word_timestamp inserts; Redis holds ephemeral session state for active connections

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot on GCP
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot handles node scaling for variable concurrent WebSocket load without manual node pool management

privacy_retention_policy.value: Transcription segments and word timestamps retained 90 days then purged; audio chunk buffers discarded immediately after ASR processing; session metadata retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Partners retain transcripts for on-demand playback; long-term audio storage is partner responsibility

future_scope.value: Batch transcription REST endpoint for pre-recorded files, speaker diarization output, lyric correction feedback loop from partner editors
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after real-time WebSocket pipeline validated at partner scale

constraints.value: Transcription word-timestamp latency must be under 800ms from audio chunk receipt; system must handle 500 concurrent WebSocket sessions per pod
constraints.rationale: Partner SLA requires sub-800ms word events for synchronized display; 500 sessions/pod drives pod sizing

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for WebSocket session metrics and ASR latency percentiles; structured JSON logs per session with partner_id and session_id
observability_baseline.rationale: Real-time latency SLA requires per-percentile monitoring; partner-specific session visibility

execution_preference.value: Phased rollout with load testing at each stage before increasing partner onboarding
execution_preference.rationale: WebSocket session concurrency and ASR latency must be validated under load before opening to all partners

compliance_context.value: Partner audio content governed by platform content agreements; audio buffers must not be stored; session data subject to partner data processing agreements
compliance_context.rationale: Partner content confidentiality requires strict audio buffer discard policy; session data DPAs required

project.goal: Real-time PCM audio transcription with word-level timestamps over WebSocket for partner platforms
project.target_users: Podcast and music platform engineering teams

backend.framework: Gorilla WebSocket for session management, standard library HTTP for REST
backend.asr: Google Cloud Speech-to-Text Streaming API for word-level timestamp output

security.auth: Auth0 M2M client credentials; JWT validated on WebSocket upgrade handshake
security.transport: TLS 1.3 for all connections; wss:// for WebSocket connections
security.isolation: Sessions scoped to partner_id; no cross-partner data access

data.db: TimescaleDB for transcription_segments and word_timestamps hypertables
data.cache: Redis 7 for active session_tokens and rate limit counters

constraints.latency: word-timestamp events under 800ms from chunk receipt

open_questions.asr_provider: Google Cloud Speech-to-Text selected for word-level confidence and streaming support

reasoner_reviews.product: Sub-800ms word-timestamp latency is the defining product requirement — everything else is secondary. Partners will abandon the integration if synchronized display lags. Confirm that Google Cloud Speech-to-Text streaming mode returns interim word boundaries fast enough; consider Deepgram as an alternative ASR provider with a lower time-to-first-word metric if GCP testing exceeds the SLA.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with Gorilla WebSocket is the right choice for 500 concurrent sessions per pod — Go goroutines handle this concurrency at far lower memory overhead than a thread-per-connection model. Each WebSocket session should own a dedicated goroutine pair: one for reading PCM chunks from the partner and one for writing timestamp events back. Redis session state must expire automatically (TTL equal to max session duration) to prevent stale session accumulation on pod restart.
reasoner_reviews.security: JWT validation must happen before the WebSocket upgrade completes — reject at the HTTP handshake stage, not after the connection is established. Audio chunk buffers must be passed directly to the ASR streaming client and never written to disk or Redis. Partner session isolation enforced by scoping all TimescaleDB queries with partner_id and session_id in WHERE clauses.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 500 concurrent sessions per pod at 500ms chunking = 1000 goroutines per pod plus ASR streaming goroutines. Profile memory at 500 sessions in load testing. TimescaleDB word_timestamps table will receive ~50 inserts/second per active session at average word rate; confirm chunk insert batching at 10-word boundaries to reduce insert frequency by 10x.

backend.service_design: Session manager handles WebSocket lifecycle: upgrade with JWT validation, goroutine pair spawn (reader and writer), ASR streaming client creation per session, Redis session_token registration, graceful drain on disconnect. ASR adapter abstracts Google Cloud Speech-to-Text streaming API, emitting word_timestamp events to the writer goroutine. Timestamp formatter converts ASR word boundaries to lyric-sync event JSON.
backend.api_patterns: WebSocket: wss://api.lyricsync.example/v1/stream — upgrade requires Authorization: Bearer JWT header. REST: POST /v1/sessions/:id/close (explicit session end), GET /v1/sessions/:id/transcript (post-session full transcript), GET /v1/sessions/:id/words?after_ms= (word timestamps since offset). Session events emitted as JSON: {type:'word',word:'hello',start_ms:1234,end_ms:1456,confidence:0.97}.
backend.scaling_notes: GKE Autopilot HPA on WebSocket session count metric (custom metric via Prometheus adapter). Target: 400 active sessions per pod before scaling out. Session affinity via GKE Gateway sticky sessions to avoid session migration on scale events.
backend.failure_handling: ASR streaming error: emit error event to partner, close ASR stream, re-initialize with same session_id for reconnect. WebSocket client disconnect: goroutines drain in-flight word events, write final word batch to TimescaleDB, expire Redis session_token. Pod termination: GKE sends SIGTERM, session manager waits for active sessions to drain (30-second grace period).

data.entities: audio_streams (id UUID PK, partner_id UUID FK, session_token TEXT, status ENUM active/closed/error, asr_provider TEXT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, closed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). transcription_segments (id UUID PK, stream_id UUID FK, partner_id UUID FK, segment_text TEXT, start_ms BIGINT, end_ms BIGINT, confidence FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- TimescaleDB hypertable partitioned by created_at. lyric_sync_events (id UUID PK, stream_id UUID FK, partner_id UUID FK, event_type ENUM word/sentence/silence, payload JSONB, emitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- TimescaleDB hypertable. word_timestamps (id UUID PK, stream_id UUID FK, partner_id UUID FK, word TEXT, start_ms BIGINT, end_ms BIGINT, confidence FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- TimescaleDB hypertable partitioned by created_at. session_tokens (session_id UUID PK, partner_id UUID FK, status ENUM active/closed, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- Redis hash. platform_clients (id UUID PK, name TEXT, auth0_client_id TEXT UNIQUE, allowed_stream_count INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: TimescaleDB hypertables on transcription_segments and word_timestamps partitioned weekly by created_at with chunk compression enabled after 7 days. Continuous aggregates for per-session word count and average confidence queryable by partners. Audio chunk buffers held only in goroutine memory; never written to disk or database.
data.retention_and_deletion: transcription_segments and word_timestamps purged after 90 days via TimescaleDB data retention policy drop_chunks(). audio_streams session metadata retained 1 year. session_tokens expire in Redis after max_session_duration (4 hours).

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot deployment: api pods with 2 vCPU / 4GB per pod, HPA custom metric on active_websocket_sessions (target 400/pod). GKE Gateway with sticky session affinity. Cloud SQL for TimescaleDB (Cloud SQL PostgreSQL with TimescaleDB extension). Redis via Memorystore.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build: go test ./..., golangci-lint, govulncheck, docker build, Artifact Registry push, Helm upgrade to GKE canary (10% traffic) validated by Prometheus p95 word-event latency metric before full rollout.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus custom metrics: active_websocket_sessions_total, word_event_latency_p95_ms (alert >800ms), asr_error_rate_per_min, timescaledb_insert_latency_ms. Grafana dashboards: per-partner session count, latency percentiles, ASR error rates. structlog JSON logs per session with partner_id, session_id, and trace_id.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a real-time audio transcription WebSocket API in Go. Key decisions: Go goroutine pairs per WebSocket session for 500 concurrent sessions per pod without thread-per-connection overhead, Google Cloud Speech-to-Text streaming for sub-800ms word timestamps, TimescaleDB hypertables for high-frequency word_timestamp inserts with 90-day retention, JWT validation at HTTP upgrade handshake before WebSocket connection established, audio buffers never persisted beyond goroutine memory to satisfy partner content security requirements.
target_output.title: Real-Time Audio Transcription and Lyrics Sync WebSocket API
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 WebSocket API on GKE Autopilot enabling podcast and music platform partners to stream live PCM audio and receive sub-800ms word-level timestamp events for synchronized lyrics and caption display. Each partner session opens a WebSocket connection authenticated via Auth0 JWT at upgrade, spawning a goroutine pair that pipes audio chunks to a Google Cloud Speech-to-Text streaming client and emits word-timestamp JSON events back to the partner. Transcription segments and word_timestamps are persisted in TimescaleDB hypertables for post-session retrieval, with a 90-day data retention policy. Audio chunk buffers are held only in goroutine memory and never written to disk.
target_output.architecture_overview: Go WebSocket server on GKE Autopilot handles up to 500 concurrent partner sessions per pod via goroutine pairs. Partner authenticates via Auth0 M2M client credentials and presents JWT in the WebSocket upgrade request; Go HTTP handler validates JWT against Auth0 JWKS before completing the upgrade. On upgrade, session manager registers a session_token in Redis (TTL 4 hours), spawns a reader goroutine consuming PCM chunks from the partner and a writer goroutine emitting word-timestamp events. Reader pipes 100ms PCM chunks to a Google Cloud Speech-to-Text streaming client in a dedicated goroutine; Speech-to-Text returns interim and final word boundaries with start_ms and end_ms offsets. Writer goroutine batches word events at 10-word boundaries before writing to TimescaleDB word_timestamps hypertable to reduce insert frequency. On session close, all in-flight events are flushed to TimescaleDB, session_token expires in Redis, and audio_streams record is updated to closed. GKE HPA scales pod count on custom Prometheus metric active_websocket_sessions_total (target 400/pod). Sticky session affinity via GKE Gateway prevents mid-session pod migration.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gorilla WebSocket, google/cloud/speech Go client (streaming), pgx/v5 for TimescaleDB, go-redis for session state. Auth: Auth0 M2M client credentials, golang-jwt for JWKS validation. Database: TimescaleDB (Cloud SQL PostgreSQL with extension) for transcription_segments and word_timestamps hypertables. Cache: Memorystore Redis 7 for session_tokens and rate limit counters. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot, GKE Gateway with sticky sessions, Artifact Registry, Cloud Build. Observability: Prometheus custom metrics, Grafana dashboards, structlog JSON logs.
target_output.functional_feature_map: WebSocket Session Lifecycle: partner opens wss connection with JWT, session registered in Redis, goroutine pair spawned for PCM read and event write. Real-Time Transcription: PCM chunks (100ms) piped to GCP Speech-to-Text streaming; word-level timestamps emitted as JSON events to partner within 800ms. Word-Timestamp Persistence: word events batched at 10-word boundaries and bulk-inserted to TimescaleDB word_timestamps hypertable. Post-Session Retrieval: GET /v1/sessions/:id/transcript returns full text; GET /v1/sessions/:id/words returns word_timestamps paginatable by time offset. Session Cleanup: explicit close or idle timeout expires session_token in Redis, flushes pending words to TimescaleDB.
target_output.system_components: GKE Autopilot pod (Go WebSocket server, 2 vCPU/4GB, 500 sessions max), GKE Gateway (sticky session affinity, TLS termination), GCP Speech-to-Text Streaming API (word-level timestamps), TimescaleDB on Cloud SQL (transcription_segments, word_timestamps, lyric_sync_events hypertables), Memorystore Redis 7 (session_tokens TTL 4h, rate limit counters), Auth0 tenant (M2M app per partner), Prometheus adapter (active_websocket_sessions custom metric for HPA), Grafana dashboards, Cloud Build CI/CD.
target_output.workflows: Live Transcription Session Workflow: Platform partner backend POST Auth0 /oauth/token, receives JWT. Partner opens WebSocket: GET wss://api.lyricsync.example/v1/stream with Authorization: Bearer JWT header. Go HTTP handler validates JWT via JWKS, checks partner active session count against allowed_stream_count limit. On success, HTTP upgrade completes; session_token registered in Redis with 4-hour TTL; audio_streams record created with status=active. Reader goroutine reads binary PCM frames from WebSocket; 100ms chunks forwarded to GCP Speech-to-Text streaming client. Writer goroutine receives word boundary callbacks from ASR goroutine: {word:'tomorrow', start_ms:1234, end_ms:1456, confidence:0.97}. Writer batches 10 words, bulk-inserts to word_timestamps hypertable, emits each word event individually to partner WebSocket. Partner receives JSON event stream and updates caption display. Post-Session Retrieval Workflow: Partner backend GETs /v1/sessions/:id/transcript with JWT after session closes. Go REST handler queries transcription_segments by stream_id and partner_id (enforced WHERE clause). Returns full text with segment boundaries. Partner editor uses GET /v1/sessions/:id/words?after_ms=5000 to paginate word timestamps for lyric sync file generation.
target_output.data_model: audio_streams (id UUID PK, partner_id UUID FK, asr_provider TEXT, status ENUM active/closed/error, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, closed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). transcription_segments (id UUID PK, stream_id UUID FK, partner_id UUID FK, segment_text TEXT, start_ms BIGINT, end_ms BIGINT, confidence FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- TimescaleDB hypertable weekly partitions, compression after 7 days. word_timestamps (id UUID PK, stream_id UUID FK, partner_id UUID FK, word TEXT, start_ms BIGINT, end_ms BIGINT, confidence FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- TimescaleDB hypertable weekly partitions. lyric_sync_events (id UUID PK, stream_id UUID FK, partner_id UUID FK, event_type ENUM word/sentence/silence, payload JSONB, emitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- TimescaleDB hypertable. session_tokens (session_id UUID PK, partner_id UUID FK, status ENUM active/closed, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ) -- Redis hash, TTL 4 hours. platform_clients (id UUID PK, name TEXT, auth0_client_id TEXT UNIQUE, allowed_stream_count INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: WebSocket: GET wss://api.lyricsync.example/v1/stream — upgrade requires Authorization: Bearer JWT header; sends binary PCM frames; receives JSON events {type:'word',word:'hello',start_ms:1234,end_ms:1456,confidence:0.97}. REST: POST /v1/sessions/:id/close — explicit session termination. GET /v1/sessions/:id/transcript — full text from transcription_segments. GET /v1/sessions/:id/words?after_ms=&limit= — word_timestamps paginated by time offset. GET /v1/partners/:partner_id/sessions — partner session history. All REST endpoints require Authorization: Bearer JWT with partner_id scope. Rate limit: 10 concurrent WebSocket sessions per partner by default, configurable per platform_clients.allowed_stream_count.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT validation at HTTP upgrade handshake — WebSocket connection rejected before upgrade completes if token is invalid or expired. Auth0 JWKS endpoint cached in-process with 5-minute TTL. Partner session isolation: all TimescaleDB queries include AND partner_id = $1 enforced by data access layer; no raw SQL in handlers. Audio chunk buffers held only in goroutine memory, passed by reference to ASR client; never written to disk, Redis, or database. Audio_streams records contain only metadata (start_ms, status), never audio content. session_tokens in Redis expire automatically (TTL 4 hours) preventing stale session accumulation. Rate limit per partner via Redis token-bucket (10 concurrent sessions default). Partner data processing agreements required before API key issuance.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Build CI: go test ./..., golangci-lint, govulncheck, docker build, Artifact Registry push, Helm upgrade to GKE canary (10% traffic). Prometheus custom metric active_websocket_sessions_total checked during canary — if p95 word-event latency exceeds 800ms, rollback triggered. GKE Autopilot: no manual node pool management; Autopilot provisions nodes as HPA scales pod count. HPA target: 400 active sessions per pod, max 20 pods. GKE Gateway sticky session affinity ensures partner WebSocket connections stay on same pod for session duration. Database migrations via golang-migrate as GKE Job pre-deployment. TimescaleDB retention policy: drop_chunks() cron job removes word_timestamps older than 90 days.
target_output.observability: Prometheus custom metrics exported by Go server: active_websocket_sessions_total (per partner and total), word_event_latency_p95_ms (alert >800ms sustained 5 min), asr_streaming_error_rate_per_min (alert >1% per partner), timescaledb_word_insert_latency_ms, session_upgrade_rejection_rate. Grafana dashboard: per-partner active session count, word latency percentile heatmap, ASR error breakdown by error type. structlog JSON logs: every session open/close event includes partner_id, session_id, duration_ms, word_count, and ASR error count.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot cost scales with active pod count; estimated $0.02 per session-hour at 2 vCPU/4GB. GCP Speech-to-Text streaming: ~$0.006 per 15-second audio chunk at standard pricing; 500 concurrent 30-minute sessions = ~$360/hour at peak. TimescaleDB Cloud SQL db-custom-4-15360 covers 500K word inserts/day. Redis Memorystore standard tier handles 500 concurrent session_token hashes. Total estimated $800-1200/day at peak concurrent session load.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): WebSocket session management with JWT upgrade validation, PCM chunking to GCP Speech-to-Text streaming, word-timestamp event emission, session_tokens in Redis, word_timestamps hypertable in TimescaleDB, basic REST transcript retrieval, Prometheus word-event latency metrics, Cloud Build CI. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Lyric-sync event formatting with sentence and silence detection, bulk transcript export API, speaker diarization output from GCP Speech-to-Text, GKE HPA auto-scaling validation under load test, TimescaleDB continuous aggregates for partner analytics. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Batch transcription REST endpoint for pre-recorded files, lyric correction feedback endpoint for partner editors, multi-ASR provider abstraction layer for Deepgram fallback.
target_output.development_guidelines: One goroutine pair per WebSocket session; never share session state across goroutines without channel. JWT validation via cached JWKS only — no symmetric secret validation. All TimescaleDB queries must include partner_id in WHERE clause; enforced by repository layer method signatures requiring partner_id parameter. Audio frame data must not be logged or stored; log only frame metadata (size_bytes, timestamp_ms). Word event batching at 10-word boundaries configurable via WORD_BATCH_SIZE env var for latency tuning. Integration tests use testcontainers-go with real TimescaleDB and mock ASR server returning synthetic word boundary responses.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GCP Speech-to-Text streaming latency spikes during GCP regional incidents exceed 800ms SLA. Mitigation: Deepgram streaming as hot fallback ASR provider activated by circuit breaker on 3 consecutive latency breaches per session. Risk: GKE pod restart mid-session loses in-flight word events buffered in goroutine memory. Mitigation: 30-second SIGTERM grace period allows goroutines to flush pending word batches to TimescaleDB before pod termination. Risk: TimescaleDB insert contention at 500 concurrent sessions * 5 inserts/second = 2500 inserts/second. Mitigation: 10-word batch inserts reduce to 250 inserts/second; pgx batch mode amortizes round-trip cost. Tradeoff: sticky session affinity via GKE Gateway prevents mid-session migration at cost of uneven pod load distribution during partner session bursts.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Google Cloud Speech-to-Text streaming selected over Deepgram as primary ASR for word-level confidence scores and GCP-native billing simplicity; Deepgram positioned as phase 3 fallback. 100ms PCM chunk size chosen as balance between latency and ASR model minimum context window. 10-word batch insert threshold tunable via env var. TimescaleDB weekly hypertable partitions chosen over daily to reduce partition overhead at current insert volume.

metadata.notes: Real-time WebSocket PCM transcription API with Go goroutine-per-session model, GCP Speech-to-Text, TimescaleDB word_timestamps, and Auth0 partner auth.

project_goal.value: Multi-tenant SaaS platform for independent music venues to manage artist bookings, ticketing, and event promotion at scale across hundreds of concurrent venues
project_goal.rationale: Core business need for a scalable venue management product

target_users.value: Venue managers, box office staff, ticketing buyers, and promoters at independent music venues
target_users.rationale: Primary personas identified in product discovery

project_class.rationale: Browser-based SaaS requiring server-side rendering and dynamic multi-tenant data isolation

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, payments, analytics, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Full feature surface including ticketing payments and venue admin dashboards

complexity_level.rationale: Hundreds of concurrent venues with concurrent ticket purchase traffic during on-sale events

risk_level.rationale: Handles payment card data and financial reporting for venue operators

data_sensitivity.rationale: Ticket payment data and box office revenue figures are financially sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: Ticketing pages and event listings are publicly accessible

access_model.rationale: Multi-tenant isolation requires role scoping per venue

feature_scope.rationale: Core feature set for venue operations lifecycle

mvp_scope.value: Event creation, single-tier ticket sales with Stripe, box office summary report, venue admin panel
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for first paying venue cohort

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, Auth0 RBAC, Stripe PCI-DSS scope reduction, WAF on ALB, Redis session encryption
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data and public exposure require strong baseline controls

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise and fast build iteration with Vite

backend_stack.rationale: High-throughput HTTP with low latency during ticket on-sale traffic spikes

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS with row-level security for tenant isolation, Redis 7 for session and queue
data_platform.rationale: Relational model suits booking and ticketing entities; RLS enforces venue data boundaries

hosting_target.value: EKS on AWS with ALB, Karpenter autoscaling, multi-AZ
hosting_target.rationale: Kubernetes enables rapid scale-out during high-demand on-sale events

privacy_retention_policy.value: Ticket buyer PII retained 7 years for financial audit, deleted on request outside dispute window
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR right to erasure balanced against financial record obligations

future_scope.value: Mobile app for buyers, dynamic pricing engine, festival multi-stage support
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap

constraints.rationale: Buyer experience and PCI scope minimization

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM, structured JSON logs, PagerDuty for p95 checkout latency >3s and payment error rate >0.5%
observability_baseline.rationale: Real-time visibility during on-sale traffic events

execution_preference.rationale: Enables safe incremental rollouts to live venues

compliance_context.value: PCI-DSS SAQ A via Stripe Elements (no card data on-platform); GDPR for EU buyer personal data; financial record retention per jurisdiction
compliance_context.rationale: Payment card data and EU attendee personal data trigger compliance requirements

project.goal: Multi-tenant venue booking, ticketing, and box office platform
project.target_users: Venue managers, box office staff, promoters, ticket buyers

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for cart and session

security.auth: Auth0 with RBAC, venue-scoped JWT claims
security.payments: Stripe Elements for PCI scope reduction

data.cache: Redis 7 for sessions, idempotency keys, and ticket reservation locks

constraints.pci_scope: SAQ A, no card data stored

reasoner_reviews.product: The multi-tenant venue model is well-structured. Optimistic locking for concurrent ticket purchases is critical to prevent overselling at high-demand shows. Box office reporting is table-stakes for venue operators to reconcile nightly revenues.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Fastify with RLS on PostgreSQL is a sound choice for tenant isolation without application-layer filtering overhead. Redis-based ticket reservation locks with a 10-minute TTL prevent double-booking during the hold window before payment capture. Consider a read replica for box office reporting queries to isolate OLAP load from transactional writes.
reasoner_reviews.security: Auth0 RBAC with venue-scoped JWT claims ensures cross-tenant data leakage is impossible at the API layer. RLS acts as a defense-in-depth boundary. Stripe Elements keeps card data out of platform scope entirely. WAF rules should include rate limiting on ticket purchase endpoints to prevent scalper bot abuse.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 3-second p95 checkout constraint requires Redis reservation locks, Stripe's pre-authorization flow, and efficient SQL within the transaction. Karpenter autoscaling must warm sufficiently ahead of on-sale events — consider scheduled scaling policies for pre-announced high-demand shows.
reasoner_reviews.critic: ISS-002 from round 1 regarding scalper bot mitigation remains partially addressed. Adding bot score challenges at the ticket selection step, not just at checkout, would close the gap. Also, the box office report generation should move to an async job to avoid blocking API responses for large events.

backend.service_design: Fastify monolith with domain plugins: /events, /bookings, /tickets, /payments, /reports. Tenant isolation enforced via RLS — every query runs in a connection pool session with SET app.venue_id.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful OpenAPI 3.1. POST /events creates event and ticket tiers. POST /tickets/reserve acquires Redis lock and creates pending reservation. POST /payments/capture calls Stripe PaymentIntent confirm. GET /reports/box-office/:event_id is async job — returns 202 with job ID.
backend.failure_handling: Idempotency keys on all Stripe calls. Reservation lock expiry (10 min) releases seats automatically. Dead-letter queue for failed webhook events from Stripe.
backend.background_jobs: Async box office report generation via Bull queue on Redis. Nightly reconciliation job comparing Stripe settlement data against ticket_sales records. Pre-event reminder emails via SES.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with code splitting per venue subdomain. Public event listing pages rendered via SSR-lite using React Router loaders. Venue admin panel behind auth boundary.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Public: Event Detail, Ticket Selection, Checkout (Stripe Elements), Order Confirmation. Admin: Event Builder, Booking Calendar, Box Office Dashboard, Artist Roster.
frontend.state_management: React Query for event and booking data. Zustand for cart state and ticket hold timer countdown. Stripe Elements manages card input state client-side.
frontend.performance_notes: Route-based code splitting. Ticket Selection component lazy-loaded. Optimistic UI updates on reservation with server-side confirmation.

security.auth_design: Auth0 Universal Login. JWT includes venue_id claim for tenant scoping. Short-lived access tokens (15 min) with refresh token rotation.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: venue_owner (full admin), box_office (reporting and check-in), promoter (event creation), buyer (purchase only). RLS policies enforce venue_id boundaries on all tables.
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limiting on POST /tickets/reserve: 5 per IP per minute. CAPTCHA challenge on checkout for suspicious bot scores from Cloudflare Turnstile. Ticket limit enforcement (max 4 per buyer per event) at reservation step.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All payment events logged to payment_audit table with Stripe event ID, amount, status, and buyer_id. Admin actions logged to admin_audit_log with actor, action, and resource.

data.entities: venues (id, name, slug, timezone, capacity, stripe_account_id, created_at). events (id, venue_id FK, title, artist_id, event_date, status ENUM draft/published/on_sale/cancelled). ticket_tiers (id, event_id FK, name, price_cents, capacity, sold_count). ticket_sales (id, tier_id FK, buyer_id FK, stripe_payment_intent_id, status ENUM pending/confirmed/refunded, purchased_at). box_office_reports (id, event_id FK, gross_cents, refunds_cents, net_cents, generated_at, status ENUM pending/ready).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS policies on venues FK. Redis for reservation locks keyed by tier_id:seat_block. S3 for exported box office PDFs.
data.analytics_events: ticket_purchased, reservation_expired, refund_issued, event_published, on_sale_opened — streamed to Datadog for conversion funnel analysis.

ISS-001.title: Box office report blocks API thread for large events
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan generated box office reports synchronously, causing 15-30s API response times for events with 2000+ ticket sales.

history.note: Report endpoint times out for large events

ISS-002.title: Scalper bot mitigation insufficient at ticket selection step
ISS-002.detail: Bot mitigation was applied only at checkout; automated bots can reserve all seats before reaching the CAPTCHA-gated checkout, depleting inventory before legitimate buyers can act.

history.note: Bot mitigation missing from reservation step

ISS-003.title: Missing Stripe webhook signature verification
ISS-003.detail: Round 1 plan lacked explicit Stripe webhook signature verification, leaving payment confirmation open to spoofed webhook payloads.

history.note: Webhook endpoint lacked signature check

focus_issues.title: Scalper bot mitigation insufficient at ticket selection step
focus_issues.detail: Bot mitigation was applied only at checkout; bots deplete reservation inventory before legitimate buyers reach CAPTCHA.

latest_recommendations: Add bot score challenge at POST /tickets/reserve, not just at checkout
Move box office report to async Bull job returning 202 with job polling endpoint
Enforce Stripe-sig header validation on all webhook handlers

convergence_state.reason: ISS-002 scalper bot gap still open

Stripe Elements keeps PCI scope to SAQ A
Optimistic locking for concurrent purchases is well-designed
concerns: Box office report generation blocks API thread for large events
Bot mitigation only at checkout allows inventory depletion by automated scripts

Migrate box office report to async Bull job
Add Stripe webhook signature verification on all payment event handlers
