previous_plan.title: Music Venue Platform Architecture v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Multi-tenant Fastify API with PostgreSQL RLS for venue isolation, Stripe Elements for PCI scope reduction, and Redis reservation locks for concurrent ticket purchases.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Fastify monolith on EKS with RLS-enforced PostgreSQL tenancy. Redis handles reservation locks and session cache. Stripe Elements scopes card data off-platform.
previous_plan.data_model: venues, events, ticket_tiers, ticket_sales with RLS policies. Box office reports generated synchronously via aggregation query.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Auth0 RBAC, venue-scoped JWT, RLS, Stripe Elements SAQ A. Bot mitigation via CAPTCHA at checkout step only.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is round 2 for a multi-tenant music venue ticketing platform. The critical open issue is ISS-002: scalper bots deplete ticket reservation inventory before reaching the checkout CAPTCHA. The fix requires pushing bot score evaluation to the POST /tickets/reserve endpoint using Cloudflare Turnstile token validation, plus rate limiting. ISS-001 (sync report generation) and ISS-003 (webhook sig) were resolved in round 1 audit recommendations and are now reflected. The architecture is otherwise solid — Fastify with RLS on PostgreSQL is the right call for tenant isolation at scale.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Cloudflare Turnstile bot score token requirement on POST /tickets/reserve. Fastify preHandler validates Turnstile token before acquiring Redis reservation lock. Rate limit tightened to 3 reserve attempts per IP per minute. Ticket hold limited to max 4 per buyer per event enforced at DB constraint level.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Automated scalper bots fail at reservation step due to missing valid Turnstile token, preserving inventory for legitimate buyers through the checkout funnel.

target_output.title: Music Venue Platform Architecture — Scalper Bot Hardening
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale multi-tenant SaaS platform for independent music venues managing artist bookings, tiered ticketing, and box office reporting. Built on Fastify with PostgreSQL RLS for tenant isolation across hundreds of venues, Stripe Elements for PCI SAQ A compliance, and Redis reservation locks preventing overselling during concurrent on-sale traffic. Round 2 closes the scalper bot gap by enforcing Cloudflare Turnstile validation at the reservation step.
target_output.architecture_overview: React SPA frontend served via CloudFront communicating with a Fastify API cluster on EKS. PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security enforces venue tenant isolation at the database session level — SET app.venue_id executes per connection pool checkout. Redis 7 manages ticket reservation locks with 10-minute TTL and idempotency key deduplication for Stripe PaymentIntent calls. Auth0 issues venue-scoped JWTs. Cloudflare Turnstile bot protection is evaluated at the reservation step, not at checkout, closing the inventory depletion attack surface. Box office reports are generated asynchronously via Bull queue jobs on Redis.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18 with TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand, Stripe.js. Backend: Node.js 22 with Fastify, Zod, Bull, Stripe Node SDK, jsonwebtoken. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS on RDS Multi-AZ. Cache/Queue: Redis 7 on ElastiCache. Auth: Auth0. Bot Protection: Cloudflare Turnstile. Infrastructure: EKS with Karpenter, ALB with WAF, CloudFront for static assets, S3 for report exports. Observability: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Event Management: venue admins create events with artist booking details, event date, and venue capacity. Ticket Tier Builder: configures pricing tiers (GA standing, seated, VIP) with per-tier capacity and hold policies. Ticket Purchase Flow: buyer selects tier, passes Turnstile bot check, receives reservation hold, completes Stripe Elements checkout. Box Office Dashboard: async report generation showing gross sales, refunds, net revenue, and per-tier breakdown. Artist Booking Calendar: view and manage confirmed, pending, and cancelled artist engagements. Admin Panel: venue onboarding, staff role assignment, Stripe Connect account linking.
target_output.system_components: EKS cluster with Karpenter autoscaling (baseline 4 pods, scaling to 40 during on-sale events). ALB with WAF rules blocking SQLi, XSS, and rate-limit violations. CloudFront distribution for React SPA static assets. Fastify API pods with domain plugins per feature area. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ primary with read replica for box office report queries. Redis ElastiCache cluster for reservation locks, session tokens, and Bull job queues. Stripe Elements in browser (card data never touches platform). Cloudflare Turnstile for bot score validation. S3 bucket for exported PDF reports.
target_output.workflows: Ticket Purchase: Buyer selects ticket tier on event page. Frontend calls POST /tickets/reserve with Turnstile token. Fastify preHandler validates Turnstile server-side; rejects if score below threshold. On pass, Redis SETNX acquires reservation lock for tier:buyer_id with 10-min TTL. Stripe PaymentIntent created with idempotency key. Buyer completes Stripe Elements form. POST /payments/capture confirms PaymentIntent; on success, ticket_sales row status updated to confirmed, sold_count incremented. Confirmation email sent via SES. Box Office Report: Admin clicks Generate Report. POST /reports/box-office/:event_id enqueues Bull job, returns 202 with job_id. Worker queries read replica for sales aggregations, computes gross, refunds, net per tier. Writes box_office_reports row, uploads PDF to S3, updates status to ready. Admin polls GET /reports/box-office/jobs/:job_id until status is ready, then downloads PDF.
target_output.data_model: venues (id UUID PK, name, slug, timezone, capacity, stripe_connect_account_id, created_at). events (id UUID PK, venue_id FK, title, artist_id, event_date, doors_open, status ENUM draft/published/on_sale/sold_out/cancelled). ticket_tiers (id UUID PK, event_id FK, name, price_cents, capacity, sold_count, max_per_buyer). ticket_sales (id UUID PK, tier_id FK, buyer_id FK, stripe_payment_intent_id, status ENUM pending/confirmed/refunded, turnstile_score, purchased_at). box_office_reports (id UUID PK, event_id FK, gross_cents, refunds_cents, net_cents, tier_breakdown JSONB, s3_key, status ENUM pending/ready, generated_at). RLS policy: venue_id = current_setting('app.venue_id') on all tenant tables.
target_output.api_design: POST /events — create event with artist_id, event_date, ticket_tiers array. GET /events/:id/tickets — list available tiers with sold_count. POST /tickets/reserve — validates Turnstile token, acquires Redis lock, returns reservation_id and expires_at. POST /payments/capture — confirms Stripe PaymentIntent, updates ticket_sales status to confirmed. POST /payments/webhook — validates Stripe-Signature header, processes payment_intent.succeeded and charge.refunded events. GET /reports/box-office/:event_id — returns 200 with report if ready, 202 if generating. POST /reports/box-office/:event_id — enqueues async report job. All endpoints require Auth0 JWT; RBAC enforced per role.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PCI-DSS SAQ A via Stripe Elements — card data never reaches platform servers. Auth0 RBAC with venue-scoped JWT claims (venue_id in token payload). PostgreSQL RLS as defense-in-depth tenant boundary. Cloudflare Turnstile bot score validation on POST /tickets/reserve (score threshold 0.5, configurable). Rate limit: 3 reservation attempts per IP per minute via Fastify rate-limit plugin. Stripe webhook signature verified via stripe.webhooks.constructEvent with STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET. WAF rules on ALB: SQLi, XSS, and HTTP flood protection. GDPR: buyer PII encrypted at rest in RDS, deletion API for post-dispute requests. Audit log for all payment events and admin actions.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI pipeline: ESLint, Jest unit tests, integration tests against Dockerized PostgreSQL and Redis, Docker build and ECR push, Helm chart deployment to EKS staging. Production deploy via Helm rolling update with 0-downtime. Karpenter autoscaling: baseline 4 replicas, scale-out on CPU 60% and custom metric 'ticket_reservation_rate'. Scheduled scaling policy for announced high-demand events (pre-warm to 20 pods 30 min before on-sale). PostgreSQL RDS Multi-AZ with automated failover and daily snapshots (35-day retention). Redis ElastiCache Multi-AZ cluster with AOF persistence for reservation lock durability. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with auto-rotation for DB credentials and Auth0 secrets.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM with trace IDs on all Fastify requests. Custom metrics: ticket_reservation_rate (per minute), payment_capture_success_rate, box_office_report_queue_depth, turnstile_rejection_rate. Dashboards: on-sale event monitoring (reservations per second, conversion rate, Stripe latency). Alerts via PagerDuty: p95 checkout latency >3s, payment error rate >0.5%, Redis reservation lock failure rate >1%, Turnstile API timeout. CloudWatch retention 90 days for application logs; Datadog 15-day trace retention.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $1,800/month at 50 active venues and 10,000 monthly ticket sales. EKS baseline cost ~$400/month with Karpenter spot nodes for burst. RDS Multi-AZ ~$300/month. ElastiCache ~$150/month. Datadog ~$400/month. On-sale event burst cost minimal with spot Karpenter nodes. Stripe 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction passed through to venues. Cost optimization: RDS read replica on-demand for reports, CloudFront caching for event listing pages.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): venue onboarding, event creation, single-tier ticket sales with Stripe Elements, Turnstile bot protection at reservation, basic box office summary, Auth0 RBAC, CI/CD pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): multi-tier ticket pricing, async box office reports with PDF export, Stripe Connect for venue payouts, promoter analytics dashboard. Phase 3 (4 weeks): artist booking calendar, dynamic capacity rules, scheduled Karpenter autoscaling for high-demand events, Stripe refund automation.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode on both frontend and backend. Zod schema validation on all Fastify route inputs. Jest for unit tests targeting reservation logic, RLS policy verification, and Stripe integration mocking. Playwright E2E tests covering ticket purchase funnel and Turnstile flow. RLS policy tests run against real PostgreSQL test database (not mocked). All Stripe calls use idempotency keys. Feature flags via LaunchDarkly for gradual rollouts to venue cohorts.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Karpenter autoscaling too slow for flash on-sale events with >10,000 concurrent buyers. Mitigation: scheduled pre-warm scaling policy for announced on-sales; Redis reservation locks prevent overselling even if pods are warming. Risk: Cloudflare Turnstile latency adds to reservation response time. Mitigation: Turnstile validation async with 500ms timeout — fail-open for API availability but log suspicious sessions. Tradeoff: monolith Fastify over microservices chosen for operational simplicity at this scale; domain plugins enforce logical boundaries for future service extraction. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL RLS over application-layer filtering provides stronger tenant isolation guarantees but requires careful connection pool management.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Stripe Connect chosen over manual payouts for venue disbursements — enables per-venue sub-account tracking. Redis SETNX chosen over DB-level advisory locks for reservation because it scales independently of Postgres connections. Turnstile selected over reCAPTCHA for GDPR-friendly fingerprinting without Google data sharing.

metadata.notes: Round 2 multi-tenant venue ticketing platform; closes scalper bot ISS-002 by moving Turnstile to reservation step.

project.goal: Global static website for independent record label with fast audio previews
project.target_users: Music fans, press, industry contacts

frontend.state: No client-side state management — Astro islands only for audio player

security.csp: Content-Security-Policy restricting third-party scripts to analytics and audio player domains only

reasoner_reviews.product: The label site structure is clean: artist pages, release discography, and tour dates are the core content types. Audio preview playback is a key engagement driver — fast initial load of the audio player component is critical. Press kit downloads as static assets from Cloudflare edge is the right approach.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro islands architecture is ideal here — ship zero JS for static pages, hydrate only the audio player component. Range-request support on Cloudflare Pages for audio files enables seek-without-full-download. Image optimization via Astro Image component with AVIF/WebP output improves LCP scores. Round 1 missed explicit CSP header configuration — that needs to be in the Cloudflare Pages _headers file.
reasoner_reviews.security: Static site has minimal attack surface. CSP header restricting script-src to self plus analytics domain prevents XSS from injected third-party scripts. No cookies means no GDPR consent banner. Subresource integrity for any CDN-loaded fonts or icons is good practice.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 3-minute build constraint is achievable with Astro — typical static site builds run under 60 seconds for a label's content volume. Audio files should be committed to Git LFS or served from a separate Cloudflare R2 bucket to avoid bloating the repository.

frontend.app_structure: Astro project with pages directory: /artists/[slug].astro, /releases/[slug].astro, /tour.astro, /press.astro. Layouts: BaseLayout (global nav, footer), ArtistLayout (artist hero with bio).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Home page with featured release and artist hero. Artist roster index and per-artist detail pages. Release discography with audio preview player island. Tour dates page with venue and date listing. Press page with downloadable assets.
frontend.performance_notes: Astro Image component generates AVIF/WebP variants at build time for all artist and release photos. Audio player rendered as client:idle island to defer JS hydration until browser is idle. Tailwind CSS purge eliminates unused styles. Cloudflare Pages serves Brotli-compressed assets automatically.
frontend.accessibility_notes: Audio player includes keyboard controls, aria-label on play/pause toggle, and visible focus indicators for accessibility compliance.

security.auth_design: No authentication required — public static site.
security.abuse_prevention: Cloudflare DDoS protection and bot management at edge layer. CSP header in _headers file restricts script-src to self and analytics.cloudflare.com.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Cloudflare Web Analytics collects aggregate page view and referrer data without cookies or personal identifiers.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloudflare Pages project connected to GitHub repository. Preview deployments on every PR branch. Production deployment on merge to main.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: checkout, install Bun dependencies, run astro build, upload build artifacts. Cloudflare Pages auto-deploys from connected Git branch.
devops.observability_stack: Cloudflare Web Analytics dashboard for traffic and referrer analysis. GitHub Actions build status notifications on Slack for label team.
devops.rollback_strategy: Cloudflare Pages retains all previous deployment versions — instant rollback via Pages dashboard to any prior deployment.

ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan did not include Content-Security-Policy headers in the Cloudflare Pages _headers file, leaving the site open to XSS via injected third-party scripts.

ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan stored audio preview MP3s directly in the Git repository, causing repository clone times to exceed 2 minutes for CI builds.

previous_plan.executive_summary: Astro static site with Tailwind CSS deployed to Cloudflare Pages, featuring artist roster pages, release discography, tour dates, and audio preview player islands.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Astro 4 SSG compiling MDX content to static HTML at build time, deployed to Cloudflare Pages edge network. No server-side runtime.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 for a static record label site. Two open issues: ISS-001 (missing CSP headers) and ISS-002 (audio files bloating Git repository). The fixes are clear: add a _headers file with CSP restricting script-src, and migrate audio preview MP3s to a Cloudflare R2 bucket referenced by URL in MDX frontmatter. The rest of the Astro/Cloudflare Pages architecture is sound. This is a performance_optimization theme so I should also highlight the image optimization and audio range-request delivery improvements.

fix_report.action_taken: Added public/_headers file to Astro project with Content-Security-Policy header restricting script-src to 'self' and static.cloudflareinsights.com (Cloudflare Web Analytics). Also added X-Frame-Options: DENY and X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff headers.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Browser enforces CSP policy on all pages; third-party script injection blocked; Cloudflare Web Analytics continues to function normally.

fix_report.action_taken: Moved all audio preview MP3 files from Git repository to a Cloudflare R2 bucket (label-audio-previews). MDX frontmatter updated to reference R2 public URLs. Cloudflare R2 configured with public access and Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000 for audio assets.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Git repository clone time returns to under 10 seconds for CI; R2 serves audio previews at edge with range-request support and long-lived cache headers.

target_output.title: Record Label Static Website — CSP Hardening and Audio CDN Migration
target_output.executive_summary: A performance-optimized static website for an independent record label built with Astro and deployed to Cloudflare Pages global edge network. Artist roster, release discography, tour dates, and audio preview players are compiled to static HTML at build time with zero server-side runtime. Round 2 resolves CSP header configuration and migrates audio preview files to Cloudflare R2 for efficient edge delivery and clean Git repository management.
target_output.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static site generator compiles MDX content files — artist profiles, release metadata, tour dates — into fully static HTML at build time with no server-side execution path. The audio player is the sole client-side island, hydrated lazily on browser idle to minimize initial JavaScript bundle. Artist and release photography is processed by Astro Image at build time, outputting AVIF/WebP variants for fast LCP scores globally. Audio preview MP3s are hosted on Cloudflare R2 object storage with a public CDN URL and Cache-Control max-age of one year, enabling range-request seeking without full file downloads. The public/_headers file configures CSP, X-Frame-Options, and X-Content-Type-Options on all served pages. GitHub Actions builds the site in under 60 seconds; Cloudflare Pages deploys to edge PoPs globally on merge to main.
target_output.technology_stack: Build tool: Astro 4 with TypeScript, MDX integration, Astro Image. Styling: Tailwind CSS with @astrojs/tailwind. Audio storage: Cloudflare R2 with public CDN distribution. Hosting: Cloudflare Pages global edge network. Analytics: Cloudflare Web Analytics (cookieless). CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Bun for dependency installation. Content format: Markdown/MDX files with YAML frontmatter for structured artist and release metadata.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Artist Roster: index page listing all signed artists with photo and genre tags; per-artist detail page with biography, social links, and discography. Release Discography: per-release pages with track listing, audio preview player, and streaming platform links. Tour Dates: structured listing of upcoming shows with venue, city, date, and ticket link. Press Assets: downloadable high-resolution photos and press kit PDFs served from R2. Audio Preview Player: Astro island component with play/pause, seek bar, and duration display, loading MP3 from R2 URL in MDX frontmatter.
target_output.system_components: Cloudflare Pages project hosting compiled static HTML, CSS, and JS assets globally. Cloudflare R2 bucket (label-audio-previews) serving audio MP3 previews with public CDN URLs and range-request support. GitHub repository containing Astro project with MDX content, TypeScript components, and public/_headers configuration. GitHub Actions runner executing Bun install and astro build in CI. Cloudflare Web Analytics collecting aggregate page view and referrer metrics without cookies.
target_output.workflows: Content Update: Label team member edits an artist's MDX file in the GitHub repository and merges a PR. GitHub Actions triggers the Astro build job, installs dependencies via Bun, runs astro build generating optimized static HTML and AVIF images, and the Cloudflare Pages deployment hook picks up the new build. Preview deployments generate a unique URL per PR for content review before merge. Audio Preview Update: A new audio preview file is uploaded to the Cloudflare R2 bucket via the R2 dashboard or Wrangler CLI. The label team updates the release MDX frontmatter with the new R2 audio_preview_url. The site rebuild picks up the URL reference on next deploy without re-processing the audio binary.
target_output.data_model: Content structured as MDX frontmatter: artists (slug, name, genres array, bio, photo_url, social_links object, spotify_artist_id). releases (slug, artist_slug, title, release_date, cover_art_url, tracks array with title and audio_preview_url, streaming_links object). tour_dates (id, artist_slug, venue, city, country, event_date, ticket_url, status ENUM upcoming/sold_out/cancelled). press_assets (slug, asset_type ENUM photo/pdf, file_url, caption). All content stored as MDX files in /src/content directory, typed via Astro Content Collections schema.
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. Content accessed at build time via Astro Content Collections getCollection() and getEntry() calls. Static file delivery from Cloudflare Pages edge. Audio previews served directly from Cloudflare R2 public CDN URL in MP3 format with Content-Type: audio/mpeg and Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000 headers. Range requests supported natively by R2 for audio seeking.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Cloudflare for all Pages traffic. public/_headers file configures: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' static.cloudflareinsights.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; media-src 'self' pub-*.r2.dev; X-Frame-Options: DENY; X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff; Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Cookieless analytics via Cloudflare Web Analytics — no GDPR consent banner required. No personal data collected or processed on the site. No server-side execution surface to attack.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow on push to main: checkout code, install dependencies with Bun (bun install), build site (bun run build — astro build), validate build output exists. Cloudflare Pages auto-deploys from connected GitHub repository. Preview deployments created automatically for every PR branch with unique URL. Build completes in under 60 seconds. Cloudflare Pages deployment rollback available instantly via Pages dashboard — any prior deployment can be promoted back to production in one click. Audio files managed separately in R2; no redeployment needed for audio updates.
target_output.observability: Cloudflare Web Analytics dashboard shows page view counts, unique visitors, referrer sources, and country-level geographic distribution. No personal data in analytics. Cloudflare Pages build logs available in dashboard for debugging build failures. GitHub Actions workflow status visible in repository for label team. Cloudflare R2 request metrics show audio preview play counts and bandwidth usage per file.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloudflare Pages free tier handles label-scale traffic (100,000 requests/day, unlimited bandwidth). R2 free tier covers 10GB storage and 1 million read operations/month — sufficient for a label's audio preview catalog. Total infrastructure cost: $0/month for current scale. R2 scales linearly at $0.015/GB storage and $0.36/million reads if traffic grows significantly. No scaling configuration needed — Cloudflare Pages handles global traffic distribution automatically.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (3 weeks): Astro project scaffolding with Tailwind, artist and release MDX content types, Home page, Artist roster, Release discography pages, Audio preview player island, Cloudflare R2 bucket setup, _headers file with CSP, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Cloudflare Pages deployment. Phase 2 (2 weeks): Tour dates page, Press assets page with downloadable PDFs, AVIF image optimization for all artist photos, Cloudflare Web Analytics integration. Phase 3 Future: Headless CMS integration (Contentful or Sanity) to replace MDX for non-technical label staff content updates.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in Astro project. Astro Content Collections with Zod schema for all content types — type errors caught at build time. MDX frontmatter validated against schema on every build. Images must be committed as source files (JPEG/PNG) and processed by Astro Image — no pre-optimized images committed. Audio files must be uploaded to R2, never committed to Git repository. PR previews reviewed by label team before merge to main.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Cloudflare R2 public CDN URL exposed for audio files could allow hotlinking at scale. Mitigation: R2 custom domain with Cloudflare hotlink protection rule if bandwidth costs become a concern. Risk: MDX content schema changes require updating all existing content files. Mitigation: Astro Content Collections validate schema at build time, surfacing errors before deployment. Tradeoff: Git-based content workflow over CMS requires technical knowledge for label team; accepted for MVP with CMS migration planned in future scope. Tradeoff: Cloudflare Web Analytics cookieless approach provides less granular data than GA4 but eliminates GDPR compliance burden.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Cloudflare R2 chosen over Git LFS for audio storage — R2 provides native CDN delivery and range-request support without LFS bandwidth costs. Cloudflare Web Analytics chosen over GA4 to avoid GDPR consent requirements. Astro islands chosen over Next.js for zero-JS static pages with selective hydration for audio player only.

metadata.notes: Round 2 record label static site; resolves CSP headers ISS-001 and audio R2 migration ISS-002.

project_goal.value: Flutter mobile app enabling producers to remotely control DAW transport, automate parameters, and browse project state over a local studio network via an API-first integration layer
project_goal.rationale: Core value proposition: smartphone as universal DAW controller

target_users.value: Music producers and audio engineers working in home or professional studios with DAW software
target_users.rationale: Primary end users identified in discovery sessions

project_class.rationale: Flutter app with local Go backend sidecar on the DAW host machine

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, realtime, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Real-time bidirectional control, local persistence, DAW plugin API integration

complexity_level.rationale: Sub-10ms latency requirement, multi-DAW plugin interface layer, local device discovery

risk_level.rationale: Local network only, no cloud exposure, no PII or financial data

data_sensitivity.rationale: DAW session state and parameter values are non-sensitive creative data

external_exposure.rationale: All communication stays on the producer's LAN; no internet egress required

access_model.value: Pairing-code device authentication with JWT session tokens; single-user per DAW host
access_model.rationale: Studio LAN auth is lightweight; pairing code prevents unauthorized device connections

feature_scope.value: DAW transport control (play/stop/record/loop), parameter automation lane binding, plugin parameter adjustment, project state browsing, custom knob mappings, session persistence
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for hands-free DAW operation from smartphone

mvp_scope.value: Transport control and parameter binding with WebSocket real-time sync; Ableton Live and Logic Pro host plugins
mvp_scope.rationale: Core remote control flow covers 80% of producer use cases

security_baseline.value: Pairing-code TOTP, JWT with 8-hour expiry, TLS 1.3 over LAN, host firewall rules blocking external IPs
security_baseline.rationale: Appropriate for local-only service; prevents rogue device connections

frontend_stack.value: Flutter 3.22 with Dart, Riverpod for state, web_socket_channel for real-time
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform iOS/Android from single codebase; strong widget performance

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin, Gorilla WebSocket, DAW plugin bridge via OSC and VST3 SDK
backend_stack.rationale: Low-latency Go runtime ideal for sub-10ms control messages; native plugin bridge

data_platform.value: SQLite via modernc/sqlite for local session and mapping persistence; no external DB
data_platform.rationale: Embedded DB on DAW host eliminates network dependency for configuration data

hosting_target.value: Go sidecar binary on producer's studio Mac/PC; Flutter app distributed via App Store and Google Play
hosting_target.rationale: Zero cloud infrastructure; self-contained local install

privacy_retention_policy.value: Session metadata and parameter mappings retained indefinitely on local machine; no cloud sync
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: User-owned local data with no retention obligations

future_scope.value: Multi-DAW simultaneous control, shared band session mode, MIDI learn integration
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap pending MVP adoption

constraints.value: Must achieve sub-10ms round-trip latency on LAN; support macOS 13+ and Windows 11; no cloud dependency
constraints.rationale: Latency critical for real-time performance control; offline-first by design

observability_baseline.value: Sentry for crash reporting on both Flutter app and Go host; structured Go logs to local file; WebSocket latency histogram
observability_baseline.rationale: Local diagnostics without cloud logging infrastructure

execution_preference.value: Solo developer with GitHub Actions CI; TestFlight and Play beta tracks for distribution
execution_preference.rationale: Lean development workflow matching project scope

project.goal: Smartphone DAW remote controller via local LAN API
project.target_users: Music producers and audio engineers

security.auth: Pairing-code TOTP + JWT

data.persistence: daw_sessions, parameter_bindings, remote_commands, project_snapshots stored locally

devops.environments: local dev, beta, production app store

reasoner_reviews.product: The API-first integration layer is the correct architectural choice for DAW agnosticism. The pairing-code flow is simple enough for non-technical producers while preventing unauthorized connections on shared studio networks.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go sidecar pattern is well-suited; the process runs alongside the DAW and bridges OSC/VST3 plugin APIs to a WebSocket server. The main risk from prior rounds was the OSC-to-WebSocket bridge not handling DAW version differences, which this round resolves with a version negotiation handshake.
reasoner_reviews.security: Local-only exposure dramatically reduces the attack surface. The TOTP pairing code prevents rogue device connections on studio LANs with multiple clients. TLS 1.3 with a self-signed cert pinned on first pairing is appropriate for LAN deployment.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Sub-10ms round-trip on a gigabit LAN is achievable with Go and WebSocket; prior audit flagged that the reconnect logic needed exponential backoff, resolved in round 3.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 3 resolved parameter binding persistence and reconnect backoff but left the OSC bridge error handling underspecified. The DAW version mismatch scenario needs a graceful degradation path in this revision.

backend.service_design: Go sidecar: HTTPServer (Gin, :8765) for REST pairing/config, WebSocketHub (Gorilla /ws/control) for bidirectional control, DAWBridgeService with CompatibilityMatrix map[string]SupportedFeatures keyed by 'ableton:11', 'logic:10.8', 'reaper:6', CommandRouter dispatching transport and parameter commands
backend.api_patterns: REST for pairing (POST /pair, DELETE /session) and config (GET/PUT /mappings, GET /snapshots); WebSocket /ws/control for transport commands and parameter updates at sub-10ms cadence; server pushes state snapshots at 60Hz
backend.failure_handling: WebSocket hub detects client disconnect and pauses DAW automation; OSC bridge version mismatch returns SupportedFeatures with false flags; unknown DAW version triggers all-false manifest with upgrade prompt; exponential backoff 100ms-8s on OSC ECONNREFUSED

security.auth_design: TOTP pairing code displayed on DAW host UI as QR; mobile submits code to POST /pair, receives JWT signed with ECDSA P-256 key and SupportedFeatures manifest; 8-hour JWT expiry with refresh
security.secrets_management: ECDSA key pair generated on first binary launch, private key stored in macOS Keychain (SecItemAdd kSecClassKey) or Windows Credential Manager; never written to plaintext file
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limit pairing to 5 attempts per minute; lockout IP after 3 failed TOTP entries; JWT revocation via DELETE /session

data.entities: daw_sessions (id UUID PK, daw_type ENUM ableton/logic/reaper, daw_version VARCHAR, project_path TEXT, last_opened_at DATETIME), transport_states (id UUID PK, session_id FK, bpm REAL, time_sig_num INT, time_sig_den INT, position_beats REAL, loop_start_beats REAL, loop_end_beats REAL, is_playing BOOL, is_recording BOOL, recorded_at DATETIME), automation_lanes (id UUID PK, session_id FK, plugin_instance_id TEXT, parameter_id TEXT, parameter_name TEXT, min_val REAL, max_val REAL), parameter_bindings (id UUID PK, automation_lane_id FK, mobile_control_type ENUM knob/slider/button, grid_x INT, grid_y INT), remote_commands (id UUID PK, session_id FK, command_type TEXT, payload_json TEXT, latency_ms REAL, executed_at DATETIME), project_snapshots (id UUID PK, session_id FK, snapshot_json TEXT, label TEXT, created_at DATETIME)
data.storage_design: SQLite WAL mode on DAW host; Flutter app caches active session binding layout in Hive for offline display

devops.deployment_topology: Go binary: GitHub Releases for macOS .dmg (notarized) and Windows .msi; auto-update check via GitHub API on launch. Flutter: TestFlight for iOS beta, Google Play Internal Track for Android.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: go test and build matrix (darwin-arm64, darwin-amd64, windows-amd64); flutter analyze and test; goreleaser on tag push packages installers; integration tests against mock DAW OSC server in CI
devops.observability_stack: Sentry Go SDK for panic reports tagged with daw_type+daw_version; Sentry Flutter SDK for crash analytics; prometheus/client_golang on :9090/metrics for daw_remote_command_latency_ms histogram and daw_remote_osc_errors_total counter; structured JSON logs with 30-day rotation

frontend.app_structure: Flutter feature folders: pairing/, transport/, parameter_grid/, project_browser/, settings/; Riverpod providers scoped per feature
frontend.pages_and_flows: Pairing Screen (mDNS host list + QR scan + manual IP entry), Transport Dashboard (play/stop/record/loop with BPM display), Parameter Grid (customizable knob/slider layout bound to automation lanes, disabled controls for unsupported features), Project Browser (snapshot list with restore), Settings (mapping profiles, latency sparkline)
frontend.state_management: Riverpod StateNotifier for transport state; WebSocket stream converted to AsyncValue; Hive for persisting knob layout between app launches; SupportedFeatures manifest drives disabled state on controls
frontend.performance_notes: 60fps CustomPainter knob widgets with no layout rebuilds on value change; WebSocket messages processed on background isolate merged via StreamController

ISS-001.title: OSC bridge error handling underspecified for DAW version mismatches
ISS-001.detail: DAWBridgeService attempts OSC commands without version compatibility check, causing silent failures when unsupported DAW versions are connected. No graceful degradation path defined in architecture.

ISS-002.title: Flutter reconnect logic missing exponential backoff
ISS-002.detail: Fixed in round 3 with exponential backoff 100ms base, max 8s, with jitter.

ISS-003.title: JWT key stored in plain file instead of OS keychain
ISS-003.detail: Fixed in round 2 using macOS Security framework and Windows Credential Manager.

focus_issues.title: OSC bridge error handling underspecified for DAW version mismatches
focus_issues.detail: DAWBridgeService needs version detection on connection, a CompatibilityMatrix, and graceful fallback disabling unsupported commands while keeping transport control active.

latest_recommendations: Add DAW version negotiation handshake returning SupportedFeatures manifest to Flutter client so unsupported controls are disabled in UI
Document OSC command compatibility matrix per DAW version in architecture plan
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: DAW Remote Control Mobile App - Round 3 Architecture

convergence_state.reason: ISS-001 high-severity OSC compatibility issue unresolved across 3 rounds

previous_audits.summary: Strong real-time architecture with well-resolved auth and reconnect issues, but OSC bridge version handling is the blocking gap.
strengths: Pairing-code TOTP with ECDSA keychain integration is well-specified
Exponential backoff reconnect correctly handles LAN interruptions
SQLite WAL mode appropriate for concurrent read/write during live sessions
concerns: OSC bridge silently ignores DAW version incompatibility instead of negotiating capabilities
No UI feedback when parameter command fails due to version mismatch
blocking_issues: DAWBridgeService must negotiate DAW version and return supported feature set before accepting control commands
recommendations: Implement version handshake in DAWBridgeService.Connect() returning SupportedFeatures struct
Flutter client renders disabled state for unsupported controls based on feature manifest
Log version negotiation outcomes to Sentry for compatibility telemetry

issue_updates.title: OSC bridge error handling underspecified for DAW version mismatches
issue_updates.detail: Plan describes OSC bridge but does not specify version detection logic or capability negotiation protocol

best_plan.title: DAW Remote Control Mobile App - Round 3 Architecture
best_plan.executive_summary: Flutter mobile app with Go sidecar enabling real-time DAW transport and parameter control over LAN. Round 3 resolved JWT keychain storage and reconnect backoff; OSC bridge version compatibility remains open.
best_plan.architecture_overview: Go sidecar binary exposes REST pairing API and WebSocket control hub; Flutter app pairs via TOTP and sends control commands over TLS WebSocket targeting sub-10ms latency.
best_plan.security_and_compliance: ECDSA key stored in OS keychain; TOTP pairing lockout; TLS 1.3 self-signed cert pinned.
best_plan.workflows: Transport: Flutter sends play/stop/record; Go routes via OSC. Parameter binding persisted to SQLite.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 addresses ISS-001 by adding a DAW version negotiation handshake to DAWBridgeService.Connect(). The handshake queries DAW version via OSC /version, cross-references a CompatibilityMatrix, and returns a SupportedFeatures struct. The Flutter client receives this manifest and renders unsupported controls as disabled with a version tooltip. This targeted fix does not change the core WebSocket/SQLite design established in prior rounds, and closes the last blocking issue.

fix_report.action_taken: Added DAWBridgeService.Connect() version negotiation: queries DAW version via OSC /version endpoint, cross-references CompatibilityMatrix map keyed by 'ableton:11'/'logic:10.8'/'reaper:6', returns SupportedFeatures{transport_control, parameter_automation, project_browse bool}. Flutter receives feature manifest in WebSocket handshake message and disables controls with tooltip showing minimum required DAW version. Unknown DAW versions return all-false manifest. Version negotiation outcome logged to Sentry with daw_type and daw_version tags.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Producers using unsupported DAW versions see clearly disabled controls instead of silent command failures; ISS-001 resolved and compatibility telemetry begins populating

target_output.title: DAW Remote Control Mobile App - Round 4 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Flutter mobile app paired with a Go sidecar binary on the producer's DAW host, enabling real-time transport control, parameter automation lane binding, and project state browsing over a local studio LAN. Pairing uses TOTP with ECDSA JWT; control messages flow over TLS 1.3 WebSocket targeting sub-10ms round-trip. Round 4 resolves the OSC bridge version negotiation gap with a CompatibilityMatrix handshake, completing production-ready support for Ableton Live 11+, Logic Pro 10.8+, and Reaper 6+.
target_output.architecture_overview: The Go sidecar runs as a background process on the DAW host (macOS 13+ or Windows 11), exposing a Gin REST API for pairing and configuration and a Gorilla WebSocket hub for real-time bidirectional control on /ws/control. DAWBridgeService translates WebSocket commands to OSC UDP messages targeting the DAW's built-in OSC server, with a version negotiation handshake on each new connection that establishes a SupportedFeatures manifest sent to the Flutter client. The Flutter app discovers the host via mDNS broadcast or manual IP entry, pairs via TOTP QR code, and renders a customizable knob/slider grid where controls unsupported by the connected DAW version are visually disabled. All configuration and session state persists in SQLite on the host; the active knob layout caches in Hive on the mobile device for offline display.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.22, Dart, Riverpod 2.x, web_socket_channel 2.x, Hive 2.x for local cache. Host Sidecar: Go 1.22, Gin, Gorilla WebSocket, modernc/sqlite, go-osc for OSC UDP, prometheus/client_golang. DAW Bridge: CompatibilityMatrix for Ableton Live 11/12, Logic Pro 10.8, Reaper 6+. CI/CD: GitHub Actions + goreleaser for macOS .dmg (notarized) and Windows .msi, TestFlight and Google Play Internal Track. Crash Reporting: Sentry Go SDK and Sentry Flutter SDK.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Transport Dashboard: play, stop, record, loop toggle, BPM display, and timeline position readout synced from DAW state at 60fps via WebSocket push. Parameter Grid: configurable 4x6 knob/slider layout where each cell binds to a DAW plugin parameter via automation_lanes; unsupported parameter controls disabled per SupportedFeatures manifest. Project Browser: list of project_snapshots with restore capability to compare session states. Pairing Flow: mDNS host discovery, TOTP QR display on host, JWT issuance including SupportedFeatures manifest on successful pair. Version Compatibility UI: disabled controls show tooltip with minimum required DAW version.
target_output.system_components: Go Sidecar: HTTPServer (Gin :8765) for REST, WebSocketHub (Gorilla /ws/control) for control stream, DAWBridgeService (go-osc UDP client + CompatibilityMatrix), CommandRouter, SQLiteStore (WAL mode). Flutter App: PairingScreen, TransportDashboard, ParameterGrid (CustomPainter knobs with disabled state), ProjectBrowser, RiverpodProviders. DAWBridgeService.CompatibilityMatrix: map[string]SupportedFeatures keyed by 'ableton:11', 'ableton:12', 'logic:10.8', 'reaper:6'. mDNS: _dawremote._tcp service advertisement.
target_output.workflows: DAW Discovery and Pairing: Flutter scans LAN via mDNS for _dawremote._tcp, presents host list; producer selects host; host displays TOTP QR; app submits code to POST /pair, receives JWT and SupportedFeatures manifest; controls for unsupported features rendered disabled with minimum-version tooltip. Transport Control: producer taps play on TransportDashboard; Flutter sends {cmd:transport, action:play} over WebSocket; CommandRouter calls DAWBridgeService.SendTransport('play'); OSC /live/play dispatched to Ableton on UDP:11000; DAW state change event received via OSC /live/state and broadcast to Flutter at 60Hz; full round-trip target under 10ms on gigabit LAN. Parameter Binding: producer long-presses empty grid cell, selects 'Bind Parameter'; app lists automation_lanes for current session via GET /mappings; producer picks lane; binding saved to SQLite parameter_bindings and Hive cache; subsequent knob movements send {cmd:param, lane_id:uuid, value:0.73} at 50Hz throttle.
target_output.data_model: daw_sessions (id UUID PK, daw_type ENUM ableton/logic/reaper, daw_version VARCHAR, project_path TEXT, last_opened_at DATETIME). transport_states (id UUID PK, session_id FK, bpm REAL, time_sig_num INT, time_sig_den INT, position_beats REAL, loop_start_beats REAL, loop_end_beats REAL, is_playing BOOL, is_recording BOOL, recorded_at DATETIME). automation_lanes (id UUID PK, session_id FK, plugin_instance_id TEXT, parameter_id TEXT, parameter_name TEXT, min_val REAL, max_val REAL, default_val REAL). parameter_bindings (id UUID PK, automation_lane_id FK, mobile_control_type ENUM knob/slider/button, grid_x INT, grid_y INT, label TEXT). remote_commands (id UUID PK, session_id FK, command_type TEXT, payload_json TEXT, latency_ms REAL, executed_at DATETIME). project_snapshots (id UUID PK, session_id FK, snapshot_json TEXT, label TEXT, created_at DATETIME).
target_output.api_design: REST: POST /pair {totp_code} -> {jwt, supported_features:{transport_control:bool, parameter_automation:bool, project_browse:bool}} | GET /mappings?session_id -> {automation_lanes:[...]} | PUT /mappings {bindings:[...]} | GET /snapshots?session_id | DELETE /session. WebSocket /ws/control inbound: {cmd:transport, action:play|stop|record|loop_toggle} | {cmd:param, lane_id:uuid, value:0.0-1.0} | {cmd:snapshot, action:save|restore, snapshot_id:uuid}. Outbound push: {type:state, bpm:120, position_beats:4.0, is_playing:true} at 60Hz | {type:features, manifest:{...}} on connect handshake.
target_output.security_and_compliance: TOTP pairing code with 30s window and 5-attempt lockout. ECDSA P-256 key pair generated on first launch, private key stored in macOS Keychain (SecItemAdd kSecClassKey) or Windows Credential Manager; never written to disk as plaintext. JWT signed with host ECDSA key, 8-hour expiry, revoked via DELETE /session. TLS 1.3 with self-signed cert generated on first run and pinned in the Flutter app on first pairing. Go HTTP server bound to studio subnet CIDR from config, rejecting connections from outside local network.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Host Binary: goreleaser builds darwin-arm64, darwin-amd64, windows-amd64; macOS .dmg with Apple notarization via GitHub Actions; Windows .msi via WiX Toolset. Auto-update: binary checks GitHub Releases API for new tags on launch and prompts user. Mobile: iOS via TestFlight beta then App Store production; Android via Google Play Internal Track then production. GitHub Actions pipeline runs go test (full suite), flutter analyze and flutter test, integration tests against mock DAW OSC server, goreleaser on tag push. Rollback: previous installer hosted on GitHub Releases.
target_output.observability: Sentry Go SDK on host sidecar with panic recovery middleware; version negotiation errors tagged with daw_type+daw_version for compatibility telemetry. Sentry Flutter SDK for crash reports with session context. prometheus/client_golang on :9090/metrics exposing daw_remote_command_latency_ms histogram (buckets 1,5,10,25,50ms), daw_remote_ws_connections gauge, daw_remote_osc_errors_total counter by daw_type. Structured JSON logs to ~/Library/Logs/DAWRemote/ with 30-day rotation. Latency histogram surfaced as a 'Connection Health' sparkline in the Flutter Settings screen.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud infrastructure cost; all compute on producer's own hardware. GitHub Actions free tier covers CI. App Store ($99/yr) and Google Play ($25 one-time) developer accounts. Goreleaser open-source. Single WebSocket connection per host; Go goroutine overhead negligible at single-device scale. SQLite scales to years of session history on local disk without maintenance.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Go sidecar with REST pairing, WebSocket hub, Ableton Live 11 OSC bridge with version negotiation handshake, SQLite persistence, TOTP+ECDSA auth. Flutter app with pairing flow, transport dashboard, 4x4 fixed parameter grid. TestFlight and GitHub Releases distribution. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Logic Pro 10.8 and Reaper 6 OSC bridge entries in CompatibilityMatrix; custom grid layout with Hive caching; project browser with snapshots. Phase 3 (4 weeks): mDNS auto-discovery; latency health sparkline; Sentry telemetry for compatibility data; macOS notarization pipeline.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: gofmt and golangci-lint in CI; table-driven unit tests for CommandRouter and CompatibilityMatrix; mock OSC server for integration tests. Flutter: flutter analyze and flutter test in CI; widget tests for TransportDashboard and ParameterGrid; integration tests against mock WebSocket server. SQLite migrations via numbered .sql files applied on binary startup. Semantic versioning with goreleaser CHANGELOG on each tag. ADRs in /docs/adr/ for DAW bridge protocol decisions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: OSC server port and command schema vary across DAW versions and OS. Mitigation: CompatibilityMatrix version-gated by daw_type:major_version; unknown versions return all-false SupportedFeatures with upgrade prompt displayed in app. Risk: mDNS unreliable on enterprise Wi-Fi networks blocking multicast. Mitigation: manual IP entry fallback in pairing screen. Tradeoff: SQLite over embedded key-value store - relational queries for automation lane joins justify the overhead. Tradeoff: self-signed TLS cert over plaintext - LAN-only deployment still vulnerable to ARP spoofing on shared studio networks.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected OSC over MIDI for DAW bridge due to richer parameter addressing and UDP network transport. Confirmed SQLite WAL mode over journal mode for concurrent read during live sessions. Chose Flutter over React Native for superior 60fps CustomPainter knob rendering. TOTP pairing chosen over Bluetooth pairing for reliability across macOS/Windows without OS-specific Bluetooth stack handling.

metadata.notes: Round 4 mobile DAW controller resolving OSC version negotiation gap with CompatibilityMatrix handshake; local-only LAN architecture.

project_goal.value: Greenfield infrastructure platform for a music AI startup establishing GPU-enabled Kubernetes clusters, audio job queues, and model serving infrastructure to support inference and batch audio processing workloads at scale
project_goal.rationale: Core infrastructure to serve ML models for audio feature extraction, stem separation, and generative tasks

target_users.value: Internal ML engineers and audio processing services consuming the platform via job submission APIs
target_users.rationale: Internal platform consumers — no public user-facing interface

project_class.rationale: Platform-level infrastructure provisioning — not an application but an environment and runtime for AI workloads

capabilities.rationale: Infrastructure provisioning, job orchestration API, and batch processing capabilities

complexity_level.rationale: GPU node pool autoscaling, multi-model serving, high-throughput audio batch processing

risk_level.rationale: Internal platform with proprietary ML models and audio assets; no public exposure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Audio training data and model weights are proprietary internal assets

external_exposure.rationale: Platform accessed only from internal VPC; no public endpoints

access_model.value: Service accounts per team with least-privilege GCP IAM; Keycloak for human operator authentication to management UIs
access_model.rationale: Separate machine and human identity management for audit clarity

feature_scope.value: GPU node pool provisioning, Kubernetes cluster management, model serving endpoints, inference job queue, batch audio processing pipeline, autoscaling policies, canary rollout for model versions
feature_scope.rationale: Full platform capability set for ML workload lifecycle

mvp_scope.value: Single GKE cluster with GPU node pool, basic inference job queue, model serving endpoint with canary routing, Terraform modules for environment provisioning
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum infrastructure for ML team to begin model serving

security_baseline.value: GKE Workload Identity, Keycloak authentication for operators, GCP Secret Manager for credentials, VPC-native cluster with private nodes, Binary Authorization for container images
security_baseline.rationale: GPU infrastructure with proprietary models requires strong access controls and supply chain security

frontend_stack.value: None — infrastructure project with no user-facing frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: Platform infrastructure requires no UI component

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI for job orchestration API
backend_stack.rationale: Consistent with ML team's Python expertise; FastAPI for low-latency job submission and status APIs

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL for job state and model registry; Redis 7 on Memorystore for job queue and inference result cache
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for job state; Redis queue enables async inference patterns

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot with GPU node pools on GCP; Terraform for infrastructure-as-code
hosting_target.rationale: Autopilot manages node lifecycle; GPU node pools support NVIDIA T4 and A100 instances

privacy_retention_policy.value: Audio processing jobs retained 90 days; model artifacts retained indefinitely in GCS versioned bucket; inference logs retained 30 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Operational data retention aligned with internal data governance policy

future_scope.value: Multi-region GPU clusters, federated model training infrastructure, real-time streaming inference pipeline
future_scope.rationale: Platform evolution as startup scales

constraints.value: GPU nodes must autoscale to zero when idle to control costs; model serving must support canary rollouts without downtime; Terraform modules must be reusable across environments
constraints.rationale: Cost control on GPU compute and zero-downtime deployment requirements

observability_baseline.value: Grafana with Prometheus for cluster metrics; Grafana Loki for log aggregation; DCGM Exporter for GPU utilization metrics; Alertmanager for GPU saturation and job queue depth alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: GPU workload observability requires specialized metrics beyond standard Kubernetes instrumentation

execution_preference.value: Infrastructure-as-code with Terraform; GitOps for Kubernetes manifests via ArgoCD; all changes reviewed via pull request
execution_preference.rationale: IaC and GitOps ensure reproducible, auditable infrastructure changes

compliance_context.value: Proprietary ML model weights and audio training data covered by internal IP policy; Binary Authorization enforces trusted container provenance
compliance_context.rationale: Internal data sensitivity requires container supply chain controls and IP protection

project.goal: GPU Kubernetes platform for music AI inference and batch audio processing
project.target_users: Internal ML engineers and audio processing services

backend.queue: Redis 7 via Memorystore for inference job queue

security.auth: GKE Workload Identity for service accounts; Keycloak for human operators
security.container_policy: Binary Authorization for trusted image provenance

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL for job state and model registry
data.cache: Redis 7 Memorystore for job queue and inference result cache

devops.iac: Terraform for GKE cluster, node pools, VPC, Cloud SQL, Memorystore

constraints.canary_rollout: Model version canary with configurable traffic split

reasoner_reviews.product: The platform scope is well-defined for a greenfield music AI startup. GPU scale-to-zero is critical for cost control — idle GPU nodes are expensive. The canary rollout for model versions enables safe A/B testing of new stem separation or feature extraction models before full traffic promotion.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: GKE Autopilot with custom GPU node pools is a sound foundation. DCGM Exporter for GPU utilization metrics is essential — standard Kubernetes CPU/memory metrics are insufficient for GPU workload capacity planning. The inference job queue on Redis with a FastAPI orchestration API provides a clean async pattern for batch audio processing. Binary Authorization ensures only Cloud Build-signed images run in the cluster, preventing supply chain attacks on proprietary model containers.
reasoner_reviews.security: GKE Workload Identity eliminates static service account keys for pod-to-GCP API communication. VPC-native private nodes with no public IP ensure GPU workloads are not internet-reachable. Secret Manager with Workload Identity bindings ensures model inference keys and credentials are never baked into container images. Binary Authorization with attestation from Cloud Build provides container provenance validation.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: GPU scale-to-zero requires node pool minimum=0 with GKE cluster autoscaler. Cold-start latency for GPU nodes (2-4 minutes) must be accounted for in job queue timeouts. Canary rollout with Argo Rollouts or Istio weighted routing enables configurable traffic splits between model versions without redeployment.

backend.service_design: FastAPI job orchestration service with endpoints for job submission, status polling, and result retrieval. Model registry module tracks deployed model versions, canary weights, and performance metrics.
backend.api_patterns: POST /inference/jobs — submit audio file GCS URI and model_id, returns job_id. GET /inference/jobs/:id — returns status (queued/processing/completed/failed) and result_uri. POST /models/:id/deploy — deploys new model version with canary_weight parameter. GET /models — lists model registry entries with version, status, and accuracy metrics.
backend.background_jobs: GPU worker pods consume inference job queue from Redis, download audio from GCS, run model inference, upload result stems/features to GCS, update job status in PostgreSQL. Nightly batch job computes aggregate job success rates and GPU utilization per model version.
backend.failure_handling: Job retry with exponential backoff (max 3 attempts) on transient GPU errors. Dead-letter queue for failed jobs with alerting after 5 DLQ entries. Circuit breaker on model serving pods: if error rate >10% in 60s, canary weight set to 0 and alert fired.

security.auth_design: GKE Workload Identity binds Kubernetes service accounts to GCP service accounts. Each team namespace gets dedicated service account with least-privilege IAM (e.g., storage.objectViewer for audio input, storage.objectCreator for results). Keycloak with OIDC for human operator access to ArgoCD, Grafana, and job status API.
security.secrets_management: GCP Secret Manager stores model API keys, PostgreSQL credentials, Redis auth tokens. External Secrets Operator syncs Secret Manager values to Kubernetes Secrets on pod startup. No secrets in Terraform state or container images.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Cloud Audit Logs for all GCP API calls. Kubernetes audit log forwarded to Cloud Logging. Job submission and model deployment events logged to audit_events table in PostgreSQL with actor service account and timestamp.

data.entities: inference_jobs (id UUID PK, model_id FK, input_gcs_uri, output_gcs_uri, status ENUM queued/processing/completed/failed, gpu_node_id, submitted_at, completed_at, retry_count). model_versions (id UUID PK, model_name, version_tag, gcs_artifact_uri, deployed_at, canary_weight DECIMAL, status ENUM staging/canary/production/deprecated). resource_quotas (id UUID PK, team_namespace, max_concurrent_jobs INT, max_gpu_hours_daily DECIMAL).
data.storage_design: GCS bucket (audio-job-inputs) for uploaded audio files. GCS bucket (model-artifacts) versioned for model weight storage. GCS bucket (inference-outputs) for result stems and feature files. PostgreSQL Cloud SQL for job state and model registry. Redis Memorystore for job queue (list-based) and inference result cache (hash by job_id).
data.migration_notes: Greenfield — no migration. Initial schema applied via Alembic migrations in Cloud Build pipeline. Seed data populates model_versions for initial model deployments.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster with two node pool configurations: CPU node pool (e2-standard-4, min 1, max 20) for orchestration services. GPU node pool (n1-standard-8 + NVIDIA T4, min 0, max 10) for inference workers. VPC-native private nodes, no public IPs. Cloud NAT for egress-only internet access (for model hub downloads during initial setup).
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build pipeline on push to main: build Docker image, sign with Binary Authorization attestor, push to Artifact Registry. ArgoCD watches manifests repo and syncs Kubernetes deployments on image tag update. Canary rollouts managed by Argo Rollouts with configurable traffic split steps (10%, 25%, 50%, 100%).
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus operator for cluster and application metrics. DCGM Exporter for GPU memory utilization, GPU compute utilization, and SM clock metrics per node. Grafana dashboards: GPU cluster overview, inference job throughput, model version canary comparison. Alertmanager rules: GPU utilization >90% for 5 minutes, job queue depth >100, GPU node cold-start failures.
devops.rollback_strategy: Argo Rollouts abort canary on analysis failure — reverts to prior model version with zero traffic. ArgoCD sync revert for application manifest changes. Terraform state stored in GCS bucket with versioning; manual terraform apply from prior plan for infrastructure rollback.
devops.cost_controls: GPU node pool min=0 with scale-to-zero when job queue is empty. Committed use discounts for CPU node baseline. GCS Intelligent-Tiering for inference outputs older than 30 days. Resource quotas per team namespace enforce max concurrent job limits. Monthly budget alert in GCP at 80% of approved GPU compute budget.

target_output.thinking_summary: Greenfield GPU infrastructure platform for a music AI startup. Key decisions: GKE Autopilot with GPU node pools scaling to zero for cost control, Redis-backed inference job queue with FastAPI orchestration, Binary Authorization for container provenance on proprietary model containers, and DCGM Exporter for GPU-specific observability. The architecture must support async batch audio processing (stem separation, feature extraction) and synchronous inference with canary model rollouts. ArgoCD GitOps ensures all Kubernetes changes are auditable and reproducible.
target_output.title: Music AI GPU Platform Infrastructure Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A greenfield Kubernetes infrastructure platform on GCP for a music AI startup, providing GPU-enabled inference workers, async audio job queues, model registry with canary rollout support, and comprehensive GPU observability. GKE Autopilot manages the cluster lifecycle with GPU node pools that autoscale to zero during idle periods. FastAPI orchestrates job submission and status tracking. All infrastructure is provisioned via Terraform and managed via ArgoCD GitOps for reproducible, auditable deployments.
target_output.architecture_overview: GKE Autopilot cluster with two node pool configurations: a CPU node pool for orchestration services (FastAPI job API, ArgoCD, Prometheus stack) and a GPU node pool (NVIDIA T4 and A100) that autoscales from zero to ten nodes based on inference job queue depth. Audio inference jobs are submitted via FastAPI POST /inference/jobs, enqueued in Redis on Memorystore, and consumed by GPU worker pods that download audio from GCS, run model inference, upload results back to GCS, and update job status in Cloud SQL PostgreSQL. Model versions are tracked in a model registry with canary weights managed by Argo Rollouts, enabling 10%/25%/50%/100% staged rollouts of new stem separation or feature extraction models. Binary Authorization validates that only Cloud Build-attested container images run in the cluster, protecting proprietary model containers. All GCP-to-pod credential binding uses GKE Workload Identity — no static service account keys.
target_output.technology_stack: Infrastructure: Terraform for GKE cluster, GPU node pools, VPC, Cloud SQL, Memorystore, GCS buckets, IAM bindings. Kubernetes: GKE Autopilot, Argo Rollouts for canary, ArgoCD for GitOps, External Secrets Operator. Job Orchestration: Python 3.12 FastAPI. Queue: Redis 7 on GCP Memorystore. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL. Storage: GCS for audio inputs, model artifacts, inference outputs. Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, DCGM Exporter, Alertmanager, Grafana Loki. CI/CD: Cloud Build with Binary Authorization. Auth: GKE Workload Identity, Keycloak with OIDC for human operators.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Inference Job Submission: ML services POST audio GCS URI and model_id; job queued and job_id returned. GPU Worker Processing: workers consume queue, run stem separation or feature extraction model, return output GCS URI. Model Registry: tracks model versions, deployment status, canary weights, and per-version accuracy metrics. Canary Rollout: new model version deployed at 10% traffic; Argo Rollouts promotes based on error rate and latency analysis. Resource Quotas: per-team namespace limits on concurrent jobs and daily GPU hours. Infrastructure Provisioning: reusable Terraform modules for dev, staging, and production environment creation.
target_output.system_components: GKE Autopilot cluster with CPU node pool (e2-standard-4, min 1, max 20) and GPU node pool (n1-standard-8 + NVIDIA T4, min 0, max 10). FastAPI job orchestration service (2 CPU replicas, HPA on request rate). Redis Memorystore cluster for job queue and inference result cache. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with private IP for job state and model registry. GCS buckets: audio-job-inputs, model-artifacts (versioned), inference-outputs. Artifact Registry for signed container images. ArgoCD server for GitOps manifest sync. Argo Rollouts controller for canary deployment management. Prometheus operator with DCGM Exporter sidecar on GPU nodes. Grafana with Alertmanager for dashboards and alerts.
target_output.workflows: Inference Job Lifecycle: ML service authenticates with Workload Identity service account token. POST /inference/jobs with input_gcs_uri (e.g., gs://audio-job-inputs/track-abc123.wav) and model_id (stem-sep-v3). FastAPI validates request, writes inference_jobs row with status=queued, pushes job_id to Redis queue. GPU worker pod (scheduled on T4 node) pops job_id, fetches job record, downloads audio from GCS, loads model weights from in-memory cache or GCS artifact, runs inference, uploads separated stems to gs://inference-outputs/job-abc123/, updates job status to completed with output_gcs_uri. ML service polls GET /inference/jobs/:id until completed, retrieves output URI. Model Canary Rollout: ML engineer submits new model version artifact to GCS model-artifacts bucket, calls POST /models/:id/deploy with version_tag and canary_weight=0.10. Argo Rollouts creates a new ReplicaSet for the new version, routes 10% of inference worker traffic to it. Cloud Build analysis job evaluates error rate and p95 latency against baseline over 30 minutes. On pass, Argo Rollouts advances to 25%, 50%, 100%. On failure, rollout aborted and prior version receives 100% traffic.
target_output.data_model: inference_jobs (id UUID PK, model_id UUID FK, input_gcs_uri TEXT, output_gcs_uri TEXT, status ENUM queued/processing/completed/failed, gpu_node_id TEXT, error_message TEXT, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, retry_count INT DEFAULT 0). model_versions (id UUID PK, model_name TEXT, version_tag TEXT, gcs_artifact_uri TEXT, deployed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, canary_weight DECIMAL(4,3), status ENUM staging/canary/production/deprecated, accuracy_score DECIMAL(5,4)). resource_quotas (id UUID PK, team_namespace TEXT UNIQUE, max_concurrent_jobs INT, max_gpu_hours_daily DECIMAL(8,2), current_gpu_hours_today DECIMAL(8,2)). audit_events (id UUID PK, actor_service_account TEXT, action TEXT, resource_type TEXT, resource_id TEXT, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /inference/jobs — body: {input_gcs_uri, model_id, priority ENUM normal/high}; returns {job_id, status: queued, estimated_wait_seconds}. GET /inference/jobs/:id — returns {job_id, status, output_gcs_uri, completed_at, retry_count}. POST /inference/jobs/:id/cancel — cancels queued job before worker pickup. POST /models/:id/deploy — body: {version_tag, gcs_artifact_uri, canary_weight}; initiates Argo Rollout. GET /models — returns model registry with version list and canary status. GET /cluster/quota/:namespace — returns team namespace quota usage and limits. All endpoints require Workload Identity JWT or Keycloak OIDC token; RBAC enforced per namespace.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GKE Workload Identity binds Kubernetes service accounts to GCP service accounts per team namespace — no static keys. GPU worker pods get storage.objectViewer on audio-job-inputs and storage.objectCreator on inference-outputs; no broader storage access. Binary Authorization policy: require Cloud Build attestation on all container images in cluster; deny unattested images. GCP Secret Manager with External Secrets Operator syncs PostgreSQL credentials and Redis auth to Kubernetes Secrets at pod startup. VPC-native private GKE nodes with no public IPs; Cloud NAT for controlled egress. Keycloak with OIDC for human operator access to ArgoCD dashboard and Grafana; MFA required. Cloud Audit Logs for all GCP API calls. Kubernetes audit log forwarded to Cloud Logging with 90-day retention.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform modules: gke-cluster (VPC, GKE Autopilot config, node pools, Workload Identity), cloud-sql (PostgreSQL with private IP, automated backups), memorystore (Redis cluster), gcs-buckets (audio inputs, model artifacts, inference outputs with lifecycle policies), iam-bindings (service account per team namespace). Modules parameterized by environment (dev, staging, production) via tfvars files. Cloud Build pipeline: on push to main, builds and signs Docker images with Binary Authorization attestor, pushes to Artifact Registry, updates image tag in Kubernetes manifests repo. ArgoCD detects manifest change and syncs deployment to GKE cluster. GPU node pool autoscaling: Karpenter-style scale-to-zero when Redis queue depth=0 for 10 minutes; scale-up triggered when queue depth >0.
target_output.observability: Prometheus operator scrapes FastAPI job API (custom metrics: inference_job_queue_depth, inference_job_p95_latency_seconds, model_version_error_rate), DCGM Exporter per GPU node (DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL, DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED, DCGM_FI_DEV_SM_CLOCK). Grafana dashboards: GPU cluster overview (utilization per node, memory pressure, SM clock frequency), inference job throughput (jobs/min, success rate, p95 latency per model), canary rollout comparison (error rate and latency for baseline vs canary model). Alertmanager rules: GPU utilization >90% for 5 min (scale-up alert), job queue depth >100 (capacity alert), inference error rate >5% for 10 min (model quality alert), GPU node cold-start failure >2 in 30 min (infrastructure alert). Loki for structured application and worker pod logs with job_id label for trace-through.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GPU node pool scales to zero when queue empty — idle cost $0 for GPU compute. Active cost: NVIDIA T4 node ~$0.45/hour; A100 node ~$3.20/hour. Estimated $800/month for 1,000 inference jobs/day at 3 min average GPU time per job. Committed use discounts for CPU node pool baseline (~$200/month). Cloud SQL ~$150/month. Memorystore Redis ~$100/month. GCS storage ~$50/month for model artifacts and inference outputs. Total estimated $1,300/month at initial workload volume. Budget alert at 80% of $2,000/month approved GPU budget triggers Slack notification.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Foundation (4 weeks): Terraform modules for GKE cluster, GPU node pool, Cloud SQL, Memorystore, GCS buckets, IAM bindings. FastAPI job orchestration API with submission and status endpoints. Redis job queue with GPU worker base image. Binary Authorization pipeline in Cloud Build. ArgoCD GitOps setup. Prometheus and DCGM Exporter deployed. Smoke test: submit stem separation job, validate GPU worker processes and returns output. Phase 2 Model Infrastructure (3 weeks): Model registry implementation in PostgreSQL. Argo Rollouts canary configuration for model versions. Inference result caching in Redis. Resource quota enforcement per team namespace. Grafana dashboards for GPU and job metrics. Phase 3 Operations Hardening (2 weeks): Automated canary analysis with Argo Rollouts AnalysisTemplate evaluating error rate and p95 latency. Dead-letter queue with PagerDuty alerting. External Secrets Operator integration. Audit event logging to PostgreSQL. Runbook documentation for GPU node pool operations.
target_output.development_guidelines: All infrastructure changes via Terraform pull requests with plan output reviewed before merge. Kubernetes manifest changes via GitOps pull requests reviewed by platform team. No kubectl apply in production — only ArgoCD syncs. Docker base images pinned to SHA digest, not mutable tags. DCGM Exporter metrics required in model acceptance criteria before production promotion. GPU worker Dockerfiles must not embed model weights — load from GCS at startup. All Terraform modules include variable validation blocks and output documentation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GPU node cold-start latency (2-4 minutes) causes long initial job wait times. Mitigation: warm pool of 1 T4 node during business hours via scheduled autoscaling; cold-start time documented in job API response via estimated_wait_seconds field. Risk: Binary Authorization blocks emergency hotfix deployments if attestor is unavailable. Mitigation: break-glass procedure with GCP IAM override logged to Cloud Audit Logs; reviewed in post-incident process. Risk: Argo Rollouts canary promotion based on error rate could pass a model with degraded audio quality not reflected in error metrics. Mitigation: quality metrics (MOS score for stem separation) added to AnalysisTemplate alongside error rate. Tradeoff: GKE Autopilot over Standard mode — Autopilot limits custom node configuration but reduces cluster management overhead; accepted for startup scale.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: GKE Autopilot chosen over Standard for managed node lifecycle at startup scale. Redis list-based queue chosen over Pub/Sub for lower latency and simpler job status tracking in same Redis instance. Argo Rollouts chosen over Flagger for canary management due to richer AnalysisTemplate DSL for custom metric evaluation. DCGM Exporter deployed as DaemonSet on GPU nodes for per-node GPU metrics visibility.

metadata.notes: Greenfield GPU Kubernetes infrastructure for music AI startup; strong observability and canary rollout design.

project_goal.value: Fullstack platform enabling remote music production teams to share project files, stems, and version histories with fine-grained RBAC and end-to-end encrypted file storage
project_goal.rationale: Core business need: secure async collaboration replacing consumer cloud storage for unreleased music

target_users.value: Music producers, session musicians, mix engineers, and label A&R managers collaborating on unreleased projects
target_users.rationale: All roles identified in stakeholder discovery; each requires distinct access scoping

project_class.rationale: Requires React/Next.js UI, FastAPI backend, real-time notifications, and admin panel

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, realtime, integrations, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: File management, RBAC, real-time comment threads, label admin, DAW plugin integration hooks

complexity_level.rationale: Customer-managed encryption keys, immutable audit trail, per-project IP allowlisting, version tree management

risk_level.rationale: Unreleased music under NDA; breach would cause commercial and reputational harm to label clients

data_sensitivity.rationale: Projects contain unreleased masters, stems, and compositional data covered by NDA

external_exposure.rationale: All access requires Auth0 authentication; no public endpoints expose project data

access_model.value: RBAC with project-scoped roles: owner, editor, reviewer, listener; IP allowlisting per project; MFA required for all roles
access_model.rationale: Fine-grained scoping prevents session musicians from accessing other projects; label admin manages cross-project visibility

feature_scope.value: Project creation and versioning, stem upload and download, version tree browser, real-time comment threads on stems, RBAC invite management, immutable audit trail, admin panel for label account management
feature_scope.rationale: Full collaboration lifecycle from project creation to delivery

mvp_scope.value: Project and stem management with RBAC, file upload/download, version history, and audit trail; no real-time comments in MVP
mvp_scope.rationale: Core secure file exchange covers primary use case for Q2 pilot with 3 label clients

security_baseline.value: AES-256 encryption at rest with customer-managed KMS keys, TLS 1.3 in transit, MFA for all users, automatic session expiry after 30 minutes of inactivity, IP allowlisting per project
security_baseline.rationale: SOC 2 Type II and NDA obligations require defense-in-depth for unreleased content

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 15 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, WebSocket client for real-time notifications
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; Next.js App Router for SSR of project metadata; React Query for file list state

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.x, Celery for async jobs, Boto3 for S3
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async performance; SQLAlchemy for complex RBAC queries; Celery for waveform generation and virus scan jobs

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ, Redis 7 on ElastiCache for sessions and job queues
data_platform.rationale: Relational model required for project-scoped RBAC relationships and version tree; Redis for Celery broker

hosting_target.value: AWS EKS in us-east-1 with multi-AZ worker nodes; S3 with SSE-KMS for file storage
